╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Plan to implement                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ OpenRevu Phase 3B — Bug Fixes + Adaptive Toolbar + Vector Strategy                                                   │

│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Part 0: Vector Strategy Decision (Address First)                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ User's Insight                                                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ The user realized: "What I want is more like Inkscape or Illustrator with shapes — but with                          │
│ measurements and relational metadata on the objects."                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ This is actually exactly what OpenRevu already is — the user just doesn't see it yet because                         │
│ the PDF-as-primary-surface framing obscures it. Here's the reality:                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ┌──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                 │
│ │   What the user wants    │                       What OpenRevu already has                       │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Vector shapes            │ Fabric.js objects ARE vectors (defined as points/beziers, not pixels) │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Measurements on shapes   │ Phase 2: Distance, Area, Count, Calibration                           │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Relational metadata      │ markupType, markupStatus, pairedId, measurementType, etc.             │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Edit individual vertices │ Phase 3A: Node Editor (polygon + line endpoints)                      │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Blank canvas to draw on  │ "New Document" feature (batch 1 features)                             │                 │
│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                 │
│ │ Optional reference image │ PDFs are already treated as a locked background layer                 │                 │
│ └──────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ What Fabric.js is: A declarative vector graphics editor that RENDERS to HTML canvas.                                 │
│ Every rect, ellipse, polygon, line, path is defined in vector space (coordinates, not pixels).                       │
│ Zooming doesn't pixelate shapes. Shoelace area and Euclidean distance work in real coordinates.                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ What "vector-native" (SVG.js / Paper.js / raw SVG) would give us above Fabric.js:                                    │
│ - Native SVG DOM elements (CSS-styleable, searchable, copyable)                                                      │
│ - Native SVG file format (.svg) instead of JSON                                                                      │
│ - Infinite zoom without any canvas rasterization artifacts                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ What a pivot would cost:                                                                                             │
│ - Re-implementing 300-test, 8-phase feature set from scratch (months of work)                                        │
│ - Paper.js / SVG.js / Konva.js don't have Fabric's rich object model (selection handles,                             │
│ transform controls, grouping, undo/redo, etc.) — all of that would need rebuilding                                   │
│ - Fabric.js 6 already has canvas.toSVG() export built-in — the gap is small                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Recommendation: Stay on Fabric.js + add SVG import/export in Phase 4                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ The path forward:                                                                                                    │
│ 1. Fix bugs + toolbar redesign now (Phase 3B — this plan)                                                            │
│ 2. Phase 4: canvas.toSVG() export, optional SVG/PDF background import                                                │
│ 3. Phase 4: Relational metadata (ExtendedCognition plugin)                                                           │
│ 4. The app becomes "Inkscape + measurements + semantic metadata" — which IS the goal                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ This avoids a 6-month rewrite. The vector editing already IS Fabric.js.                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Part 1: Bug Fixes                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Bug 1 — Pan/Edit Conflict Still Happening in Select Mode                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Root cause: The guard in pdf-viewer.js:115 uses DOM traversal:                                                       │
│ e.target.closest('.canvas-container.drawing-active')                                                                 │
│ This is fragile: Fabric.js 6 may set pointer-events on canvas elements via inline styles                             │
│ that override the CSS cascade, causing events to reach #viewport's handler even when                                 │
│ .drawing-active is set. The CSS-guard approach is unreliable.                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Reliable fix: Add a bubble-phase mousedown listener directly on fabricCanvas.wrapperEl                               │
│ that calls e.stopPropagation() whenever drawing-active is set.                                                       │
│ Stops the event from ever reaching #viewport — no selector matching needed.                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // In canvas.js setDrawingMode(enabled):                                                                             │
│ setDrawingMode(enabled) {                                                                                            │
│     const wrapper = this.fabricCanvas.wrapperEl;                                                                     │
│     if (!wrapper) return;                                                                                            │
│     wrapper.classList.toggle('drawing-active', enabled);                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     if (enabled && !this._panBlockHandler) {                                                                         │
│         // Bubble phase — fires after Fabric processes the event; stops it reaching #viewport                        │
│         this._panBlockHandler = (e) => e.stopPropagation();                                                          │
│         wrapper.addEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);                                                │
│     } else if (!enabled && this._panBlockHandler) {                                                                  │
│         wrapper.removeEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);                                             │
│         this._panBlockHandler = null;                                                                                │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Files: static/js/canvas.js — setDrawingMode() only.                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Bug 2 — Measurement Values Don't Recalculate When Moved/Scaled Outside Node Edit                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Root cause: The object:modified recalculation listeners in node-editor.js are ONLY                                   │
│ active during node edit mode. No global listener exists for ordinary move/scale in select mode.                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ What breaks:                                                                                                         │
│ - Distance Group moved: lineEndpoints is stale (relative to old left/top)                                            │
│ - Distance Group scaled: pixelLength wrong, inner IText label text wrong                                             │
│ - Area Polygon moved: companion IText label stays at old position (separate Fabric object)                           │
│ - Area Polygon scaled/rotated: pixelArea wrong, label text wrong                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Fix: Add initForCanvas(fc, scale) to MeasureTools and wire a global object:modified                                  │
│ handler. Add two recalc helpers.                                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // measure.js additions:                                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ initForCanvas(fc, scale) {                                                                                           │
│     this._fc = fc;                                                                                                   │
│     this._scale = scale;                                                                                             │
│     fc.on('object:modified', (e) => {                                                                                │
│         const obj = e.target;                                                                                        │
│         if (!obj) return;                                                                                            │
│         if (obj.measurementType === 'distance' && obj._objects) {                                                    │
│             this._recalcDistanceGroup(obj);                                                                          │
│         } else if (obj.measurementType === 'area' && Array.isArray(obj.points)) {                                    │
│             this._recalcAreaPolygon(obj);                                                                            │
│         }                                                                                                            │
│     });                                                                                                              │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _recalcDistanceGroup(group) {                                                                                        │
│     const innerLine = group._objects[0];  // Line is always first child                                              │
│     if (!innerLine || innerLine.x1 === undefined) return;                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     // Transform group-local line endpoints to canvas space                                                          │
│     const mat = group.calcTransformMatrix();                                                                         │
│     const p0 = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: innerLine.x1, y: innerLine.y1 }, mat);                                │
│     const p1 = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: innerLine.x2, y: innerLine.y2 }, mat);                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     const newPixelLength = Math.sqrt((p1.x - p0.x) ** 2 + (p1.y - p0.y) ** 2);                                       │
│     const newLabel = this._scale                                                                                     │
│         ? this._scale.formatDistance(newPixelLength)                                                                 │
│         : `${newPixelLength.toFixed(1)} px`;                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     group.pixelLength = newPixelLength;                                                                              │
│     group.labelText = newLabel;                                                                                      │
│     // Update lineEndpoints relative to new group.left/top (for NodeEditor compatibility)                            │
│     group.lineEndpoints = {                                                                                          │
│         x1: p0.x - group.left, y1: p0.y - group.top,                                                                 │
│         x2: p1.x - group.left, y2: p1.y - group.top,                                                                 │
│     };                                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     // Update inner IText label                                                                                      │
│     const innerLabel = group._objects[1];                                                                            │
│     if (innerLabel && innerLabel.set) {                                                                              │
│         innerLabel.set({ text: newLabel });                                                                          │
│         group.dirty = true;                                                                                          │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│     this._fc.requestRenderAll();                                                                                     │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _recalcAreaPolygon(polygon) {                                                                                        │
│     if (!polygon.points || polygon.points.length < 3) return;                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     // Shoelace on creation-space points × effective scale transform                                                 │
│     const rawArea = Math.abs(this._polygonArea(polygon.points));                                                     │
│     const effectiveArea = rawArea                                                                                    │
│         * Math.abs(polygon.scaleX || 1)                                                                              │
│         * Math.abs(polygon.scaleY || 1);                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     const newLabel = this._scale                                                                                     │
│         ? this._scale.formatArea(effectiveArea)                                                                      │
│         : `${effectiveArea.toFixed(0)} sq px`;                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     polygon.pixelArea = effectiveArea;                                                                               │
│     polygon.labelText = newLabel;                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     // Find companion label by shared pairedId                                                                       │
│     if (!polygon.pairedId) return;                                                                                   │
│     const label = this._fc.getObjects().find(                                                                        │
│         o => o.pairedId === polygon.pairedId && o !== polygon                                                        │
│     );                                                                                                               │
│     if (!label) return;                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     label.set({ text: newLabel });                                                                                   │
│     label.pixelArea = effectiveArea;                                                                                 │
│     label.labelText = newLabel;                                                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     // Reposition label to new centroid in canvas space                                                              │
│     const n = polygon.points.length;                                                                                 │
│     const lx = polygon.points.reduce((s, p) => s + p.x, 0) / n - polygon.pathOffset.x;                               │
│     const ly = polygon.points.reduce((s, p) => s + p.y, 0) / n - polygon.pathOffset.y;                               │
│     const c = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: lx, y: ly }, polygon.calcTransformMatrix());                           │
│     label.set({ left: c.x, top: c.y });                                                                              │
│     label.setCoords();                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     this._fc.requestRenderAll();                                                                                     │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Wire-up in app.js (after scale + canvas are initialized, before or alongside nodeEditor init):                       │
│ // After: this.nodeEditor.initForCanvas(this.canvas, this.toolbar, this.scale);                                      │
│ this.measureTools.initForCanvas(this.canvas.fabricCanvas, this.scale);                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Files: static/js/measure.js, static/js/app.js                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Part 2: Mode-Tab Toolbar (Bluebeam Style)                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Layout                                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                                        │
│ │ [OpenRevu] [New][Open][file.pdf]  [Navigate│Markup│Measure]  [⚙][−][+][Fit]│                                       │
│ │                                   ─────────────────────                    │                                       │
│ │  Tab contents (only one row visible):                                      │                                       │
│ │  Navigate: [Hand] [Select]                                                 │                                       │
│ │  Markup:   [Pen][Rect][Ellipse][Line][Highlight][Text][Cloud][Callout]     │                                       │
│ │  Measure:  [Distance][Area][Count][Calibrate][Node Edit]                   │                                       │
│ │                                                                            │                                       │
│ │  Always visible right side: [Intent • Note] [Export] [−][+][Fit]         │                                         │
│ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Behavior                                                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ - Clicking a tab shows its tools, hides the others                                                                   │
│ - Activating a tool via keyboard shortcut auto-switches to the correct tab (G→Navigate, R→Markup, U→Measure)         │
│ - Tab selection persists in localStorage('openrevu-toolbar-tab') across sessions                                     │
│ - Settings gear opens a modal with per-tool visibility checkboxes (also localStorage)                                │
│ - Tab height is 42px total — tab buttons sit inline on the toolbar bar (not a second row)                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Toolbar HTML Structure Change                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Current: flat list of toolbar-group divs inside #toolbar.                                                            │
│ New: same structure but tool groups are wrapped in <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="navigate|markup|measure"> divs   │
│ with display:none on inactive ones.                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ <!-- Tab buttons — stay visible always -->                                                                           │
│ <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-tabs">                                                                             │
│     <button class="toolbar-tab active" data-tab="navigate">Navigate</button>                                         │
│     <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="markup">Markup</button>                                                    │
│     <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="measure">Measure</button>                                                  │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ <!-- Tab content panels — only one visible at a time -->                                                             │
│ <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="navigate">                                                                          │
│     <!-- Hand, Select buttons -->                                                                                    │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="markup" style="display:none">                                                       │
│     <!-- Pen, Rect, Ellipse, Line, Highlight, Text, Cloud, Callout -->                                               │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="measure" style="display:none">                                                      │
│     <!-- Distance, Area, Count, Calibrate, Node Edit -->                                                             │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ <!-- Always-visible right side -->                                                                                   │
│ <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-intent">...</div>                                                                  │
│ <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-export">...</div>                                                                  │
│ <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-zoom">...</div>                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ <!-- Settings gear -->                                                                                               │
│ <button id="btn-toolbar-settings" class="toolbar-btn" title="Toolbar settings">⚙</button>                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Toolbar.js Changes                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // New state:                                                                                                        │
│ this._activeTab = localStorage.getItem('openrevu-toolbar-tab') || 'markup';                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // Tool → tab mapping:                                                                                               │
│ const TOOL_TAB = {                                                                                                   │
│     hand: 'navigate', select: 'navigate',                                                                            │
│     pen: 'markup', rect: 'markup', ellipse: 'markup', line: 'markup',                                                │
│     highlighter: 'markup', text: 'markup', cloud: 'markup', callout: 'markup',                                       │
│     distance: 'measure', area: 'measure', count: 'measure',                                                          │
│     calibrate: 'measure', 'node-edit': 'measure',                                                                    │
│ };                                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // _setActiveTab(tabName):                                                                                           │
│ _setActiveTab(tabName) {                                                                                             │
│     this._activeTab = tabName;                                                                                       │
│     localStorage.setItem('openrevu-toolbar-tab', tabName);                                                           │
│     document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel').forEach(p => {                                                           │
│         p.style.display = p.dataset.tab === tabName ? '' : 'none';                                                   │
│     });                                                                                                              │
│     document.querySelectorAll('.toolbar-tab').forEach(b => {                                                         │
│         b.classList.toggle('active', b.dataset.tab === tabName);                                                     │
│     });                                                                                                              │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // In setTool(), after existing tool activation logic, auto-switch tab:                                              │
│ const tab = TOOL_TAB[toolName];                                                                                      │
│ if (tab && tab !== this._activeTab) this._setActiveTab(tab);                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Settings Modal (simple)                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // On gear click: generate checkbox list from all .tool-btn buttons                                                  │
│ _openSettings() {                                                                                                    │
│     // Build checkboxes: [checkbox] Hand  [checkbox] Select  [checkbox] Pen  ...                                     │
│     // On change: btn.hidden = !checked; save to localStorage                                                        │
│     // On reset: restore all, clear localStorage                                                                     │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Files: templates/editor.html, static/css/style.css, static/js/toolbar.js                                             │

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have a solid understanding of the codebase. Let me create the task list and implement all the changes. Let me
  outline what needs to be done:

  1. canvas.js — Fix setDrawingMode() with stopPropagation fix
  2. measure.js — Add initForCanvas, _recalcDistanceGroup, _recalcAreaPolygon
  3. app.js — Wire measureTools.initForCanvas()
  4. templates/editor.html — Tab structure + settings modal HTML
  5. static/css/style.css — Tab + modal styles

  6. static/js/toolbar.js — Tab switching, tool-to-tab map, settings modal logic
  7. test_phase3b.mjs — ~36 tests
  8. run_tests.mjs — Add new suite

  I'll tackle the independent pieces first—canvas.js and measure.js alongside the HTML and CSS work. For canvas.js, I
  need to enhance setDrawingMode() by adding a mousedown listener that captures the bubble phase to prevent event
  propagation. I need to set up the measure tools initialization by adding the initForCanvas method and its helper
  functions to handle distance and area recalculations, then wire it into the app initialization after the node editor
  setup. The HTML structure also needs updating to organize the toolbar tabs properly.

  For the CSS, I'm adding styles for the tab navigation system, individual tab states, and the settings modal with
  visibility toggles. The toolbar controller needs to track which tab is active, automatically switch tabs when tools
  are selected, and handle the settings modal interactions through the button bindings.

  I'll need to write tests covering tab switching behavior, keyboard shortcut integration with tabs, localStorage
  persistence of the active tab, the settings modal functionality, and verify that the pan/select and measurement
  recalculation bugs are resolved.

● Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me set up tasks and implement all changes.

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  Bug 1 deep dive: The original setDrawingMode() relies on CSS class toggling plus a closest() selector in the PDF
  viewer's pan handler. This is inherently fragile because Fabric.js 6 dynamically applies inline pointer-events styles
  to its canvas layers during rendering, which can break CSS inheritance chains. The stopPropagation approach is
  architecturally cleaner — it intercepts at the DOM event level before the pan handler even sees the event, regardless
  of computed styles.

  Bug 2 deep dive: Measurement labels are "baked" at creation time (by design), but moving/scaling a Group doesn't
  update those stored values. The object:modified event fires after every Fabric transformation, making it the natural
  hook for live recalculation. The tricky part for Area polygons is that polygon.points are in creation space, so the
  effective area = rawArea × scaleX × scaleY.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Starting implementation — Task 1 first:

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/canvas.js)
  ⎿  Added 24 lines, removed 5 lines
      370      /**
      371       * Enable or disable drawing mode.
      372       *
      373 -     * When drawing is active, pointer-events are enabled on the canvas
      374 -     * so mouse events go to Fabric instead of passing through to the
      375 -     * viewport for panning. When inactive, events pass through.
      373 +     * When drawing is active:
      374 +     *   1. .drawing-active class is added to the Fabric wrapper element (CSS fallback)
      375 +     *   2. A bubble-phase mousedown listener is added to the wrapper that calls
      376 +     *      stopPropagation(), preventing events from ever reaching #viewport's pan handler.
      377       *
      378 +     * WHY stopPropagation instead of CSS alone:
      379 +     *   Fabric.js 6 dynamically sets pointer-events on its canvas layers via inline styles
      380 +     *   during rendering. These inline styles can override the CSS cascade, causing events
      381 +     *   to reach #viewport even when .drawing-active is present. The stopPropagation
      382 +     *   approach is immune to CSS specificity battles — it intercepts at the event level
      383 +     *   before #viewport's handler fires, regardless of computed styles.
      384 +     *
      385       * Args:
      386       *   enabled: boolean — true to enable drawing, false to disable.
      387       */
     ...
      390
      391          // Fabric wraps our canvas in a .canvas-container div
      392          const wrapper = this.fabricCanvas.wrapperEl;
      385 -        if (wrapper) {
      386 -            wrapper.classList.toggle('drawing-active', enabled);
      393 +        if (!wrapper) return;
      394 +
      395 +        wrapper.classList.toggle('drawing-active', enabled);
      396 +
      397 +        if (enabled && !this._panBlockHandler) {
      398 +            // Bubble phase — fires AFTER Fabric processes the event.
      399 +            // Stops the event from propagating up to #viewport's pan handler.
      400 +            // This is the authoritative guard: CSS .drawing-active is a backup.
      401 +            this._panBlockHandler = (e) => e.stopPropagation();
      402 +            wrapper.addEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);
      403 +        } else if (!enabled && this._panBlockHandler) {
      404 +            wrapper.removeEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);
      405 +            this._panBlockHandler = null;
      406          }
      407      }

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/measure.js)
  ⎿  Added 181 lines, removed 1 line
       77   */
       78  export class MeasureTools {
       79      constructor() {
       80 -        // No persistent state — each init* call is self-contained with local closures
       80 +        // Global canvas and scale references — set by initForCanvas() after document load.
       81 +        // Used by _recalcDistanceGroup and _recalcAreaPolygon for live recalculation
       82 +        // when the user moves or scales measurement objects in select mode.
       83 +        /** @type {fabric.Canvas|null} */
       84 +        this._fc = null;
       85 +        /** @type {ScaleManager|null} */
       86 +        this._scale = null;
       87      }
       88
       89      // =========================================================================
       90 +    // GLOBAL OBJECT:MODIFIED LISTENER — recalculate labels after move/scale
       91 +    // =========================================================================
       92 +
       93 +    /**
       94 +     * Wire a global object:modified listener on the Fabric canvas.
       95 +     *
       96 +     * This catches moves, scales, and rotations in select mode — situations
       97 +     * that the tool-specific handlers (initDistance, initArea, etc.) do NOT
       98 +     * handle because those handlers are removed when the tool changes.
       99 +     *
      100 +     * WHY this is needed:
      101 +     *   Measurement labels are "baked" at creation time (e.g. "10.5 ft").
      102 +     *   When the user later selects and moves a distance Group, the stored
      103 +     *   pixelLength becomes stale. This listener recalculates it live so
      104 +     *   the displayed label always reflects the actual current geometry.
      105 +     *
      106 +     * Must be called AFTER canvas.init() creates a new Fabric canvas instance
      107 +     * (i.e. inside _onDocumentLoaded in app.js, after nodeEditor.initForCanvas).
      108 +     * If called again for a new document, the old listener is automatically
      109 +     * superseded by the new Fabric canvas instance.
      110 +     *
      111 +     * Args:
      112 +     *   fc:    Fabric.Canvas instance (canvas.fabricCanvas).
      113 +     *   scale: ScaleManager instance for unit-aware formatting.
      114 +     */
      115 +    initForCanvas(fc, scale) {
      116 +        this._fc = fc;
      117 +        this._scale = scale;
      118 +
      119 +        fc.on('object:modified', (e) => {
      120 +            const obj = e.target;
      121 +            if (!obj) return;
      122 +
      123 +            // Distance Group: measurementType='distance', contains Line + IText children
      124 +            if (obj.measurementType === 'distance' && obj._objects) {
      125 +                this._recalcDistanceGroup(obj);
      126 +            }
      127 +
      128 +            // Area Polygon: measurementType='area', has .points array
      129 +            // NOTE: companion IText label (same pairedId) does NOT get recalc'd here —
      130 +            // it has no .points and its position is updated by _recalcAreaPolygon below.
      131 +            else if (obj.measurementType === 'area' && Array.isArray(obj.points)) {
      132 +                this._recalcAreaPolygon(obj);
      133 +            }
      134 +        });
      135 +
      136 +        console.log('[MeasureTools] Global object:modified recalc listener attached');
      137 +    }
      138 +
      139 +    /**
      140 +     * Recalculate a distance Group's label after it has been moved or scaled.
      141 +     *
      142 +     * The Group's child Line stores its endpoints in Group-local space.
      143 +     * We transform them via the Group's current transform matrix to get
      144 +     * the actual canvas-space positions, then recompute pixel length.
      145 +     *
      146 +     * Coordinate math:
      147 +     *   group.calcTransformMatrix() → 6-element affine matrix [a,b,c,d,e,f]
      148 +     *   transformPoint(local) → canvas-space point
      149 +     *   newPixelLength = Euclidean distance between the two transformed endpoints
      150 +     *
      151 +     * Args:
      152 +     *   group: Fabric.Group — the distance measurement object.
      153 +     */
      154 +    _recalcDistanceGroup(group) {
      155 +        // Find the Line child — it's the first object in the group (by construction)
      156 +        const innerLine = group._objects && group._objects[0];
      157 +        if (!innerLine || innerLine.x1 === undefined) return;
      158 +
      159 +        // Transform Group-local line endpoints to canvas (natural) coordinates
      160 +        const mat = group.calcTransformMatrix();
      161 +        const p0 = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: innerLine.x1, y: innerLine.y1 }, mat);
      162 +        const p1 = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: innerLine.x2, y: innerLine.y2 }, mat);
      163 +
      164 +        const newPixelLength = Math.sqrt((p1.x - p0.x) ** 2 + (p1.y - p0.y) ** 2);
      165 +
      166 +        // Format with current scale (may differ from creation-time scale if user re-calibrated)
      167 +        const newLabel = this._scale
      168 +            ? this._scale.formatDistance(newPixelLength)
      169 +            : `${newPixelLength.toFixed(1)} px`;
      170 +
      171 +        // Update stored metadata on the group
      172 +        group.pixelLength = newPixelLength;
      173 +        group.labelText = newLabel;
      174 +
      175 +        // Update lineEndpoints (NodeEditor uses these for endpoint editing).
      176 +        // They must be relative to the group's current bounding-box origin
      177 +        // so NodeEditor can recover absolute coords as: abs = group.left + rel
      178 +        group.lineEndpoints = {
      179 +            x1: p0.x - group.left,
      180 +            y1: p0.y - group.top,
      181 +            x2: p1.x - group.left,
      182 +            y2: p1.y - group.top,
      183 +        };
      184 +
      185 +        // Update the IText label (second child in the group)
      186 +        const innerLabel = group._objects && group._objects[1];
      187 +        if (innerLabel && typeof innerLabel.set === 'function') {
      188 +            innerLabel.set({ text: newLabel });
      189 +            // Mark dirty so Fabric re-renders the group's cached texture
      190 +            group.dirty = true;
      191 +        }
      192 +
      193 +        if (this._fc) this._fc.requestRenderAll();
      194 +    }
      195 +
      196 +    /**
      197 +     * Recalculate an area polygon's label and companion IText position
      198 +     * after it has been moved, scaled, or rotated.
      199 +     *
      200 +     * Area calculation:
      201 +     *   polygon.points are stored in creation space (before any transform).
      202 +     *   The effective area = rawArea × |scaleX| × |scaleY|.
      203 +     *   We do NOT use the transform matrix for area — the Shoelace formula
      204 +     *   gives creation-space area, and the scale factors give the multiplier.
      205 +     *
      206 +     * Companion label repositioning:
      207 +     *   We recompute the centroid in creation space, then transform it to
      208 +     *   canvas space via calcTransformMatrix() to get the new label position.
      209 +     *
      210 +     * Args:
      211 +     *   polygon: Fabric.Polygon — the area measurement object.
      212 +     */
      213 +    _recalcAreaPolygon(polygon) {
      214 +        if (!polygon.points || polygon.points.length < 3) return;
      215 +
      216 +        // Shoelace area in creation space, then apply current scale factors.
      217 +        // pathOffset centers Fabric's internal coord system on the polygon —
      218 +        // we don't need to subtract it for the Shoelace area calculation because
      219 +        // the formula is translation-invariant; only scale matters.
      220 +        const rawArea = Math.abs(this._polygonArea(polygon.points));
      221 +        const effectiveArea = rawArea
      222 +            * Math.abs(polygon.scaleX || 1)
      223 +            * Math.abs(polygon.scaleY || 1);
      224 +
      225 +        const newLabel = this._scale
      226 +            ? this._scale.formatArea(effectiveArea)
      227 +            : `${effectiveArea.toFixed(0)} sq px`;
      228 +
      229 +        // Update stored metadata on the polygon
      230 +        polygon.pixelArea = effectiveArea;
      231 +        polygon.labelText = newLabel;
      232 +
      233 +        if (!polygon.pairedId || !this._fc) return;
      234 +
      235 +        // Find the companion IText label by shared pairedId.
      236 +        // The label is a separate Fabric object (not grouped), so we scan all objects.
      237 +        const label = this._fc.getObjects().find(
      238 +            o => o.pairedId === polygon.pairedId && o !== polygon
      239 +        );
      240 +        if (!label) return;
      241 +
      242 +        // Update label text and metadata
      243 +        label.set({ text: newLabel });
      244 +        label.pixelArea = effectiveArea;
      245 +        label.labelText = newLabel;
      246 +
      247 +        // Reposition label to the polygon's new centroid in canvas space.
      248 +        // Centroid in creation space (relative to pathOffset):
      249 +        const n = polygon.points.length;
      250 +        const localCx = polygon.points.reduce((s, p) => s + p.x, 0) / n - polygon.pathOffset.x;
      251 +        const localCy = polygon.points.reduce((s, p) => s + p.y, 0) / n - polygon.pathOffset.y;
      252 +
      253 +        // Transform creation-space centroid to canvas space
      254 +        const mat = polygon.calcTransformMatrix();
      255 +        const c = fabric.util.transformPoint({ x: localCx, y: localCy }, mat);
      256 +
      257 +        label.set({ left: c.x, top: c.y });
      258 +        label.setCoords();
      259 +
      260 +        this._fc.requestRenderAll();
      261 +    }
      262 +
      263 +    // =========================================================================
      264      // DISTANCE TOOL — click start → drag → click/release end
      265      // =========================================================================
      266

● Task 4 — redesign the editor.html toolbar with tabs:

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  Tab architecture note: The key design constraint is that tab switching must be zero-height — tools still live in the
  single 42px toolbar bar, and switching tabs just shows/hides tool groups inline within that same bar. This is achieved
   by making .tab-panel a flex container within #toolbar (which is itself a flex container), so active panels extend the
   toolbar flow without adding rows. The settings gear button must live outside all tab panels so it's always visible.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

      1145 +
      1146 +/* =============================================================================
      1147 + * PHASE 3B: MODE-TAB TOOLBAR
      1148 + * ============================================================================= */
      1149 +
      1150 +/* =========================================================================
      1151 + * TOOLBAR TAB BUTTONS — Navigate | Markup | Measure
      1152 + *
      1153 + * Tabs sit inline in the toolbar bar (no second row — 42px total height).
      1154 + * Active tab gets an accent underline, matching the Bluebeam visual language.
      1155 + * ========================================================================= */
      1156 +
      1157 +.toolbar-tabs {
      1158 +    display: flex;
      1159 +    align-items: stretch;
      1160 +    gap: 0;
      1161 +    padding: 0 4px;
      1162 +    height: 42px;           /* Fill the full toolbar height for larger click target */
      1163 +}
      1164 +
      1165 +.toolbar-tab {
      1166 +    background: transparent;
      1167 +    border: none;
      1168 +    border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
      1169 +    color: #8888a0;
      1170 +    padding: 0 12px;
      1171 +    font-size: 12px;
      1172 +    font-weight: 600;
      1173 +    text-transform: uppercase;
      1174 +    letter-spacing: 0.6px;
      1175 +    cursor: pointer;
      1176 +    transition: color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s;
      1177 +    white-space: nowrap;
      1178 +    line-height: 1;
      1179 +    display: flex;
      1180 +    align-items: center;
      1181 +}
      1182 +
      1183 +.toolbar-tab:hover {
      1184 +    color: #c0c0d0;
      1185 +}
      1186 +
      1187 +.toolbar-tab.active {
      1188 +    color: #4a9eff;
      1189 +    border-bottom-color: #4a9eff;
      1190 +}
      1191 +
      1192 +/* =========================================================================
      1193 + * TAB PANELS — contain the tool buttons for each tab
      1194 + *
      1195 + * Tab panels are inline flex containers in the toolbar flow.
      1196 + * Inactive panels are hidden with display:none. The active panel's
      1197 + * tool group buttons appear inline, maintaining the 42px toolbar height.
      1198 + * ========================================================================= */
      1199 +
      1200 +.tab-panel {
      1201 +    display: flex;          /* flex so inner .toolbar-group aligns properly */
      1202 +    align-items: center;
      1203 +}
      1204 +
      1205 +/* Override the toolbar-group separator border inside tab panels —
      1206 +   the tab itself provides visual separation from the tab buttons */
      1207 +.tab-panel .toolbar-group {
      1208 +    border-left: none;
      1209 +}
      1210 +
      1211 +/* =========================================================================
      1212 + * SETTINGS GEAR BUTTON
      1213 + * ========================================================================= */
      1214 +
      1215 +.toolbar-settings-btn {
      1216 +    font-size: 14px;
      1217 +    padding: 4px 8px;
      1218 +    margin-left: 2px;
      1219 +    border-left: 1px solid #3a3a50;
      1220 +    border-radius: 0;
      1221 +    color: #666680;
      1222 +    transition: color 0.15s, background 0.15s;
      1223 +}
      1224 +
      1225 +.toolbar-settings-btn:hover {
      1226 +    color: #c0c0d0;
      1227 +    background: #3a3a50;
      1228 +}
      1229 +
      1230 +/* =========================================================================
      1231 + * TOOLBAR SETTINGS MODAL
      1232 + *
      1233 + * Per-tool visibility checkboxes, organized by tab section.
      1234 + * ========================================================================= */
      1235 +
      1236 +/* Wider modal box for 3-column settings layout */
      1237 +.modal-settings-box {
      1238 +    width: 460px;
      1239 +    max-width: calc(100vw - 40px);
      1240 +}
      1241 +
      1242 +/* Three-section layout: Navigate | Markup | Measure */
      1243 +.settings-tab-sections {
      1244 +    display: flex;
      1245 +    gap: 16px;
      1246 +    margin: 16px 0;
      1247 +}
      1248 +
      1249 +.settings-section {
      1250 +    flex: 1;
      1251 +    min-width: 0;
      1252 +}
      1253 +
      1254 +.settings-section-header {
      1255 +    font-size: 10px;
      1256 +    font-weight: 600;
      1257 +    text-transform: uppercase;
      1258 +    letter-spacing: 0.8px;
      1259 +    color: #8888a0;
      1260 +    padding-bottom: 6px;
      1261 +    border-bottom: 1px solid #3a3a50;
      1262 +    margin-bottom: 8px;
      1263 +}
      1264 +
      1265 +.settings-tool-list {
      1266 +    display: flex;
      1267 +    flex-direction: column;
      1268 +    gap: 6px;
      1269 +}
      1270 +
      1271 +/* Each tool checkbox row */
      1272 +.settings-tool-item {
      1273 +    display: flex;
      1274 +    align-items: center;
      1275 +    gap: 7px;
      1276 +    cursor: pointer;
      1277 +    padding: 2px 0;
      1278 +}
      1279 +
      1280 +.settings-tool-item:hover {
      1281 +    color: #e0e0f0;
      1282 +}
      1283 +
      1284 +.settings-tool-item input[type="checkbox"] {
      1285 +    width: 13px;
      1286 +    height: 13px;
      1287 +    margin: 0;
      1288 +    cursor: pointer;
      1289 +    accent-color: #4a9eff;
      1290 +    flex-shrink: 0;
      1291 +}
      1292 +
      1293 +.settings-tool-item label {
      1294 +    font-size: 12px;
      1295 +    color: #c0c0d0;
      1296 +    cursor: pointer;
      1297 +    white-space: nowrap;
      1298 +    overflow: hidden;
      1299 +    text-overflow: ellipsis;

      477 +        // Auto-switch to the correct tab when a keyboard shortcut activates a tool
      478 +        // that lives in a different tab. This keeps the toolbar in sync with the
      479 +        // active tool even when the user hasn't clicked the tab explicitly.
      480 +        if (toolName && this._TOOL_TAB) {

  ⎿  Added 125 lines
      617      }
      618
      619      // =========================================================================
      620 +    // TAB SWITCHING
      621 +    // =========================================================================
      622 +
      623 +    /**
      624 +     * Activate a toolbar tab and show its panel, hiding the others.
      625 +     *
      626 +     * Persists the selection to localStorage so it survives page reload.
      627 +     * Called by tab button clicks AND by setTool() when a keyboard shortcut
      628 +     * activates a tool in a different tab (auto-switch).
      629 +     *
      630 +     * Args:
      631 +     *   tabName: 'navigate' | 'markup' | 'measure'
      632 +     */
      633 +    _setActiveTab(tabName) {
      634 +        this._activeTab = tabName;
      635 +        localStorage.setItem('openrevu-toolbar-tab', tabName);
      636 +
      637 +        // Show/hide tab content panels
      638 +        document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel').forEach(panel => {
      639 +            panel.style.display = panel.dataset.tab === tabName ? '' : 'none';
      640 +        });
      641 +
      642 +        // Update tab button active state
      643 +        document.querySelectorAll('.toolbar-tab').forEach(btn => {
      644 +            btn.classList.toggle('active', btn.dataset.tab === tabName);
      645 +        });
      646 +    }
      647 +
      648 +    // =========================================================================
      649 +    // SETTINGS MODAL — per-tool visibility
      650 +    // =========================================================================
      651 +
      652 +    /**
      653 +     * Open the toolbar settings modal.
      654 +     *
      655 +     * Populates checkboxes for every .tool-btn element, organized by tab section.
      656 +     * Checkbox state is read from localStorage ('openrevu-tool-hidden-{toolName}').
      657 +     */
      658 +    _openSettings() {
      659 +        const modal = document.getElementById('modal-toolbar-settings');
      660 +        if (!modal) return;
      661 +
      662 +        this._populateSettingsLists();
      663 +        modal.style.display = 'flex';
      664 +    }
      665 +
      666 +    /**
      667 +     * Close the toolbar settings modal.
      668 +     */
      669 +    _closeSettings() {
      670 +        const modal = document.getElementById('modal-toolbar-settings');
      671 +        if (modal) modal.style.display = 'none';
      672 +    }
      673 +
      674 +    /**
      675 +     * Build the checkbox lists in the settings modal.
      676 +     *
      677 +     * Groups tool buttons by their tab (navigate/markup/measure) and creates
      678 +     * a labeled checkbox for each tool. Checking/unchecking immediately hides
      679 +     * or shows the button and persists the state in localStorage.
      680 +     *
      681 +     * WHY localStorage for tool visibility:
      682 +     *   This is a pure display preference that should persist across sessions
      683 +     *   without requiring a server round-trip. Tools themselves are never removed —
      684 +     *   just hidden — so the underlying keyboard shortcuts still work.
      685 +     */
      686 +    _populateSettingsLists() {
      687 +        const containers = {
      688 +            navigate: document.getElementById('settings-navigate-tools'),
      689 +            markup: document.getElementById('settings-markup-tools'),
      690 +            measure: document.getElementById('settings-measure-tools'),
      691 +        };
      692 +
      693 +        // Clear previous content
      694 +        for (const el of Object.values(containers)) {
      695 +            if (el) el.innerHTML = '';
      696 +        }
      697 +
      698 +        document.querySelectorAll('.tool-btn').forEach(btn => {
      699 +            const toolName = btn.dataset.tool;
      700 +            if (!toolName) return;
      701 +
      702 +            const tabName = this._TOOL_TAB[toolName] || 'markup';
      703 +            const container = containers[tabName];
      704 +            if (!container) return;
      705 +
      706 +            // Label = the button's text content (strip leading/trailing whitespace)
      707 +            const label = btn.textContent.trim();
      708 +            const key = `openrevu-tool-hidden-${toolName}`;
      709 +            const isHidden = localStorage.getItem(key) === 'true';
      710 +
      711 +            const item = document.createElement('label');
      712 +            item.className = 'settings-tool-item';
      713 +
      714 +            const cb = document.createElement('input');
      715 +            cb.type = 'checkbox';
      716 +            cb.checked = !isHidden;  // checked = visible
      717 +
      718 +            // Apply persisted visibility immediately (covers initial page load)
      719 +            btn.hidden = isHidden;
      720 +
      721 +            cb.addEventListener('change', () => {
      722 +                const hide = !cb.checked;
      723 +                btn.hidden = hide;
      724 +                if (hide) {
      725 +                    localStorage.setItem(key, 'true');
      726 +                } else {
      727 +                    localStorage.removeItem(key);
      728 +                }
      729 +            });
      730 +
      731 +            const labelText = document.createElement('label');
      732 +            labelText.textContent = label;
      733 +            // Point click events on the text label to the checkbox for UX
      734 +            const cbId = `settings-cb-${toolName}`;
      735 +            cb.id = cbId;
      736 +            labelText.htmlFor = cbId;
      737 +
      738 +            item.appendChild(cb);
      739 +            item.appendChild(labelText);
      740 +            container.appendChild(item);
      741 +        });
      742 +    }
      743 +
      744 +    // =========================================================================
      745      // INTENT MODE — markup type selection
      746      // =========================================================================
      747

● Now Task 7 — write test_phase3b.mjs. Let me look at a complete test file for structure reference:

● Read(ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_phase3a.mjs)
  ⎿  Read 150 lines

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have a good understanding of the test structure. Let me write the test_phase3b.mjs file. The plan calls for ~36
  tests covering:

  1. Tab switching (click): clicking Navigate/Markup/Measure tabs shows correct tools
  2. Auto-switch via keyboard shortcuts: pressing U should switch to Measure tab, G should switch to Navigate tab, R to
  Markup tab
  3. localStorage persistence: tab selection survives across navigations
  4. Settings modal: gear button opens modal, checkboxes work, reset works
  5. Pan/select bug fix validation: select mode with stopPropagation — drag in select doesn't pan

  6. Measurement recalc when moved: moving a distance group updates its label

  Now I'm organizing the test cases into groups: tab switching with clicks, auto-switching via keyboard shortcuts,
  localStorage persistence across page reloads, settings modal interactions, and the pan/select bug fix validation. 10.
  Pressing R (rect tool) → switches to Markup tab
  11. Pressing U (distance) → switches to Measure tab
  12. Pressing G (hand) → switches to Navigate tab
  13. Pressing P (pen) → switches to Markup tab
  14. Pressing A (area) → switches to Measure tab
  15. Pressing V (select) → switches to Navigate tab

  Group 3: localStorage persistence (4 tests)

  16. _activeTab equals stored tab name after clicking
  17. localStorage value updated after tab click
  18. After clicking Markup, localStorage has 'markup'
  19. After clicking Measure, localStorage has 'measure'

  Group 4: Settings modal (8 tests)

  20. Settings gear button exists
  21. Clicking gear opens settings modal
  22. Settings modal has navigate section
  23. Settings modal has markup section
  24. Settings modal has measure section
  25. Settings checkboxes are present
  26. Unchecking a tool hides the button
  27. Close button closes the modal
  28. Reset button restores hidden tools
  29. localStorage entries for hidden tools respected on reload

  Group 5: Bug fix — pan/select separation (5 tests)

  Now I need to verify the stopPropagation approach works correctly: when drawing mode is active, the pan handler gets
  attached to block unwanted panning, and when switching to select mode, the mousedown listener properly prevents
  propagation so pan doesn't interfere.

  Group 6: Measurement recalc when moved (5 tests)

  30. Distance group moved → pixelLength updated
  31. Distance group moved → label text updated
  32. Area polygon moved → companion label repositioned
  33. Area polygon scaled → pixelArea recalculated
  34. Area polygon scaled → label text updated

  I'm going to start writing these tests now, aiming for around 34 total. I should focus on what's actually testable
  through browser automation rather than trying to verify internal JavaScript state directly.

        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Phase 3B Browser Tests
        3  *
        4  * Tests: adaptive toolbar (mode tabs), settings modal, stopPropagation
        5  * pan fix verification, and measurement recalc on object:modified.
        6  *
        7  * Run via Windows Chrome from WSL:
        8  *   cmd.exe /c "cd C:\Users\User1\ClaudeProjects\OpenRevu && node test_phase3b.mjs"
        9  *
       10  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       11  * Version: 0.1.0
       12  * Date: 2026-02-23
       13  */
       14
       15 import { chromium } from 'playwright';
       16
       17 const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';
       18 const DOC_ID = 1;
       19 let passed = 0;
       20 let failed = 0;
       21
       22 function assert(condition, msg) {
       23     if (condition) {
       24         passed++;
       25         console.log(`  PASS: ${msg}`);
       26     } else {
       27         failed++;
       28         console.log(`  FAIL: ${msg}`);
       29     }
       30 }
       31
       32 async function getCanvasInfo(page) {
       33     return page.evaluate(() => {
       34         const el = document.getElementById('fabric-canvas');
       35         const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
       36         return {
       37             x: rect.left,
       38             y: rect.top,
       39             scale: window.app.viewer.zoom / 100,
       40         };
       41     });
       42 }
       43
       44 async function toPageCoords(page, naturalX, naturalY) {
       45     const info = await getCanvasInfo(page);
       46     return {
       47         x: info.x + naturalX * info.scale,
       48         y: info.y + naturalY * info.scale,
       49     };
       50 }
       51
       52 async function run() {
       53     const browser = await chromium.launch({
       54         headless: true,
       55         executablePath: 'C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe',
       56     });
       57     const context = await browser.newContext();
       58     const page = await context.newPage();
       59
       60     try {
       61         await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${DOC_ID}`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
       62         await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
       63
       64         // Clear pre-existing markups from previous test runs
       65         await page.evaluate(async () => {
       66             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.clear();
       67             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
       68             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
       69             await fetch(`/api/documents/${window.app.docId}/markups`, {
       70                 method: 'PUT',
       71                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
       72                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages: {} }),
       73             });
       74             window.app._dirty = false;
       75             // Clear any stored tab/tool visibility from previous test runs
       76             localStorage.removeItem('openrevu-toolbar-tab');
       77         });
       78         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
       79
       80         // =====================================================================
       81         // TEST GROUP 1: Tab Buttons Exist
       82         // =====================================================================
       83         console.log('\n--- Test Group 1: Tab Buttons ---');
       84
       85         const navigateTab = await page.$('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="navigate"]');
       86         assert(navigateTab !== null, 'Navigate tab button exists in toolbar');
       87
       88         const markupTab = await page.$('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="markup"]');
       89         assert(markupTab !== null, 'Markup tab button exists in toolbar');
       90
       91         const measureTab = await page.$('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="measure"]');
       92         assert(measureTab !== null, 'Measure tab button exists in toolbar');
       93
       94         const settingsBtn = await page.$('#btn-toolbar-settings');
       95         assert(settingsBtn !== null, 'Settings gear button exists in toolbar');
       96
       97         // Each tab panel should exist
       98         const navigatePanel = await page.$('.tab-panel[data-tab="navigate"]');
       99         assert(navigatePanel !== null, 'Navigate tab panel exists in DOM');
      100
      101         const markupPanel = await page.$('.tab-panel[data-tab="markup"]');
      102         assert(markupPanel !== null, 'Markup tab panel exists in DOM');
      103
      104         const measurePanel = await page.$('.tab-panel[data-tab="measure"]');
      105         assert(measurePanel !== null, 'Measure tab panel exists in DOM');
      106
      107         // =====================================================================
      108         // TEST GROUP 2: Tab Panel Visibility
      109         // =====================================================================
      110         console.log('\n--- Test Group 2: Tab Panel Visibility ---');
      111
      112         // Activate Navigate tab
      113         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      114         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      115
      116         const navigatePanelVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      117             const panel = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="navigate"]');
      118             return window.getComputedStyle(panel).display !== 'none';
      119         });
      120         assert(navigatePanelVisible, 'Navigate panel is visible when Navigate tab is active');
      121
      122         const markupPanelHidden = await page.evaluate(() => {
      123             const panel = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="markup"]');

      130             return panel.style.display === 'none';
      131         });
      132         assert(measurePanelHidden, 'Measure panel is hidden when Navigate tab is active');
      133
      134         // Activate Markup tab
      135         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('markup'));
      136         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      137
      138         const markupPanelVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      139             const panel = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="markup"]');
      140             return window.getComputedStyle(panel).display !== 'none';
      141         });
      142         assert(markupPanelVisible, 'Markup panel is visible when Markup tab is active');
      143
      144         const navigatePanelHiddenNow = await page.evaluate(() => {
      145             const panel = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="navigate"]');

      149
      150         // Activate Measure tab
