      153
      154         const measurePanelVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      155             const panel = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="measure"]');
      156             return window.getComputedStyle(panel).display !== 'none';
      157         });
      158         assert(measurePanelVisible, 'Measure panel is visible when Measure tab is active');
      159
      160         // =====================================================================
      161         // TEST GROUP 3: Active Tab Button Has .active Class
      162         // =====================================================================
      163         console.log('\n--- Test Group 3: Tab Active State ---');
      164
      165         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      166         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      167
      168         const navigateTabActive = await page.evaluate(() => {
      169             return document.querySelector('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="navigate"]').classList.contains('acti
          ve');
      170         });
      171         assert(navigateTabActive, 'Navigate tab button has .active class when Navigate is selected');
      172
      173         const markupTabNotActive = await page.evaluate(() => {
      174             return !document.querySelector('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="markup"]').classList.contains('activ
          e');
      175         });
      176         assert(markupTabNotActive, 'Markup tab button does NOT have .active when Navigate is active');
      177
      178         // =====================================================================
      179         // TEST GROUP 4: Tab Click Interaction
      180         // =====================================================================
      181         console.log('\n--- Test Group 4: Tab Click ---');
      182
      183         // Start on Navigate, click Markup tab
      184         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      185         await page.click('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="markup"]');
      186         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      187
      188         const activeTabAfterClick = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      189         assert(activeTabAfterClick === 'markup', 'Clicking Markup tab updates _activeTab to "markup"');
      190
      191         const markupTabHasActive = await page.evaluate(() => {
      192             return document.querySelector('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="markup"]').classList.contains('active
          ');
      193         });
      194         assert(markupTabHasActive, 'Markup tab button has .active after click');
      195
      196         // =====================================================================
      197         // TEST GROUP 5: localStorage Persistence
      198         // =====================================================================
      199         console.log('\n--- Test Group 5: localStorage Persistence ---');
      200
      201         await page.click('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="measure"]');
      202         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      203
      204         const storedTab = await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.getItem('openrevu-toolbar-tab'));
      205         assert(storedTab === 'measure', 'localStorage stores "measure" after clicking Measure tab');
      206
      207         await page.click('.toolbar-tab[data-tab="navigate"]');
      208         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      209
      210         const storedTabAfterNavigate = await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.getItem('openrevu-toolbar
          -tab'));
      211         assert(storedTabAfterNavigate === 'navigate', 'localStorage stores "navigate" after clicking Na
          vigate tab');
      212
      213         // =====================================================================
      214         // TEST GROUP 6: Auto-Tab Switch via Keyboard Shortcuts
      215         // =====================================================================
      216         console.log('\n--- Test Group 6: Auto-Tab Switch via Keyboard ---');
      217
      218         // Start on Navigate tab, press R (rect) → should auto-switch to Markup
      219         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      220         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      221
      222         await page.keyboard.press('r');
      223         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      224
      225         const tabAfterR = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      226         assert(tabAfterR === 'markup', 'Pressing R (rect) auto-switches to Markup tab');
      227
      228         // Press U (distance) → should auto-switch to Measure
      229         await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      230         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      231         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      232
      233         await page.keyboard.press('u');
      234         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      235
      236         const tabAfterU = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      237         assert(tabAfterU === 'measure', 'Pressing U (distance) auto-switches to Measure tab');
      238
      239         // Press V (select) → should auto-switch to Navigate
      240         await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      241         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('markup'));
      242         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      243
      244         await page.keyboard.press('v');
      245         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      246
      247         const tabAfterV = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      248         assert(tabAfterV === 'navigate', 'Pressing V (select) auto-switches to Navigate tab');
      249
      250         // Press G (hand) → should auto-switch to Navigate
      251         await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      252         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('markup'));
      253         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      254
      255         await page.keyboard.press('g');
      256         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      257
      258         const tabAfterG = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      259         assert(tabAfterG === 'navigate', 'Pressing G (hand) auto-switches to Navigate tab');
      260
      261         // Press A (area) → should auto-switch to Measure
      262         await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      263         await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('navigate'));
      264         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      265
      266         await page.keyboard.press('a');
      267         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      268
      269         const tabAfterA = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._activeTab);
      270         assert(tabAfterA === 'measure', 'Pressing A (area) auto-switches to Measure tab');
      271
      272         // =====================================================================
      273         // TEST GROUP 7: Settings Modal
      274         // =====================================================================
      275         console.log('\n--- Test Group 7: Settings Modal ---');
      276
      277         await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      278         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      279
      280         // Settings modal starts hidden
      281         const settingsModalHidden = await page.evaluate(() => {
      282             const modal = document.getElementById('modal-toolbar-settings');
      283             return modal.style.display === 'none';
      284         });
      285         assert(settingsModalHidden, 'Settings modal is hidden on startup');
      286
      287         // Click gear to open settings
      288         await page.click('#btn-toolbar-settings');
      289         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      290
      291         const settingsModalVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      292             const modal = document.getElementById('modal-toolbar-settings');
      293             return modal.style.display !== 'none';
      294         });
      295         assert(settingsModalVisible, 'Settings modal opens when gear button is clicked');
      296
      297         // Settings sections exist
      298         const navigateSectionExists = await page.$('#settings-navigate-tools');
      299         assert(navigateSectionExists !== null, 'Navigate tools section exists in settings modal');
      300
      301         const markupSectionExists = await page.$('#settings-markup-tools');
      302         assert(markupSectionExists !== null, 'Markup tools section exists in settings modal');
      303
      304         const measureSectionExists = await page.$('#settings-measure-tools');
      305         assert(measureSectionExists !== null, 'Measure tools section exists in settings modal');
      306
      307         // Checkboxes are populated (at least one in each section)
      308         const navigateCheckboxes = await page.$$('#settings-navigate-tools input[type="checkbox"]');
      309         assert(navigateCheckboxes.length > 0, 'Navigate section has at least one checkbox');
      310
      311         const markupCheckboxes = await page.$$('#settings-markup-tools input[type="checkbox"]');
      312         assert(markupCheckboxes.length > 0, 'Markup section has at least one checkbox');
      313
      314         const measureCheckboxes = await page.$$('#settings-measure-tools input[type="checkbox"]');
      315         assert(measureCheckboxes.length > 0, 'Measure section has at least one checkbox');
      316
      317         // Uncheck a tool (pen) → button should be hidden
      318         const penCheckbox = await page.$('#settings-cb-pen');
      319         assert(penCheckbox !== null, 'Pen tool checkbox exists in settings (id="settings-cb-pen")');
      320
      321         if (penCheckbox) {
      322             await penCheckbox.uncheck();
      323             await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      324
      325             const penBtnHidden = await page.evaluate(() => {
      326                 const btn = document.querySelector('button[data-tool="pen"]');
      327                 return btn && btn.hidden === true;
      328             });
      329             assert(penBtnHidden, 'Unchecking pen in settings hides the pen button');
      330
      331             const penHiddenInStorage = await page.evaluate(() => {
      332                 return localStorage.getItem('openrevu-tool-hidden-pen') === 'true';
      333             });
      334             assert(penHiddenInStorage, 'Hiding pen stores "true" in localStorage');
      335         }
      336
      337         // Reset button restores all tools
      338         await page.click('#settings-reset');
      339         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      340
      341         const penBtnRestored = await page.evaluate(() => {
      342             const btn = document.querySelector('button[data-tool="pen"]');
      343             return btn && btn.hidden === false;
      344         });
      345         assert(penBtnRestored, 'Reset button restores pen button visibility');
      346
      347         const penStorageCleared = await page.evaluate(() => {
      348             return localStorage.getItem('openrevu-tool-hidden-pen') === null;
      349         });
      350         assert(penStorageCleared, 'Reset button clears pen hidden state from localStorage');
      351
      352         // Close button closes the modal
      353         await page.click('#settings-close');
      354         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      355
      356         const settingsModalClosedAfterDone = await page.evaluate(() => {
      357             const modal = document.getElementById('modal-toolbar-settings');
      358             return modal.style.display === 'none';
      359         });
      360         assert(settingsModalClosedAfterDone, 'Settings modal closes when Done button is clicked');
      361
      362         // =====================================================================
      363         // TEST GROUP 8: stopPropagation Pan Fix (Bug 1)
      364         // =====================================================================
      365         console.log('\n--- Test Group 8: stopPropagation Pan Fix ---');
      366
      367         // setDrawingMode(true) should attach _panBlockHandler to wrapper
      368         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.toolbar.setTool('select'); });
      369         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      370
      371         const panBlockHandlerExists = await page.evaluate(() => {
      372             return typeof window.app.canvas._panBlockHandler === 'function';
      373         });
      374         assert(panBlockHandlerExists, 'setDrawingMode(true) creates _panBlockHandler on canvas');
      375
      376         // setDrawingMode(false) should remove _panBlockHandler
      377         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.canvas.setDrawingMode(false); });
      378         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      379
      380         const panBlockHandlerRemoved = await page.evaluate(() => {
      381             return window.app.canvas._panBlockHandler === null;
      382         });
      383         assert(panBlockHandlerRemoved, 'setDrawingMode(false) removes _panBlockHandler');
      384
      385         // Re-enable for further tests
      386         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.canvas.setDrawingMode(true); });
      387         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      388
      389         // Select tool: dragging an object should NOT pan the viewport
      390         await page.evaluate(() => {
      391             const { fabricCanvas } = window.app.canvas;
      392             const rect = new fabric.Rect({
      393                 left: 120, top: 120, width: 80, height: 60,
      394                 fill: 'transparent', stroke: '#ff4444', strokeWidth: 2,
      395                 selectable: true,
      396             });
      397             window.app.canvas.stampDefaults(rect, { markupType: 'issue' });
      398             fabricCanvas.add(rect);
      399             fabricCanvas.renderAll();
      400         });
      401         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      402
      403         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.toolbar.setTool('select'); });
      404         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      405
      406         // Reset scroll to 0
      407         await page.evaluate(() => {
      408             document.getElementById('viewport').scrollLeft = 0;
      409             document.getElementById('viewport').scrollTop = 0;
      410         });
      411
      412         const scrollBefore8 = await page.evaluate(() => ({
      413             left: document.getElementById('viewport').scrollLeft,
      414             top: document.getElementById('viewport').scrollTop,
      415         }));
      416
      417         // Drag the rect — should move the object, not pan the viewport
      418         const rectCenter = await toPageCoords(page, 160, 150);
      419         await page.mouse.move(rectCenter.x, rectCenter.y);
      420         await page.mouse.down();
      421         await page.mouse.move(rectCenter.x + 40, rectCenter.y + 30);
      422         await page.mouse.up();
      423         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      424
      425         const scrollAfter8 = await page.evaluate(() => ({
      426             left: document.getElementById('viewport').scrollLeft,
      427             top: document.getElementById('viewport').scrollTop,
      428         }));
      429
      430         assert(
      431             scrollAfter8.left === scrollBefore8.left && scrollAfter8.top === scrollBefore8.top,
      432             'Dragging rect in select mode does NOT pan viewport (stopPropagation fix active)'
      433         );
      434
      435         // =====================================================================
      436         // TEST GROUP 9: Measurement Recalc on object:modified (Bug 2)
      437         // =====================================================================
      438         console.log('\n--- Test Group 9: Measurement Recalc on Move ---');
      439
      440         // Clear canvas
      441         await page.evaluate(() => {
      442             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
      443             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
      444         });
      445         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      446
      447         // Draw a distance measurement programmatically
      448         await page.evaluate(() => {
      449             window.app.toolbar.setTool('distance');
      450         });
      451         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      452
      453         const distStart = await toPageCoords(page, 100, 200);
      454         const distEnd = await toPageCoords(page, 300, 200);
      455
      456         await page.mouse.move(distStart.x, distStart.y);
      457         await page.mouse.down();
      458         await page.mouse.move(distEnd.x, distEnd.y);
      459         await page.mouse.up();
      460         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      461
      462         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.toolbar.setTool('select'); });
      463         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      464
      465         // Capture initial pixelLength
      466         const initialPixelLength = await page.evaluate(() => {
      467             const objs = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects();
      468             const dist = objs.find(o => o.measurementType === 'distance');
      469             return dist ? dist.pixelLength : null;
      470         });
      471         assert(initialPixelLength !== null && initialPixelLength > 0, 'Distance group has pixelLength a
          fter drawing');
      472
      473         // Select and move the distance group
      474         await page.evaluate(() => {
      475             const objs = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects();
      476             const dist = objs.find(o => o.measurementType === 'distance');
      477             if (dist) {
      478                 window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.setActiveObject(dist);
      479             }
      480         });
      481         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      482
      483         const distGroupCenter = await toPageCoords(page, 200, 200);
      484         await page.mouse.move(distGroupCenter.x, distGroupCenter.y);
      485         await page.mouse.down();
      486         await page.mouse.move(distGroupCenter.x, distGroupCenter.y + 50);
      487         await page.mouse.up();
      488         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      489
      490         // After move, pixelLength should remain the same (distance didn't change — just position)
      491         const pixelLengthAfterMove = await page.evaluate(() => {
      492             const objs = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects();
      493             const dist = objs.find(o => o.measurementType === 'distance');
      494             return dist ? dist.pixelLength : null;
      495         });
      496         // A vertical move shouldn't change the length (it's horizontal)
      497         assert(
      498             pixelLengthAfterMove !== null,
      499             'Distance group still has pixelLength after move'
      500         );
      501         // The pixelLength should be approximately the same (allow 2px tolerance)
      502         const lengthStable = Math.abs((pixelLengthAfterMove || 0) - (initialPixelLength || 0)) < 2;
      503         assert(lengthStable, 'Distance pixelLength stays stable after a vertical move');
      504
      505         // Draw a horizontal line, then scale it to verify recalc fires
      506         await page.evaluate(() => {
      507             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
      508             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
      509         });
      510         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      511
      512         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.toolbar.setTool('distance'); });
      513         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      514
      515         const d2Start = await toPageCoords(page, 100, 300);
      516         const d2End = await toPageCoords(page, 300, 300);
      517         await page.mouse.move(d2Start.x, d2Start.y);
      518         await page.mouse.down();
      519         await page.mouse.move(d2End.x, d2End.y);
      520         await page.mouse.up();
      521         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      522
      523         await page.evaluate(() => { window.app.toolbar.setTool('select'); });
      524         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      525
      526         // The recalc listener is wired — verify initForCanvas ran
      527         const initForCanvasWired = await page.evaluate(() => {
      528             // Check that the measureTools has _fc pointing to the current Fabric canvas
      529             return window.app.measureTools._fc === window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      530         });
      531         assert(initForCanvasWired, 'measureTools._fc is wired to current Fabric canvas after initForCan
          vas()');
      532
      533         const initScaleWired = await page.evaluate(() => {
      534             return window.app.measureTools._scale === window.app.scale;
      535         });
      536         assert(initScaleWired, 'measureTools._scale is wired to ScaleManager after initForCanvas()');
      537
      538         // =====================================================================
      539         // TEST GROUP 10: Tool Buttons in Correct Tab Panels
      540         // =====================================================================
      541         console.log('\n--- Test Group 10: Tool Buttons in Correct Panels ---');
      542
      543         // Navigate panel should contain hand and select
      544         const handInNavigate = await page.evaluate(() => {
      545             const nav = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="navigate"]');
      546             return nav ? nav.querySelector('button[data-tool="hand"]') !== null : false;
      547         });
      548         assert(handInNavigate, 'Hand button is inside Navigate panel');
      549
      550         const selectInNavigate = await page.evaluate(() => {
      551             const nav = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="navigate"]');
      552             return nav ? nav.querySelector('button[data-tool="select"]') !== null : false;
      553         });
      554         assert(selectInNavigate, 'Select button is inside Navigate panel');
      555
      556         // Markup panel should contain pen, rect, etc.
      557         const rectInMarkup = await page.evaluate(() => {
      558             const mu = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="markup"]');
      559             return mu ? mu.querySelector('button[data-tool="rect"]') !== null : false;
      560         });
      561         assert(rectInMarkup, 'Rect button is inside Markup panel');
      562
      563         const penInMarkup = await page.evaluate(() => {
      564             const mu = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tool="markup"]');
      565             // Try alternate selector
      566             const mu2 = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="markup"]');
      567             return mu2 ? mu2.querySelector('button[data-tool="pen"]') !== null : false;
      568         });
      569         assert(penInMarkup, 'Pen button is inside Markup panel');
      570
      571         // Measure panel should contain distance, area, count, etc.
      572         const distanceInMeasure = await page.evaluate(() => {
      573             const ms = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="measure"]');
      574             return ms ? ms.querySelector('button[data-tool="distance"]') !== null : false;
      575         });
      576         assert(distanceInMeasure, 'Distance button is inside Measure panel');
      577
      578         const areaInMeasure = await page.evaluate(() => {
      579             const ms = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="measure"]');
      580             return ms ? ms.querySelector('button[data-tool="area"]') !== null : false;
      581         });
      582         assert(areaInMeasure, 'Area button is inside Measure panel');
      583
      584         const nodeEditInMeasure = await page.evaluate(() => {
      585             const ms = document.querySelector('.tab-panel[data-tab="measure"]');
      586             return ms ? ms.querySelector('button[data-tool="node-edit"]') !== null : false;
      587         });
      588         assert(nodeEditInMeasure, 'Node Edit button is inside Measure panel');
      589
      590     } catch (err) {
      591         console.error('Test suite error:', err);
      592         failed++;
      593     } finally {
      594         await browser.close();
      595     }
      596
      597     // =========================================================================
      598     // SUMMARY
      599     // =========================================================================
      600     console.log('\n' + '─'.repeat(50));
      601     console.log(`Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed, ${passed + failed} total`);
      602     console.log('─'.repeat(50));
      603
      604     process.exit(failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
      605 }
      606
      607 run();

∴ Thinking…

  There's one failure in test_shapes.mjs — the error is "element is not visible". This is a pre-existing test issue —
  the test is trying to click a tool button that's no longer visible because our tab redesign moved the tool buttons
  behind tabs. Let me look at what test_shapes.mjs is trying to do.

  The test_shapes.mjs fails at the tool activation test. The issue is that the old tests assumed all tool buttons were
  directly visible in the toolbar, but now they're inside tab panels that may be hidden. The test is clicking a button
  element that's not visible (because the toolbar tab panel is hidden).

  Let me look at test_shapes.mjs to understand exactly what's failing:

● One pre-existing test in test_shapes.mjs is failing because tool buttons now live inside tab panels. Let me check what
   it's doing:

● Read(ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_shapes.mjs)
  ⎿  Read 120 lines

∴ Thinking…

  I see the issue. On line 78, await rectBtn.click() fails because the rect button is inside the Markup tab panel which
  is now display:none by default. The old test used page.$() to get a reference to the hidden element and then tried to
  click it — but Playwright can't click hidden elements.

  The test_shapes.mjs starts with 5 tests (Group 1: Shape tool buttons) which pass, but then in Group 2, it tries to
  click rectBtn directly. The issue is that rectBtn was fetched while the Markup panel was hidden (since on startup we
  now default to Navigate tab), so the button exists in the DOM but is display:none inside the tab panel.

  The real fix is to switch to the Markup tab before attempting to click any of those tool buttons in the test file,
  rather than trying to work around the hidden state. The rect button click is failing because it's now hidden inside a
  tab panel that Playwright can't interact with. I could either update the test to switch to the Markup tab first, or
  change the default active tab from "navigate" to "markup" so those buttons are visible by default. The test clears
  localStorage at startup, which means it's relying on whatever the hardcoded default tab is, so I should probably just
  make "markup" the initial default instead of "navigate".

● The issue: test_shapes.mjs tries to click() markup tool buttons that are now hidden inside the tab panel. I need to
  update the test to switch to the Markup tab before clicking markup tool buttons:

● Read(ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_shapes.mjs)
  ⎿  Read 50 lines

∴ Thinking…

  The fix is simple: before clicking the markup tool buttons in Group 2, switch to the Markup tab. This is a minimal
  change to the existing test file.

● The fix is minimal — switch to the Markup tab before clicking markup tool buttons in Group 2:

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_shapes.mjs)
  ⎿  Added 5 lines
      74          // =====================================================================
      75          console.log('\n--- Test Group 2: Tool Activation ---');
      76
      77 +        // Switch to Markup tab first — markup tool buttons live in that panel.
      78 +        // Phase 3B introduced mode tabs; buttons are hidden when their panel is inactive.
      79 +        await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar._setActiveTab('markup'));
      80 +        await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      81 +
      82          // Click rect button
      83          await rectBtn.click();
      84          let activeToolName = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.toolbar.activeTool);

● 353/353 — Phase 3B is complete.

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The auto-tab-switch pattern: When a keyboard shortcut fires (e.g. U for distance), setTool() activates the tool AND
  calls _setActiveTab('measure') if the tool's home tab isn't currently shown. This keeps the toolbar in visual sync
  with the active tool even when the user bypasses the UI entirely — a key principle of mode-based interfaces (the mode
  indicator must always be truthful).

  stopPropagation vs. CSS: The old guard relied on pointer-events: none toggling via .drawing-active, but Fabric.js 6
  sets inline pointer-events on its canvas layers during rendering, which has higher CSS specificity than class-based
  rules. The addEventListener('mousedown', stopPropagation) approach is immune to this because it operates at the DOM
  event system level, after Fabric has processed the event but before it bubbles to #viewport.

  Measurement recalc architecture: initForCanvas() registers exactly one object:modified listener per document load. The
   listener is automatically garbage-collected when the old Fabric canvas is disposed (in canvas.init()'s cleanup path),
   so repeated document loads don't accumulate stale listeners.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Here's a summary of everything implemented:

  ---
  Phase 3B Complete — 353/353 tests ✓

  Bug Fix 1: Pan/Select Conflict (canvas.js)

  setDrawingMode() now adds a bubble-phase mousedown listener that calls e.stopPropagation() on Fabric's wrapper
  element. This is stored as canvas._panBlockHandler and removed on setDrawingMode(false). The CSS .drawing-active guard
   remains as a backup, but the event-level guard is authoritative.

  Bug Fix 2: Measurement Recalc on Move (measure.js + app.js)

  MeasureTools.initForCanvas(fc, scale) wires a global object:modified listener. When a distance Group is moved,
  _recalcDistanceGroup() recomputes endpoints via calcTransformMatrix() + transformPoint(). When an area polygon is
  scaled, _recalcAreaPolygon() reapplies scaleX/scaleY to the Shoelace area and repositions the companion IText label to
   the new centroid.

  Adaptive Toolbar (editor.html + style.css + toolbar.js)

  - Three mode tabs inline in the 42px toolbar bar: Navigate (Hand, Select) | Markup (8 annotation tools) | Measure (5
  measurement tools)
  - Tab selection persists in localStorage across sessions
  - Keyboard shortcuts auto-switch to the correct tab (e.g. U → Measure tab)
  - Settings gear opens a modal with per-tool checkboxes, organized by section; hide/show state persisted in
  localStorage

∴ Thinking…

  The PIPELINE.md is fairly outdated (written before the web-first decision) and describes a much earlier vision. The
  actual current state and next steps come from MEMORY.md and the plan notes.

  Let me synthesize what's actually next:

  Based on MEMORY.md: "ExtendedCognition becomes first plugin (Phase 4)"

  Based on the plan from the Phase 3B document:
  Phase 4: canvas.toSVG() export, optional SVG/PDF background import
  Phase 4: Relational metadata (ExtendedCognition plugin)
  The app becomes "Inkscape + measurements + semantic metadata" — which IS the goal

  The original pipeline's Phase 4 was about AI integration with Ollama and YOLO, but that's the outdated pre-web-first
  vision. The actual roadmap for the current web-first, FastAPI-backed version is much more grounded: SVG export
  leveraging Fabric.js's built-in capabilities, then a measurement summary panel for CSV exports, followed by the
  ExtendedCognition plugin as the AI layer, with a desktop shell coming eventually.

  Looking at the 3B document, it confirms Phase 4 should focus on SVG export and optional background import, plus the
  relational metadata through ExtendedCognition. So the clearest immediate priorities are SVG export from the canvas, a
  comprehensive measurement table that's exportable, and then the ExtendedCognition plugin architecture for AI
  integration.

● Based on the pipeline + plan notes, here's where things stand after Phase 3B completes:

  ---
  What's Next: Phase 4

  The Phase 3B plan itself outlined the Phase 4 roadmap:

  "The app becomes 'Inkscape + measurements + semantic metadata' — which IS the goal"

  Three tracks are ready to pick up, roughly in priority order:

  Track A — SVG Export (Quick Win, ~1 session)

  Fabric.js 6 has canvas.toSVG() built in. This means:
  - Export current page markup as SVG
  - Optional: SVG background import (so blank documents can import a floorplan SVG instead of PDF)
  - Low effort, high value — closes the "vector-native" gap the plan identified

  Track B — Measurement Summary Panel (~1-2 sessions)

  Currently measurements exist per page but there's no aggregate view:
  - A panel/modal showing all measurements across all pages
  - Totals by type (total area, total linear distance, count tallies)
  - Export to CSV for estimating workflows
  - This is the "takeoff report" that makes Phase 2 actually useful in practice

  Track C — ExtendedCognition Plugin / AI Layer (~3-4 sessions)

  The biggest one — MEMORY.md calls this "first plugin (Phase 4)":
  - Ollama connection manager (detect running models)
  - AI chat panel that knows the current document + markups
  - Semantic markup assist (AI suggests what a selected region might be)
  - The tool.measure.* / tool.markup.* API the pipeline describes

  ---
  My recommendation: Start with Track B (measurement summary). It's the feature that makes the existing Phase 2 work
  immediately useful for a real takeoff workflow, it has clear scope, and it paves the data model groundwork that the AI
   layer needs to query.

  Want to proceed with that, or go a different direction?

  The original pipeline's Phase 4 was about AI integration with Ollama and YOLO, but that's the outdated pre-web-first
  vision. The actual roadmap for the current web-first, FastAPI-backed version is much more grounded: SVG export
  leveraging Fabric.js's built-in capabilities, then a measurement summary panel for CSV exports, followed by the
  ExtendedCognition plugin as the AI layer, with a desktop shell coming eventually.

  Looking at the 3B document, it confirms Phase 4 should focus on SVG export and optional background import, plus the
  relational metadata through ExtendedCognition. So the clearest immediate priorities are SVG export from the canvas, a
  comprehensive measurement table that's exportable, and then the ExtendedCognition plugin architecture for AI
  integration.

● Based on the pipeline + plan notes, here's where things stand after Phase 3B completes:

  ---
  What's Next: Phase 4

  The Phase 3B plan itself outlined the Phase 4 roadmap:

  "The app becomes 'Inkscape + measurements + semantic metadata' — which IS the goal"

  Three tracks are ready to pick up, roughly in priority order:

  Track A — SVG Export (Quick Win, ~1 session)

  Fabric.js 6 has canvas.toSVG() built in. This means:
  - Export current page markup as SVG
  - Optional: SVG background import (so blank documents can import a floorplan SVG instead of PDF)
  - Low effort, high value — closes the "vector-native" gap the plan identified

  Track B — Measurement Summary Panel (~1-2 sessions)

  Currently measurements exist per page but there's no aggregate view:
  - A panel/modal showing all measurements across all pages
  - Totals by type (total area, total linear distance, count tallies)
  - Export to CSV for estimating workflows
  - This is the "takeoff report" that makes Phase 2 actually useful in practice

  Track C — ExtendedCognition Plugin / AI Layer (~3-4 sessions)

  The biggest one — MEMORY.md calls this "first plugin (Phase 4)":
  - Ollama connection manager (detect running models)
  - AI chat panel that knows the current document + markups
  - Semantic markup assist (AI suggests what a selected region might be)
  - The tool.measure.* / tool.markup.* API the pipeline describes

  ---
  My recommendation: Start with Track B (measurement summary). It's the feature that makes the existing Phase 2 work
  immediately useful for a real takeoff workflow, it has clear scope, and it paves the data model groundwork that the AI
   layer needs to query.

  Want to proceed with that, or go a different direction?

  Excellent! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me design the measurement summary panel plan.
   Here's what I know:

  Data model:
  - Distance: measurementType='distance', pixelLength, labelText, markupAuthor, markupTimestamp, Fabric.Group
  - Area: measurementType='area', pixelArea, labelText, pairedId, Fabric.Polygon (+ companion IText with same pairedId)
  - Count: measurementType='count', countIndex, countGroup, labelText, Fabric.Group

  Multi-page aggregation:

  I'll follow the same pattern as markup-list.js by combining live canvas objects from the current page with the
  pageMarkups map for all other pages, making sure to deduplicate area measurements since the polygon and its label
  share the same pairedId.

  Panel location and UI approach:
  The left panel already has "Pages" and "Markups" tabs, so I could add a "Measures" tab there to stay consistent with
  existing patterns, or create a separate floating panel triggered from the Measure toolbar tab.

  Option 1 feels right—adding a "Measures" tab to the left panel reuses the existing infrastructure and keeps
  measurement data alongside markup data.

  Panel content and functionality:
  I'll put a summary section at the top showing total distances, areas, and counts, then a sortable and filterable table
   listing all measurements across pages with type badges, formatted values, and page numbers. Each row should be
  clickable to navigate to that measurement, and I'll add a CSV export button that generates the file client-side with
  type, formatted value, raw pixel value, page, and timestamp.

  I need to create a new measure-summary.js file for the MeasureSummary class and update the HTML to add the "Measures"
  tab to the left panel.

  Now I'm mapping out the implementation: modifying the editor template to add the tab and content panel, adding CSS for
   the measurement table and summary cards, wiring up the MeasureSummary class in the main app, and creating tests to
  cover the new functionality—aiming to add around 28 tests to bring the total from 353 to 381.

  Building the MeasureSummary class...

  For multi-page aggregation, I need to handle deduplication carefully—area measurements have a paired polygon and text
  label, so I'll filter by checking the object type to only count the polygon itself, and for count measurements I'll
  only count the top-level group element, not the nested text inside it. Looking at the data structure more carefully,
  the area labels are actually top-level IText objects on the canvas, so I need to explicitly filter them out. The
  simplest approach is to exclude all IText objects from measurement aggregation since they're just display
  artifacts—the distance labels are nested inside Groups anyway, and the area labels are separate IText companions that
  shouldn't be counted toward totals.

  For the summary panel, I'm planning to show three stat cards displaying the count and total for each measurement type,
   then below that a table with columns for Type, Value, Page, and Time to list out all the individual measurements.

  Clicking a row will navigate to that page and highlight the corresponding markup, and there'll be an export button at
  the bottom to download the data as CSV with additional columns for raw pixel values, author, and timestamp.

  I'm calling this Phase 4A and will create a new file to handle the measurement summary panel logic.

  Now I'm mapping out the implementation across six files: the core class, the HTML template updates, styling, wiring it
   into the app, and a comprehensive test suite covering tab visibility, empty states, single and multi-page
  aggregation, summary statistics, and CSV export functionality.

  table—the agent mentioned it exists but didn't provide the exact implementation. Since I'm still in planning mode,
  I'll just note that I need to match that same interaction pattern.

  I have enough context now to write out the full plan.

  Looking at where to place the Measures panel, I'm weighing three options: adding it as a third tab in the left panel
  alongside Pages and Markups, creating a new right-side panel, or using a floating modal. The third tab approach feels
  most consistent with the existing UI and requires the least additional work, so I'll go with that.

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have all the information I need. Let me write the plan.

  Key findings:
  1. Panel tab switching: _bindTabSwitching() in markup-list.js handles tab switching for the left panel. It uses
  data-panel attribute on .panel-tab buttons and id="tab-{panel}" on .tab-content divs. Adding a "Measures" tab just
  requires: new button + new content div + updating _bindTabSwitching to handle data-panel="measures" refresh.
  2. Click-to-navigate pattern: this.onNavigate = (page, objIndex) callback, which in app.js calls
  this._navigateToMarkup(page, objIndex). I'll use the same pattern for measure-summary.js.

  3. Multi-page aggregation: Same as markup-list — live canvas + pageMarkups map.

  Now I'm designing the architecture. I'll create a new MeasureSummary class that mirrors the markup-list structure,
  with an init() method called from app.js, a refresh() method to rebuild and render entries, and an _aggregate() method
   that scans the live canvas and all pageMarkups to build the entries list, filtering for distance, area, and count
  measurements while skipping IText companions.

  I need to add the UI components to the editor template — a new tab button for measures, a panel with summary stat
  cards, a filter dropdown, a scrollable table, and an export CSV button. Then I'll update markup-list.js to handle the
  tab switching for the measures panel.

  For wiring the refresh logic, I'm debating whether to have MeasureSummary listen for its own tab events or let the tab
   switching handler call it directly. The cleanest approach is probably to let the existing tab-switching logic handle
  the DOM visibility, then have app.js wire up the refresh callback separately to avoid coupling the modules. This
  approach is clean and follows existing patterns. I'm mapping out the implementation: creating a new MeasureSummary
  class, modifying the editor template and styles, wiring up the tab switching logic in markup-list and app, and
  planning a comprehensive test suite covering the UI, data loading, and interactions.

  For testing, I'm thinking through the tab visibility and stat card rendering, then the empty state behavior, and
  finally the core functionality—creating different measurement types, verifying they aggregate correctly on the page,
  checking that formatted values respect the scale, and ensuring row clicks navigate properly.

  Beyond single-page measurements, I need to verify that measurements from multiple pages both appear and sum correctly
  in the stats, that page numbers display accurately, and that switching pages and reopening the Measures tab shows
  everything. I'm also planning CSV export tests to confirm the button works, headers and rows are correct, all pages
  are included, and values are formatted properly. For filtering, I want to test that the "All" type shows everything,
  individual type filters hide the others, and the filter state persists across refresh.

  Now I'm sketching out the MeasureSummary class...

  Defining the class structure... Building the entry object... Building the summary stats method... Writing the HTML
  structure... Building the measurement list interface...

        8 -## Part 0: Vector Strategy Decision (Address First)
        9 +## Context
       10
       10 -### User's Insight
       11 +Phase 2 added Distance/Area/Count tools. Each measurement is stored as a Fabric.js object
       12 +with `measurementType`, `pixelLength`/`pixelArea`/`countIndex`, and `labelText`. But there
       13 +is currently no way to see *all* measurements at once — you have to navigate page-by-page.
       14
       12 -The user realized: "What I want is more like Inkscape or Illustrator with shapes — but with
       13 -measurements and relational metadata on the objects."
       15 +Track B of the Phase 4 plan fills this gap: a measurement summary panel that aggregates
       16 +across pages, shows totals, and enables export to CSV for estimating workflows.
       17
       15 -This is actually **exactly what OpenRevu already is** — the user just doesn't see it yet because
       16 -the PDF-as-primary-surface framing obscures it. Here's the reality:
       17 -
       18 -| What the user wants | What OpenRevu already has |
       19 -|---------------------|--------------------------|
       20 -| Vector shapes | Fabric.js objects ARE vectors (defined as points/beziers, not pixels) |
       21 -| Measurements on shapes | Phase 2: Distance, Area, Count, Calibration |
       22 -| Relational metadata | markupType, markupStatus, pairedId, measurementType, etc. |
       23 -| Edit individual vertices | Phase 3A: Node Editor (polygon + line endpoints) |
       24 -| Blank canvas to draw on | "New Document" feature (batch 1 features) |
       25 -| Optional reference image | PDFs are already treated as a locked background layer |
       26 -
       27 -**What Fabric.js is:** A declarative vector graphics editor that RENDERS to HTML canvas.
       28 -Every rect, ellipse, polygon, line, path is defined in vector space (coordinates, not pixels).
       29 -Zooming doesn't pixelate shapes. Shoelace area and Euclidean distance work in real coordinates.
       30 -
       31 -**What "vector-native" (SVG.js / Paper.js / raw SVG) would give us above Fabric.js:**
       32 -- Native SVG DOM elements (CSS-styleable, searchable, copyable)
       33 -- Native SVG file format (`.svg`) instead of JSON
       34 -- Infinite zoom without any canvas rasterization artifacts
       35 -
       36 -**What a pivot would cost:**
       37 -- Re-implementing 300-test, 8-phase feature set from scratch (months of work)
       38 -- Paper.js / SVG.js / Konva.js don't have Fabric's rich object model (selection handles,
       39 -  transform controls, grouping, undo/redo, etc.) — all of that would need rebuilding
       40 -- Fabric.js 6 already has `canvas.toSVG()` export built-in — the gap is small
       41 -
       42 -**Recommendation: Stay on Fabric.js + add SVG import/export in Phase 4**
       43 -
       44 -The path forward:
       45 -1. Fix bugs + toolbar redesign now (Phase 3B — this plan)
       46 -2. Phase 4: `canvas.toSVG()` export, optional SVG/PDF background import
       47 -3. Phase 4: Relational metadata (ExtendedCognition plugin)
       48 -4. The app becomes "Inkscape + measurements + semantic metadata" — which IS the goal
       49 -
       50 -This avoids a 6-month rewrite. The vector editing already IS Fabric.js.
       51 -

       54 -## Part 1: Bug Fixes
       20 +## Architecture Overview
       21
       56 -### Bug 1 — Pan/Edit Conflict Still Happening in Select Mode
       22 +### New file: `static/js/measure-summary.js` — `MeasureSummary` class
       23
       58 -**Root cause:** The guard in `pdf-viewer.js:115` uses DOM traversal:
       59 -```javascript
       60 -e.target.closest('.canvas-container.drawing-active')
       24  ```
       62 -This is fragile: Fabric.js 6 may set `pointer-events` on canvas elements via inline styles
       63 -that override the CSS cascade, causing events to reach `#viewport`'s handler even when
       64 -`.drawing-active` is set. The CSS-guard approach is unreliable.
       65 -
       66 -**Reliable fix:** Add a bubble-phase `mousedown` listener directly on `fabricCanvas.wrapperEl`
       67 -that calls `e.stopPropagation()` whenever `drawing-active` is set.
       68 -Stops the event from ever reaching `#viewport` — no selector matching needed.
       69 -
       70 -```javascript
       71 -// In canvas.js setDrawingMode(enabled):
       72 -setDrawingMode(enabled) {
       73 -    const wrapper = this.fabricCanvas.wrapperEl;
       74 -    if (!wrapper) return;
       75 -    wrapper.classList.toggle('drawing-active', enabled);
       76 -
       77 -    if (enabled && !this._panBlockHandler) {
       78 -        // Bubble phase — fires after Fabric processes the event; stops it reaching #viewport
       79 -        this._panBlockHandler = (e) => e.stopPropagation();
       80 -        wrapper.addEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);
       81 -    } else if (!enabled && this._panBlockHandler) {
       82 -        wrapper.removeEventListener('mousedown', this._panBlockHandler);
       83 -        this._panBlockHandler = null;
       84 -    }
       85 -}

       26 +  ├── init(canvas, scale)        — wire canvas + scale, bind filter/export handlers
       27 +  ├── refresh()                  — aggregate + render (called on tab switch)
       28 +  ├── _aggregate()               — scan live canvas + all pageMarkups, build _entries[]
       29 +  ├── _buildEntry(obj, page, i)  — parse one Fabric object into a summary entry
       30 +  ├── _getSummaryStats()         — compute totals for stat cards
       31 +  ├── _renderStats()             — update DOM stat cards
       32 +  ├── _renderRows()              — render filtered table rows
       33 +  ├── _exportCSV()               — build + download CSV
       34 +  └── onNavigate                 — (page, objIndex) callback → app.js navigates
       35  ```

       94 -**Root cause:** The `object:modified` recalculation listeners in `node-editor.js` are ONLY
       95 -active during node edit mode. No global listener exists for ordinary move/scale in select mode.
       47 +### Entry Object Shape

      101 -- Area Polygon scaled/rotated: `pixelArea` wrong, label text wrong
      102 -
      103 -**Fix:** Add `initForCanvas(fc, scale)` to `MeasureTools` and wire a global `object:modified`

 Here is Claude's plan:
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 OpenRevu Phase 4A — Measurement Summary Panel

 Date: 2026-02-23
 Goal: Aggregate all measurements across all pages into a dedicated "Measures" tab in the
 left panel, with stat cards, a filterable table, click-to-navigate rows, and CSV export.

 ---
 Context

 Phase 2 added Distance/Area/Count tools. Each measurement is stored as a Fabric.js object
 with measurementType, pixelLength/pixelArea/countIndex, and labelText. But there
 is currently no way to see all measurements at once — you have to navigate page-by-page.

 Track B of the Phase 4 plan fills this gap: a measurement summary panel that aggregates
 across pages, shows totals, and enables export to CSV for estimating workflows.

 ---
 Architecture Overview

 New file: static/js/measure-summary.js — MeasureSummary class

 MeasureSummary
   ├── init(canvas, scale)        — wire canvas + scale, bind filter/export handlers
   ├── refresh()                  — aggregate + render (called on tab switch)
   ├── _aggregate()               — scan live canvas + all pageMarkups, build _entries[]
   ├── _buildEntry(obj, page, i)  — parse one Fabric object into a summary entry
   ├── _getSummaryStats()         — compute totals for stat cards
   ├── _renderStats()             — update DOM stat cards
   ├── _renderRows()              — render filtered table rows
   ├── _exportCSV()               — build + download CSV
   └── onNavigate                 — (page, objIndex) callback → app.js navigates

 Multi-page Aggregation Strategy

 Identical to markup-list.js (exact same pattern):
 - Live page: canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects()
 - All other pages: iterate canvas.pageMarkups Map (stored Fabric JSON)

 Area deduplication: Each area polygon has a companion IText label as a separate
 top-level canvas object (same pairedId). Skip IText when measurementType === 'area'
 — only count the Polygon.

 Entry Object Shape

 {
   type: 'distance' | 'area' | 'count',
   rawValue: number,        // pixelLength / pixelArea / countIndex
   formattedValue: string,  // from labelText or scale.format*()
   page: number,            // 0-based
   pageLabel: 'p.1',
   timestamp: string,       // ISO 8601
   author: string,
   objIndex: number,        // for onNavigate callback
 }

 Summary Stats

 Three stat cards computed from _entries:
 - Distances: N measurements, Total = scale.formatDistance(sum of pixelLengths)
 - Areas: N measurements, Total = scale.formatArea(sum of pixelAreas)
 - Count Items: N items total count markers placed

 CSV Export (client-side)

 Headers: Type, Value, Raw (px), Page, Author, Timestamp
 One row per entry. Triggered by "Export CSV" button. Uses Blob URL download.

 ---
 Files Changed

 ┌──────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │             File             │                               Change                               │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/measure-summary.js │ NEW — MeasureSummary class                                         │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ templates/editor.html        │ Add "Measures" panel tab + #tab-measures content + script tag      │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/css/style.css         │ Stat card styles + measure header styles                           │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/markup-list.js     │ Add measureSummary property; call refresh() on Measures tab switch │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/app.js             │ Instantiate + wire MeasureSummary after canvas init                │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ test_phase4a.mjs             │ NEW — ~30 tests                                                    │
 ├──────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ run_tests.mjs                │ Add test_phase4a.mjs                                               │
 └──────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ---
 Detailed Changes

 1. templates/editor.html

 In .panel-tabs div — add third tab button:
 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="measures">Measures</button>

 After #tab-markups div — add new content panel:
 <div id="tab-measures" class="tab-content">
     <div id="measure-stats" class="measure-stats">
         <div class="measure-stat-card">
             <div class="stat-count" id="stat-distance-count">0</div>
             <div class="stat-label">Distances</div>
             <div class="stat-total" id="stat-distance-total">—</div>
         </div>
         <div class="measure-stat-card">
             <div class="stat-count" id="stat-area-count">0</div>
             <div class="stat-label">Areas</div>
             <div class="stat-total" id="stat-area-total">—</div>
         </div>
         <div class="measure-stat-card">
             <div class="stat-count" id="stat-count-count">0</div>
             <div class="stat-label">Count Items</div>
             <div class="stat-total" id="stat-count-total">—</div>
         </div>
     </div>
     <div id="measure-list-header">
         <select id="measure-type-filter">
             <option value="all">All Types</option>
             <option value="distance">Distance</option>
             <option value="area">Area</option>
             <option value="count">Count</option>
         </select>
         <button id="btn-export-csv" class="toolbar-btn">Export CSV</button>
     </div>
     <div id="measure-list" class="markup-list">
         <p class="muted-text" id="measure-list-empty">No measurements yet</p>
     </div>
 </div>

 2. static/css/style.css — append Phase 4A section

 Stat cards: .measure-stats flex row, .measure-stat-card with count (20px blue),
 label (10px muted uppercase), total (11px monospace). Match existing dark theme
 (#1e1e2e bg, #4a9eff accent, #888 muted).

 #measure-list-header: flex row with gap, matching #markup-list-header layout.
 #btn-export-csv: smaller font/padding than toolbar buttons.

 3. static/js/markup-list.js

 Constructor — add:
 /** @type {MeasureSummary|null} Set by app.js after init. */
 this.measureSummary = null;

 _bindTabSwitching() — add after existing markups refresh call:
 if (tab.dataset.panel === 'measures' && this.measureSummary) {
     this.measureSummary.refresh();
 }

 4. static/js/app.js

 Constructor — add:
 this.measureSummary = new MeasureSummary();

 _onDocumentLoaded() — after measureTools.initForCanvas():
 if (!this.measureSummary.canvas) {
     this.measureSummary.init(this.canvas, this.scale);
     this.measureSummary.onNavigate = (page, objIndex) => {
         this._navigateToMarkup(page, objIndex);
     };
     this.markupList.measureSummary = this.measureSummary;
 }
 this.measureSummary.scale = this.scale;  // update scale ref on reload

 5. static/js/measure-summary.js (new file)

 Key implementation details:
 - _aggregate() mirrors markup-list.js:208-239 exactly
 - Area dedup filter: obj.measurementType === 'area' && (obj.type === 'IText' || obj.type === 'i-text') → skip
 - Rows reuse .markup-row + .markup-type-badge CSS for visual consistency
 - Row click: this.onNavigate(entry.page, entry.objIndex)
 - CSV download: new Blob([csv], {type:'text/csv'}) → URL.createObjectURL() → <a>.click() → revokeObjectURL()
 - If this.scale is null/unloaded: fall back to raw px values

 ---
 Test Plan: test_phase4a.mjs (~30 tests)

 Group 1: Tab UI (5)
 - Measures tab button exists in left panel
 - Clicking Measures tab activates #tab-measures
 - Clicking Measures tab deactivates #tab-markups content
 - Stat cards are present (distance/area/count)
 - #measure-list container present

 Group 2: Empty state (3)
 - All stat counts show 0 with no measurements
 - Empty message visible in #measure-list
 - Export CSV with no measurements → produces CSV with header line only

 Group 3: Single-page data (8)
 - Add distance → appears in Measures tab after switch
 - Add area polygon → appears as 1 entry (area IText NOT listed separately)
 - Add 3 count markers → count stat card shows 3
 - Stat card distance count = 1, area count = 1
 - Formatted total distance matches labelText
 - Formatted total area matches labelText
 - Click row → onNavigate fires with correct page + index
 - Type badge shows "distance" / "area" / "count"

 Group 4: Multi-page data (5)
 - Measurements on page 0 + page 1 both appear after tab switch
 - Total stats sum across pages (distance total = sum of both pages)
 - Page labels show correct p.1 / p.2
 - Adding to page 2, switching pages, opening Measures — all pages shown
 - refresh() updates counts correctly after adding more measurements

 Group 5: Type filter (4)
 - Filter "Distance" → only distance rows shown
 - Filter "Area" → only area rows shown
 - Filter "Count" → only count rows shown
 - Filter "All" → all rows shown

 Group 6: CSV export (5)
 - Export button exists
 - CSV starts with correct header row (Type,Value,Raw (px),Page,Author,Timestamp)
 - CSV has correct number of data rows (matches unfiltered entry count)
 - CSV includes page labels (p.1, p.2, etc.)
 - CSV formatted values match what is displayed in the panel

 ---
 Implementation Order

 1. static/js/measure-summary.js — write class
 2. templates/editor.html — add tab + content + script tag
 3. static/css/style.css — append styles
 4. static/js/markup-list.js — add measureSummary property + tab refresh call
 5. static/js/app.js — instantiate + wire MeasureSummary
 6. test_phase4a.mjs — write tests
 7. run_tests.mjs — add suite
 8. Run full test suite (target: 353 + ~30 = 383 passing)

 ---
 Reused Patterns

 - Multi-page iteration: markup-list.js:208-239 (live canvas + pageMarkups Map)
 - Row DOM structure: .markup-row + .markup-type-badge CSS classes (already styled)
 - Click-to-navigate: onNavigate(page, objIndex) → app.js._navigateToMarkup()
 - Panel tab switching: markup-list.js:_bindTabSwitching() auto-handles all .panel-tab
 - Modal for details: Not needed — panel is sufficient

│ left panel, with stat cards, a filterable table, click-to-navigate rows, and CSV export.                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Context                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Phase 2 added Distance/Area/Count tools. Each measurement is stored as a Fabric.js object                            │

│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Architecture Overview                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ New file: static/js/measure-summary.js — MeasureSummary class                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ MeasureSummary                                                                                                       │
│   ├── init(canvas, scale)        — wire canvas + scale, bind filter/export handlers                                  │
│   ├── refresh()                  — aggregate + render (called on tab switch)                                         │
│   ├── _aggregate()               — scan live canvas + all pageMarkups, build _entries[]                              │
│   ├── _buildEntry(obj, page, i)  — parse one Fabric object into a summary entry                                      │
│   ├── _getSummaryStats()         — compute totals for stat cards                                                     │
│   ├── _renderStats()             — update DOM stat cards                                                             │
│   ├── _renderRows()              — render filtered table rows                                                        │
│   ├── _exportCSV()               — build + download CSV                                                              │
│   └── onNavigate                 — (page, objIndex) callback → app.js navigates                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Multi-page Aggregation Strategy                                                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Identical to markup-list.js (exact same pattern):                                                                    │
│ - Live page: canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects()                                                                        │
│ - All other pages: iterate canvas.pageMarkups Map (stored Fabric JSON)                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Area deduplication: Each area polygon has a companion IText label as a separate                                      │
│ top-level canvas object (same pairedId). Skip IText when measurementType === 'area'                                  │
│ — only count the Polygon.                                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Entry Object Shape                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ {                                                                                                                    │
│   type: 'distance' | 'area' | 'count',                                                                               │
│   rawValue: number,        // pixelLength / pixelArea / countIndex                                                   │
│   formattedValue: string,  // from labelText or scale.format*()                                                      │
│   page: number,            // 0-based                                                                                │
│   pageLabel: 'p.1',                                                                                                  │
│   timestamp: string,       // ISO 8601                                                                               │
│   author: string,                                                                                                    │
│   objIndex: number,        // for onNavigate callback                                                                │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Summary Stats                                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Three stat cards computed from _entries:                                                                             │
│ - Distances: N measurements, Total = scale.formatDistance(sum of pixelLengths)                                       │
│ - Areas: N measurements, Total = scale.formatArea(sum of pixelAreas)                                                 │
│ - Count Items: N items total count markers placed                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ CSV Export (client-side)                                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Headers: Type, Value, Raw (px), Page, Author, Timestamp                                                              │
│ One row per entry. Triggered by "Export CSV" button. Uses Blob URL download.                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
