  Now I'm implementing the MeasureSummary class. I need to handle area measurements carefully — when an area object
  exists, it comes paired with an IText companion, so I'll skip IText objects that have a matching pairedId and only
  count the polygon itself. I need to identify which IText objects are companion labels by checking if they have
  measurementType === 'area' and are of type IText or i-text. For the UI changes, I'll add the Measures tab button and
  panel to editor.html after the markups section, and append styles to the CSS file. Let me examine the measure.js file
  to understand the structure of area polygon objects and their properties.

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have a clear picture. Let me implement everything.

  Key observations:
  1. Area companion IText has measurementType === 'area' (set via _recalcAreaPolygon) and pairedId matching the polygon.
  2. The plan says to skip obj.measurementType === 'area' && (obj.type === 'IText' || obj.type === 'i-text')

  Wait, let me double-check - does the companion IText for area have measurementType? Looking at _recalcAreaPolygon, it
  sets label.pixelArea and label.labelText, but does it set label.measurementType? Let me read more of the measure.js to
   find where the area IText is created.

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have a complete picture. The area companion IText has:
  - measurementType = 'area'
  - pixelArea
  - labelText
  - pairedId
  - Its type (from Fabric.js) is 'IText' or 'i-text'

  The polygon itself has type === 'Polygon' or 'polygon'.

  So to skip the companion IText, I need to check:
  obj.measurementType === 'area' && (obj.type === 'IText' || obj.type === 'i-text')



  Now I'm building the measurement summary module that aggregates all measurements across pages into a summary panel
  with stat cards, a filterable table, click-to-navigate rows, and CSV export functionality, mirroring the multi-page
  aggregation pattern from markup-list.js. Setting up the MeasureSummary class with properties for the canvas, scale
  manager, and a callback to handle navigation when users click on measurements in the summary panel. Caching DOM
  element references for the statistics display and control buttons, then binding event handlers to set up the panel's
  interactivity.

  Now I'm attaching listeners to the type filter dropdown and export button so they can trigger filtering and CSV export
   when the user interacts with them. The refresh method is starting to scan all pages for measurement data to rebuild
  the entire panel. Iterating through stored page markups to collect measurement entries, skipping the live page and
  filtering out area companion labels, then doing the same for live canvas objects on the current page. I'm checking if
  the object is a text label attached to an area measurement by verifying its type matches one of the text variants that
   Fabric uses, then I'll move on to building the summary entry from the measurement object.

  Now I'm implementing the entry builder method that extracts the raw and formatted values based on the measurement
  type—handling distance and area cases separately, using the scale formatter when available or falling back to default
  pixel-based labels.

  Finishing the switch statement for count and default cases, then returning a structured entry object with the type,
  values, page info, and metadata like timestamp and author.

  Moving into the stats computation section where I'll calculate totals across the three measurement types. Writing the
  stats rendering logic... Writing the stats display logic...

  Now I'm setting up the row rendering method that filters measurements based on the current type selection, then
  handles showing or hiding the empty state message depending on whether there are any results to display.

  Building the row elements... Building the markup row UI...

  Now I'm setting up the CSV export function with headers for type, value, raw measurements, page, author, and
  timestamp. I'm mapping through all entries to construct rows, properly escaping quoted values to handle commas in
  formatted numbers.

  Finishing the export logic...

  Now I'm planning out the file modifications needed across the codebase. I need to add the measures panel button and
  content to the HTML, update the CSS with stat card styles, initialize the MeasureSummary in the markup list and app
  constructors, and wire up the tab switching to refresh the measures display when needed.

● Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me implement everything.

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The area measurement creates TWO Fabric objects per measurement: a Polygon (the boundary) and an IText companion label
   — both with measurementType === 'area'. We must skip the IText during aggregation to avoid double-counting. The
  detection key is obj.type === 'IText' || obj.type === 'i-text' when measurementType === 'area'.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Step 1: Create static/js/measure-summary.js

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/measure-summary.js)
  ⎿  Wrote 511 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/measure-summary.js
        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Measurement Summary Module
        3  *
        4  * Purpose:
        5  *   Aggregates all measurement objects (distance, area, count) across every page
        6  *   into a dedicated "Measures" left-panel tab.  Provides:
        7  *     - Three stat cards: total count + summed value per type
        8  *     - Filterable, scrollable row list with page labels
        9  *     - Click-to-navigate rows (same _navigateToMarkup path as markup-list.js)
       10  *     - CSV export (client-side Blob download — no server involved)
       11  *
       12  * Architecture:
       13  *   Mirrors markup-list.js multi-page aggregation exactly:
       14  *     - Live page: canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects()
       15  *     - Other pages: canvas.pageMarkups Map (Fabric serialized JSON)
       16  *
       17  *   Area deduplication: each area measurement produces TWO Fabric objects —
       18  *   a Polygon (the primary) and a companion IText centroid label (pairedId match).
       19  *   Both carry measurementType === 'area'.  We skip the IText to avoid
       20  *   double-counting (detected by Fabric type: 'IText' / 'i-text').
       21  *
       22  * Security:
       23  *   - All user-visible text written via textContent (not innerHTML) — XSS safe
       24  *   - CSV download uses Blob URL + auto-revoke — no server upload
       25  *   - Type filter values validated against a known allowlist before use
       26  *   - No eval(), no dynamic code execution
       27  *
       28  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       29  * Version: 0.1.0
       30  * Date: 2026-02-23
       31  */
       32
       33 // =============================================================================
       34 // VALID MEASUREMENT TYPES — allowlist for filter validation
       35 // =============================================================================
       36
       37 const VALID_TYPES = new Set(['all', 'distance', 'area', 'count']);
       38
       39 // =============================================================================
       40 // MEASURE SUMMARY
       41 // =============================================================================
       42
       43 /**
       44  * Aggregates measurement objects from all pages and renders a summary panel.
       45  *
       46  * Usage:
       47  *   const summary = new MeasureSummary();
       48  *   summary.init(canvas, scale);
       49  *   summary.onNavigate = (page, objIndex) => { ... };
       50  *   // summary.refresh() is called automatically on Measures tab switch
       51  */
       52 export class MeasureSummary {
       53     constructor() {
       54         /** @type {import('./canvas.js').CanvasOverlay|null} */
       55         this.canvas = null;
       56
       57         /**
       58          * ScaleManager reference — used for live unit formatting.
       59          * Updated by app.js on each document load (scale preset may change).
       60          * @type {import('./scale.js').ScaleManager|null}
       61          */
       62         this.scale = null;
       63
       64         /**
       65          * Callback fired when the user clicks a row.
       66          * Args: (pageNumber: number, objectIndex: number)
       67          * → app.js._navigateToMarkup() handles page nav + object selection.
       68          * @type {Function|null}
       69          */
       70         this.onNavigate = null;
       71
       72         /** @type {Array<object>} All measurement entries across all pages (unfiltered) */
       73         this._entries = [];
       74
       75         /**
       76          * Active type filter for row display.
       77          * One of: 'all' | 'distance' | 'area' | 'count'
       78          * @type {string}
       79          */
       80         this._typeFilter = 'all';
       81
       82         // DOM element cache — set in init()
       83         this._els = {};
       84     }
       85
       86     // =========================================================================
       87     // INITIALIZATION
       88     // =========================================================================
       89
       90     /**
       91      * Initialize the Measures panel.
       92      *
       93      * Caches DOM element references, binds the type-filter dropdown and the
       94      * CSV export button.  Safe to call only once — app.js guards with
       95      * `if (!this.measureSummary.canvas)`.
       96      *
       97      * Args:
       98      *   canvas: CanvasOverlay instance (must already be initialized).
       99      *   scale:  ScaleManager instance for unit conversion.
      100      */
      101     init(canvas, scale) {
      102         this.canvas = canvas;
      103         this.scale = scale;
      104
      105         this._els = {
      106             statDistCount:  document.getElementById('stat-distance-count'),
      107             statDistTotal:  document.getElementById('stat-distance-total'),
      108             statAreaCount:  document.getElementById('stat-area-count'),
      109             statAreaTotal:  document.getElementById('stat-area-total'),
      110             statCntCount:   document.getElementById('stat-count-count'),
      111             statCntTotal:   document.getElementById('stat-count-total'),
      112             typeFilter:     document.getElementById('measure-type-filter'),
      113             exportBtn:      document.getElementById('btn-export-csv'),
      114             list:           document.getElementById('measure-list'),
      115             emptyMsg:       document.getElementById('measure-list-empty'),
      116         };
      117
      118         this._bindHandlers();
      119
      120         console.log('[MeasureSummary] Panel initialized');
      121     }
      122
      123     // =========================================================================
      124     // EVENT BINDING
      125     // =========================================================================
      126
      127     /**
      128      * Bind the type-filter select and Export CSV button.
      129      * Both are idempotent — only called once from init().
      130      */
      131     _bindHandlers() {
      132         if (this._els.typeFilter) {
      133             this._els.typeFilter.addEventListener('change', (e) => {
      134                 // Validate against allowlist — defense-in-depth even for local UI
      135                 const val = e.target.value;
      136                 this._typeFilter = VALID_TYPES.has(val) ? val : 'all';
      137                 this._renderRows();
      138             });
      139         }
      140
      141         if (this._els.exportBtn) {
      142             this._els.exportBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      143                 this._exportCSV();
      144             });
      145         }
      146     }
      147
      148     // =========================================================================
      149     // PUBLIC API
      150     // =========================================================================
      151
      152     /**
      153      * Aggregate all measurements and re-render the panel.
      154      *
      155      * Called by markup-list.js when the user switches to the Measures tab,
      156      * and may be called by app.js after content changes if needed.
      157      */
      158     refresh() {
      159         if (!this.canvas) return;
      160
      161         this._aggregate();
      162         this._renderStats();
      163         this._renderRows();
      164     }
      165
      166     // =========================================================================
      167     // AGGREGATION — scan live canvas + stored pageMarkups
      168     // =========================================================================
      169
      170     /**
      171      * Collect all measurement entries from every page.
      172      *
      173      * Multi-page strategy mirrors markup-list.js:208-239:
      174      *   - All pages *except* the live page come from canvas.pageMarkups (JSON).
      175      *   - The live page is scanned from canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects().
      176      *   This avoids calling onPageChanging() as a side-effect.
      177      *
      178      * Area companion IText labels are skipped here (see _isAreaCompanionLabel).
      179      */
      180     _aggregate() {
      181         this._entries = [];
      182
      183         // Use canvas.viewer.currentPage as the live page reference —
      184         // same approach as markup-list.js (safe here because we are called
      185         // on tab switch, not during page navigation debounce)
      186         const livePage = this.canvas.viewer ? this.canvas.viewer.currentPage : 0;
      187
      188         // ---- Stored pages (serialized JSON in pageMarkups Map) ----
      189         for (const [pageNum, fabricJson] of this.canvas.pageMarkups) {
      190             if (pageNum === livePage) continue;  // handled below via live canvas
      191             if (!fabricJson || !fabricJson.objects) continue;
      192
      193             fabricJson.objects.forEach((obj, idx) => {
      194                 if (!obj.measurementType) return;            // skip non-measurement objects
      195                 if (this._isAreaCompanionLabel(obj)) return; // skip area IText duplicates
      196                 this._entries.push(this._buildEntry(obj, pageNum, idx));
      197             });
      198         }
      199
      200         // ---- Live canvas (current page) ----
      201         if (this.canvas.fabricCanvas) {
      202             const liveObjs = this.canvas.fabricCanvas.getObjects();
      203             liveObjs.forEach((obj, idx) => {
      204                 if (!obj.measurementType) return;
      205                 if (this._isAreaCompanionLabel(obj)) return;
      206                 this._entries.push(this._buildEntry(obj, livePage, idx));
      207             });
      208         }
      209
      210         // Sort by page number, then by object index within page
      211         this._entries.sort((a, b) => a.page - b.page || a.objIndex - b.objIndex);
      212     }
      213
      214     /**
      215      * Determine if this Fabric object is the companion IText label of an area
      216      * measurement polygon.
      217      *
      218      * Each area measurement has TWO objects: the Polygon (primary, used for
      219      * stats and navigation) and an IText label placed at the centroid.  Both
      220      * carry measurementType === 'area' and a shared pairedId.  We skip the IText
      221      * to prevent double-counting in the summary panel.
      222      *
      223      * Fabric 6 serialized objects use the lowercase type strings ('i-text'),
      224      * while live canvas objects use PascalCase ('IText').
      225      *
      226      * Args:
      227      *   obj: Fabric object or serialized JSON representation.
      228      *
      229      * Returns:
      230      *   boolean — true if this object should be skipped.
      231      */
      232     _isAreaCompanionLabel(obj) {
      233         if (obj.measurementType !== 'area') return false;
      234         const t = obj.type;
      235         return t === 'IText' || t === 'i-text' || t === 'Textbox' || t === 'textbox';
      236     }
      237
      238     /**
      239      * Build a normalized summary entry from a Fabric measurement object.
      240      *
      241      * Handles both live canvas objects (have method bindings) and serialized
      242      * JSON objects (plain property bags) — both expose the same custom
      243      * measurement properties (measurementType, pixelLength, pixelArea, etc.).
      244      *
      245      * Args:
      246      *   obj:      Fabric object (live) or plain object (from serialized JSON).
      247      *   page:     Page number, zero-indexed.
      248      *   objIndex: Object's position in the page objects array (for navigation).
      249      *
      250      * Returns:
      251      *   Entry object: { type, rawValue, formattedValue, page, pageLabel,
      252      *                   timestamp, author, objIndex }
      253      */
      254     _buildEntry(obj, page, objIndex) {
      255         const type = obj.measurementType;
      256         let rawValue = 0;
      257         let formattedValue = '';
      258
      259         switch (type) {
      260             case 'distance':
      261                 rawValue = obj.pixelLength || 0;
      262                 // Prefer live scale for current formatting; fall back to baked labelText
      263                 formattedValue = (this.scale && rawValue > 0)
      264                     ? this.scale.formatDistance(rawValue)
      265                     : (obj.labelText || `${rawValue.toFixed(1)} px`);
      266                 break;
      267
      268             case 'area':
      269                 rawValue = obj.pixelArea || 0;
      270                 formattedValue = (this.scale && rawValue > 0)
      271                     ? this.scale.formatArea(rawValue)
      272                     : (obj.labelText || `${rawValue.toFixed(0)} sq px`);
      273                 break;
      274
      275             case 'count':
      276                 // Count items: rawValue = the counter index; no summed total
      277                 rawValue = obj.countIndex || 0;
      278                 formattedValue = obj.labelText || `#${rawValue}`;
      279                 break;
      280
      281             default:
      282                 rawValue = 0;
      283                 formattedValue = obj.labelText || '';
      284         }
      285
      286         return {
      287             type,
      288             rawValue,
      289             formattedValue,
      290             page,
      291             pageLabel: `p.${page + 1}`,
      292             timestamp: obj.markupTimestamp || '',
      293             author: obj.markupAuthor || '',
      294             objIndex,
      295         };
      296     }
      297
      298     // =========================================================================
      299     // STATS — totals for stat cards
      300     // =========================================================================
      301
      302     /**
      303      * Compute per-type counts and pixel totals across all entries.
      304      *
      305      * Returns an object:
      306      *   { distance: { count, totalPx }, area: { count, totalPx }, count: { count } }
      307      */
      308     _getSummaryStats() {
      309         const stats = {
      310             distance: { count: 0, totalPx: 0 },
      311             area:     { count: 0, totalPx: 0 },
      312             count:    { count: 0 },
      313         };
      314
      315         for (const e of this._entries) {
      316             if (e.type === 'distance') {
      317                 stats.distance.count++;
      318                 stats.distance.totalPx += e.rawValue;
      319             } else if (e.type === 'area') {
      320                 stats.area.count++;
      321                 stats.area.totalPx += e.rawValue;
      322             } else if (e.type === 'count') {
      323                 stats.count.count++;
      324             }
      325         }
      326
      327         return stats;
      328     }
      329
      330     // =========================================================================
      331     // RENDER — stat cards
      332     // =========================================================================
      333
      334     /**
      335      * Update the three stat cards (Distances / Areas / Count Items) with
      336      * current totals from _entries.
      337      */
      338     _renderStats() {
      339         const s = this._getSummaryStats();
      340         const els = this._els;
      341
      342         // Distance stat card
      343         if (els.statDistCount) els.statDistCount.textContent = s.distance.count;
      344         if (els.statDistTotal) {
      345             if (s.distance.count === 0) {
      346                 els.statDistTotal.textContent = '—';
      347             } else if (this.scale && s.distance.totalPx > 0) {
      348                 els.statDistTotal.textContent = this.scale.formatDistance(s.distance.totalPx);
      349             } else {
      350                 els.statDistTotal.textContent = `${s.distance.totalPx.toFixed(1)} px`;
      351             }
      352         }
      353
      354         // Area stat card
      355         if (els.statAreaCount) els.statAreaCount.textContent = s.area.count;
      356         if (els.statAreaTotal) {
      357             if (s.area.count === 0) {
      358                 els.statAreaTotal.textContent = '—';
      359             } else if (this.scale && s.area.totalPx > 0) {
      360                 els.statAreaTotal.textContent = this.scale.formatArea(s.area.totalPx);
      361             } else {
      362                 els.statAreaTotal.textContent = `${s.area.totalPx.toFixed(0)} sq px`;
      363             }
      364         }
      365
      366         // Count stat card
      367         if (els.statCntCount) els.statCntCount.textContent = s.count.count;
      368         if (els.statCntTotal) {
      369             // "Count items" have no meaningful sum — show placed count or dash
      370             els.statCntTotal.textContent = s.count.count > 0
      371                 ? `${s.count.count} placed`
      372                 : '—';
      373         }
      374     }
      375
      376     // =========================================================================
      377     // RENDER — row list
      378     // =========================================================================
      379
      380     /**
      381      * Render filtered measurement rows into #measure-list.
      382      * Applies the current type filter (_typeFilter).
      383      * Reuses .markup-row and .markup-type-badge CSS for visual consistency.
      384      */
      385     _renderRows() {
      386         if (!this._els.list) return;
      387
      388         const filtered = this._typeFilter === 'all'
      389             ? this._entries
      390             : this._entries.filter(e => e.type === this._typeFilter);
      391
      392         // Show/hide empty state message
      393         if (this._els.emptyMsg) {
      394             this._els.emptyMsg.style.display = filtered.length === 0 ? 'block' : 'none';
      395         }
      396
      397         // Clear existing rows (preserve the empty message element)
      398         this._els.list.querySelectorAll('.markup-row').forEach(r => r.remove());
      399
      400         // Build and append rows
      401         filtered.forEach(entry => {
      402             const row = this._createRow(entry);
      403             this._els.list.appendChild(row);
      404         });
      405     }
      406
      407     /**
      408      * Create a single measurement row element.
      409      *
      410      * Layout: [type badge] [formatted value] [page label]
      411      * Reuses .markup-row + .markup-type-badge CSS for visual consistency
      412      * with the Markups tab — same dark-theme row height and colors.
      413      *
      414      * Args:
      415      *   entry: Measurement entry from _entries.
      416      *
      417      * Returns:
      418      *   HTMLElement — the row div.
      419      */
      420     _createRow(entry) {
      421         const row = document.createElement('div');
      422         row.className = 'markup-row';
      423         row.dataset.page = entry.page;
      424         row.dataset.index = entry.objIndex;
      425
      426         // ---- Type badge ----
      427         const badge = document.createElement('span');
      428         badge.className = `markup-type-badge type-${entry.type}`;
      429         // SECURITY: textContent, not innerHTML — avoids XSS from labelText values
      430         badge.textContent = { distance: 'Dist', area: 'Area', count: 'Count' }[entry.type]
      431             || entry.type;
      432         row.appendChild(badge);
      433
      434         // ---- Content: formatted value ----
      435         const content = document.createElement('div');
      436         content.className = 'markup-row-content';
      437
      438         const valueLine = document.createElement('div');
      439         valueLine.className = 'markup-row-shape';
      440         // SECURITY: textContent, not innerHTML
      441         valueLine.textContent = entry.formattedValue;
      442         content.appendChild(valueLine);
      443
      444         row.appendChild(content);
      445
      446         // ---- Page label ----
      447         const pageEl = document.createElement('span');
      448         pageEl.className = 'markup-row-page';
      449         pageEl.textContent = entry.pageLabel;
      450         row.appendChild(pageEl);
      451
      452         // ---- Click → navigate ----
      453         row.addEventListener('click', () => {
      454             if (this.onNavigate) {
      455                 this.onNavigate(entry.page, entry.objIndex);
      456             }
      457         });
      458
      459         return row;
      460     }
      461
      462     // =========================================================================
      463     // CSV EXPORT
      464     // =========================================================================
      465
      466     /**
      467      * Build a CSV string from all entries (unfiltered) and trigger a download.
      468      *
      469      * Headers: Type, Value, Raw (px), Page, Author, Timestamp
      470      * Uses all _entries regardless of current type filter — the export should
      471      * always be complete.
      472      *
      473      * Client-side only — creates a Blob URL, clicks a hidden <a>, then revokes
      474      * the URL to free memory.  No server upload.
      475      */
      476     _exportCSV() {
      477         const headers = ['Type', 'Value', 'Raw (px)', 'Page', 'Author', 'Timestamp'];
      478
      479         const rows = this._entries.map(e => [
      480             e.type,
      481             // Wrap in double-quotes and escape internal double-quotes (RFC 4180)
      482             `"${e.formattedValue.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`,
      483             e.rawValue.toFixed(2),
      484             e.pageLabel,
      485             e.author || '',
      486             e.timestamp || '',
      487         ]);
      488
      489         const csvLines = [
      490             headers.join(','),
      491             ...rows.map(r => r.join(',')),
      492         ];
      493
      494         const csv = csvLines.join('\r\n');  // RFC 4180 line ending
      495
      496         // Build a Blob and trigger a synthetic download link click
      497         const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv;charset=utf-8;' });
      498         const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
      499
      500         const a = document.createElement('a');
      501         a.href = url;
      502         a.download = 'measurements.csv';
      503         // Must be in the DOM for Firefox compatibility
      504         document.body.appendChild(a);
      505         a.click();
      506         document.body.removeChild(a);
      507
      508         // Revoke the object URL to free the Blob memory
      509         URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
      510     }
      511 }

      1302 +/* =========================================================================
      1303 + * PHASE 4A — MEASURES PANEL (Measurement Summary)
      1304 + *
      1305 + * Stat cards show aggregated distance / area / count totals.
      1306 + * The row list reuses .markup-row / .markup-type-badge styles from the
      1307 + * Markups tab so measurements look visually consistent.
      1308 + * ========================================================================= */
      1309 +
      1310 +/* Three stat cards in a horizontal flex row across the panel */
      1311 +.measure-stats {
      1312 +    display: flex;
      1313 +    flex-direction: row;
      1314 +    gap: 4px;
      1315 +    padding: 8px 6px 6px 6px;
      1316 +    flex-shrink: 0;
      1317 +    border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a3d;
      1318 +}

      1320 +/* Individual stat card — centered column layout */
      1321 +.measure-stat-card {
      1322 +    flex: 1;
      1323 +    background: #1e1e2e;
      1324 +    border: 1px solid #2a2a3d;

      1326 +    padding: 6px 4px;

      1328 +    display: flex;
      1329 +    flex-direction: column;
      1330 +    align-items: center;
      1331 +    gap: 2px;
      1332 +    min-width: 0;  /* allow flex shrink past content size */
      1333 +}

      182      }

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/app.js)

      216          // Must run after canvas.init() creates a fresh Fabric canvas instance.
      217          this.measureTools.initForCanvas(this.canvas.fabricCanvas, this.scale);
      218
      219 +        // Initialize or rebind MeasureSummary — aggregates all measurements across
      220 +        // pages for the Measures tab in the left panel.
      221 +        // Guard with canvas check: init() only runs once (first document load);
      222 +        // subsequent loads just update the scale reference for fresh formatting.
      223 +        if (!this.measureSummary.canvas) {
      224 +            this.measureSummary.init(this.canvas, this.scale);
      225 +            this.measureSummary.onNavigate = (page, objIndex) => {
      226 +                this._navigateToMarkup(page, objIndex);
      227 +            };
      228 +            // Cross-wire: MarkupList owns the tab-switching code, so it triggers
      229 +            // measureSummary.refresh() when the user clicks the Measures tab.
      230 +            this.markupList.measureSummary = this.measureSummary;
      231 +        }
      232 +        // Always update scale ref in case user loaded a different document
      233 +        // (scale preset may have changed from the previous document's settings)
      234 +        this.measureSummary.scale = this.scale;

● Step 6: Create test_phase4a.mjs

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The Playwright tests need to click the left-panel "Measures" tab (.panel-tab[data-panel="measures"]), distinct from
  the toolbar mode tabs (button.toolbar-tab). The panel tabs use data-panel while toolbar tabs use data-tab — same
  visual style but different selectors.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_phase4a.mjs)
  ⎿  Wrote 544 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_phase4a.mjs
        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Phase 4A Measurement Summary Panel Browser Tests
        3  *
        4  * Tests the Measures tab in the left panel: stat cards, filterable row list,
        5  * click-to-navigate, multi-page aggregation, area deduplication, and CSV export.
        6  *
        7  * Groups:
        8  *   1. Tab UI (5)          — Measures tab exists, activates, deactivates markups
        9  *   2. Empty state (3)     — Zero counts, empty message, CSV header only
       10  *   3. Single-page (8)     — Distance/area/count appear, deduplication, navigation
       11  *   4. Multi-page (5)      — Cross-page aggregation, page labels, totals sum
       12  *   5. Type filter (4)     — Per-type filter shows only matching rows
       13  *   6. CSV export (5)      — Button exists, correct headers, row count, page labels
       14  *
       15  * Total: 30 tests
       16  *
       17  * Run via Windows Chrome from WSL:
       18  *   cmd.exe /c "cd C:\Users\User1\ClaudeProjects\OpenRevu && node test_phase4a.mjs"
       19  *
       20  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       21  * Version: 0.1.0
       22  * Date: 2026-02-23
       23  */
       24
       25 import { chromium } from 'playwright';
       26
       27 const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';
       28 const DOC_ID = 1;
       29 let passed = 0;
       30 let failed = 0;
       31
       32 function assert(condition, msg) {
       33     if (condition) {
       34         passed++;
       35         console.log(`  PASS: ${msg}`);
       36     } else {
       37         failed++;
       38         console.log(`  FAIL: ${msg}`);
       39     }
       40 }
       41
       42 async function getCanvasInfo(page) {
       43     return page.evaluate(() => {
       44         const el = document.getElementById('fabric-canvas');
       45         const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
       46         return {
       47             x: rect.left,
       48             y: rect.top,
       49             scale: window.app.viewer.zoom / 100,
       50         };
       51     });
       52 }
       53
       54 async function toPageCoords(page, naturalX, naturalY) {
       55     const info = await getCanvasInfo(page);
       56     return {
       57         x: info.x + naturalX * info.scale,
       58         y: info.y + naturalY * info.scale,
       59     };
       60 }
       61
       62 /**
       63  * Switch to the Measures tab in the left panel and wait for render.
       64  * Uses the panel-tab selector (not the toolbar-tab selector).
       65  */
       66 async function openMeasuresTab(page) {
       67     await page.click('.panel-tab[data-panel="measures"]');
       68     await page.waitForTimeout(200);
       69 }
       70
       71 /**
       72  * Draw a distance measurement line on the canvas (natural coords).
       73  * Activates the Measure toolbar tab first so the distance button is visible.
       74  */
       75 async function drawDistance(page, x1, y1, x2, y2) {
       76     // Ensure Measure tab is active in toolbar so distance button is accessible
       77     await page.evaluate(() => {
       78         const measureTab = document.querySelector('button.toolbar-tab[data-tab="measure"]');
       79         if (measureTab) measureTab.click();
       80     });
       81     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
       82
       83     await page.keyboard.press('u');  // U = distance tool
       84     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
       85
       86     const start = await toPageCoords(page, x1, y1);
       87     const end   = await toPageCoords(page, x2, y2);
       88
       89     await page.mouse.move(start.x, start.y);
       90     await page.mouse.down();
       91     await page.mouse.move(end.x, end.y, { steps: 5 });
       92     await page.mouse.up();
       93     await page.waitForTimeout(300);
       94
       95     // Return to select
       96     await page.keyboard.press('v');
       97     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
       98 }
       99
      100 /**
      101  * Draw a triangular area polygon (3 vertices + click to close).
      102  * Activates the Measure toolbar tab first.
      103  */
      104 async function drawArea(page, x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3) {
      105     await page.evaluate(() => {
      106         const measureTab = document.querySelector('button.toolbar-tab[data-tab="measure"]');
      107         if (measureTab) measureTab.click();
      108     });
      109     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      110
      111     await page.keyboard.press('a');  // A = area tool
      112     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      113
      114     const p1 = await toPageCoords(page, x1, y1);
      115     const p2 = await toPageCoords(page, x2, y2);
      116     const p3 = await toPageCoords(page, x3, y3);
      117     // Click near p1 to close polygon
      118     const pClose = await toPageCoords(page, x1 + 2, y1 + 2);
      119
      120     await page.mouse.click(p1.x, p1.y);
      121     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      122     await page.mouse.click(p2.x, p2.y);
      123     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      124     await page.mouse.click(p3.x, p3.y);
      125     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      126     // Close: click snap-threshold distance from vertex 1
      127     await page.mouse.click(pClose.x, pClose.y);
      128     await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      129
      130     await page.keyboard.press('v');
      131     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      132 }
      133
      134 /**
      135  * Place one count marker on the canvas.
      136  * Activates Measure toolbar tab first.
      137  */
      138 async function placeCount(page, x, y) {
      139     await page.evaluate(() => {
      140         const measureTab = document.querySelector('button.toolbar-tab[data-tab="measure"]');
      141         if (measureTab) measureTab.click();
      142     });
      143     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      144
      145     await page.keyboard.press('n');  // N = count tool
      146     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      147
      148     const pt = await toPageCoords(page, x, y);
      149     await page.mouse.click(pt.x, pt.y);
      150     await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      151
      152     await page.keyboard.press('v');
      153     await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      154 }
      155
      156 async function run() {
      157     const browser = await chromium.launch({
      158         headless: true,
      159         executablePath: 'C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe',
      160     });
      161     const context = await browser.newContext();
      162     const page = await context.newPage();
      163
      164     try {
      165         await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${DOC_ID}`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
      166         await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
      167
      168         // Clear any markups from previous test runs so we start from a known state
      169         await page.evaluate(async () => {
      170             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.clear();
      171             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
      172             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
      173             await fetch(`/api/documents/${window.app.docId}/markups`, {
      174                 method: 'PUT',
      175                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      176                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages: {} }),
      177             });
      178             window.app._dirty = false;
      179         });
      180         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
      181
      182         // =====================================================================
      183         // TEST GROUP 1: Tab UI
      184         // =====================================================================
      185         console.log('\n--- Test Group 1: Tab UI ---');
      186
      187         // 1.1 — Measures tab button exists in left panel
      188         const measuresTabBtn = await page.$('.panel-tab[data-panel="measures"]');
      189         assert(measuresTabBtn !== null, 'Measures tab button exists in left panel');
      190
      191         // 1.2 — Clicking Measures tab activates #tab-measures panel
      192         await page.click('.panel-tab[data-panel="measures"]');
      193         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      194         const measuresActive = await page.$eval(
      195             '#tab-measures',
      196             el => el.classList.contains('active')
      197         );
      198         assert(measuresActive, 'Clicking Measures tab makes #tab-measures active');
      199
      200         // 1.3 — Markups tab content deactivates when Measures is clicked
      201         const markupsInactive = await page.$eval(
      202             '#tab-markups',
      203             el => !el.classList.contains('active')
      204         );
      205         assert(markupsInactive, '#tab-markups deactivates when Measures tab clicked');
      206
      207         // 1.4 — All three stat cards are present in the DOM
      208         const statCardsCount = await page.$$eval('.measure-stat-card', els => els.length);
      209         assert(statCardsCount === 3, 'Three stat cards exist in #tab-measures');
      210
      211         // 1.5 — #measure-list container is present
      212         const measureListEl = await page.$('#measure-list');
      213         assert(measureListEl !== null, '#measure-list container exists');
      214
      215         // =====================================================================
      216         // TEST GROUP 2: Empty state
      217         // =====================================================================
      218         console.log('\n--- Test Group 2: Empty State ---');
      219
      220         // Make sure we are on the Measures tab (from 1.2 we are, but be explicit)
      221         await openMeasuresTab(page);
      222
      223         // 2.1 — All stat count cells show "0" with no measurements
      224         const distCount0 = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      225         const areaCount0 = await page.$eval('#stat-area-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      226         const cntCount0  = await page.$eval('#stat-count-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      227         assert(
      228             distCount0 === '0' && areaCount0 === '0' && cntCount0 === '0',
      229             'All stat counts show 0 when no measurements exist'
      230         );
      231
      232         // 2.2 — Empty message visible in #measure-list
      233         const emptyVisible = await page.$eval(
      234             '#measure-list-empty',
      235             el => el.style.display !== 'none' && el.textContent.includes('No measurements')
      236         );
      237         assert(emptyVisible, 'Empty message is visible when no measurements exist');
      238
      239         // 2.3 — Export CSV with no measurements → download URL triggered (count rows = 0)
      240         //       We capture the download URL via page.waitForEvent, then check content.
      241         //       Instead, test via JS interception: stub URL.createObjectURL and capture csv.
      242         const csvContent = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      243             // Intercept Blob download by temporarily patching createObjectURL
      244             let capturedCsv = null;
      245             const origCreate = URL.createObjectURL.bind(URL);
      246             URL.createObjectURL = (blob) => {
      247                 return blob.text().then(t => { capturedCsv = t; });
      248             };
      249             window.app.measureSummary._exportCSV();
      250             // Give any async .text() a tick
      251             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
      252             URL.createObjectURL = origCreate;
      253             return capturedCsv;
      254         });
      255         // CSV should start with the header line
      256         assert(
      257             typeof csvContent === 'string' && csvContent.startsWith('Type,Value,Raw (px),Page'),
      258             'Empty CSV starts with correct header row'
      259         );
      260
      261         // =====================================================================
      262         // TEST GROUP 3: Single-page data
      263         // =====================================================================
      264         console.log('\n--- Test Group 3: Single-page data ---');
      265
      266         // Draw measurements: 1 distance, 1 area, 3 count markers
      267         await drawDistance(page, 100, 150, 400, 150);   // ~300px horizontal distance
      268         await drawArea(page, 200, 300, 350, 300, 275, 400); // triangle
      269         await placeCount(page, 150, 250);
      270         await placeCount(page, 200, 250);
      271         await placeCount(page, 250, 250);
      272
      273         // Open Measures tab to refresh
      274         await openMeasuresTab(page);
      275
      276         // 3.1 — Distance appears in the list (count card = 1)
      277         const distCount1 = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      278         assert(distCount1 === '1', 'Distance stat count = 1 after drawing one distance');
      279
      280         // 3.2 — Area appears as 1 entry (IText companion NOT counted separately)
      281         const areaCount1 = await page.$eval('#stat-area-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      282         assert(areaCount1 === '1', 'Area stat count = 1 (IText companion deduplicated)');
      283
      284         // 3.3 — Count stat shows 3
      285         const cntCount3 = await page.$eval('#stat-count-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      286         assert(cntCount3 === '3', 'Count stat = 3 after placing 3 count markers');
      287
      288         // 3.4 — Total row count in list = 1 distance + 1 area + 3 count = 5
      289         const totalRows = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els => els.length);
      290         assert(totalRows === 5, `Row list has 5 entries (dist+area+3count), got ${totalRows}`);
      291
      292         // 3.5 — Distance stat total is non-dash (has a formatted value)
      293         const distTotal1 = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-total', el => el.textContent.trim());
      294         assert(distTotal1 !== '—' && distTotal1.length > 0, 'Distance total is formatted (not —)');
      295
      296         // 3.6 — Area stat total is non-dash
      297         const areaTotal1 = await page.$eval('#stat-area-total', el => el.textContent.trim());
      298         assert(areaTotal1 !== '—' && areaTotal1.length > 0, 'Area total is formatted (not —)');
      299
      300         // 3.7 — Type badge shows correct labels
      301         const badges = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-type-badge', els =>
      302             els.map(el => el.textContent.trim())
      303         );
      304         assert(badges.includes('Dist'), 'Dist badge appears in row list');
      305         assert(badges.includes('Area'), 'Area badge appears in row list');
      306         assert(badges.some(b => b === 'Count'), 'Count badge appears in row list');
      307
      308         // 3.8 — Click distance row → onNavigate fires with correct page (0)
      309         //        We capture the navigation call by overriding the callback
      310         const navResult = await page.evaluate(() => {
      311             let capturedPage = -1;
      312             window.app.measureSummary.onNavigate = (pg, idx) => { capturedPage = pg; };
      313             const distRow = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('#measure-list .markup-row'))
      314                 .find(r => r.querySelector('.type-distance'));
      315             if (distRow) distRow.click();
      316             return capturedPage;
      317         });
      318         assert(navResult === 0, `Row click fires onNavigate with page=0, got page=${navResult}`);
      319
      320         // =====================================================================
      321         // TEST GROUP 4: Multi-page data
      322         // =====================================================================
      323         console.log('\n--- Test Group 4: Multi-page data ---');
      324
      325         // Save current page markups and navigate to page 2 (if available)
      326         // We simulate multi-page by directly stuffing page 1 markups into pageMarkups
      327         await page.evaluate(() => {
      328             // Save current live objects to page 0 in pageMarkups
      329             window.app.canvas.onPageChanging(0);
      330             // Inject a synthetic distance measurement on page 1
      331             const fakePage1Json = {
      332                 objects: [
      333                     {
      334                         type: 'Group',
      335                         measurementType: 'distance',
      336                         pixelLength: 200,
      337                         labelText: '200.0 px',
      338                         markupAuthor: 'TestUser',
      339                         markupTimestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      340                         left: 100, top: 100, width: 200, height: 20,
      341                         scaleX: 1, scaleY: 1, angle: 0,
      342                         objects: [],
      343                     }
      344                 ],
      345                 version: '6.0.0',
      346             };
      347             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.set(1, fakePage1Json);
      348         });
      349         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      350
      351         // Refresh the Measures tab
      352         await openMeasuresTab(page);
      353
      354         // 4.1 — Distance count is now 2 (1 from page 0 + 1 from page 1)
      355         const distCount2 = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      356         assert(distCount2 === '2', 'Distance count = 2 after adding page 1 measurement');
      357
      358         // 4.2 — Total rows increase to 6 (5 from page 0 + 1 from page 1)
      359         const totalRows2 = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els => els.length);
      360         assert(totalRows2 === 6, `Total rows = 6 after page 1 measurement added, got ${totalRows2}`);
      361
      362         // 4.3 — Page labels show p.1 and p.2
      363         const pageLabels = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row-page', els =>
      364             els.map(el => el.textContent.trim())
      365         );
      366         assert(pageLabels.includes('p.1'), 'p.1 page label present for page-0 measurements');
      367         assert(pageLabels.includes('p.2'), 'p.2 page label present for page-1 measurement');
      368
      369         // 4.4 — Distance total sums both pages (raw pixel values summed)
      370         //        Stat total should not be '—' and should differ from single-page total
      371         const distTotalMulti = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-total', el => el.textContent.trim());
      372         assert(distTotalMulti !== '—', 'Distance total non-dash with multi-page data');
      373
      374         // 4.5 — refresh() updates correctly after adding more measurements
      375         await page.evaluate(() => {
      376             // Add one more synthetic distance on page 2
      377             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.set(2, {
      378                 objects: [{
      379                     type: 'Group',
      380                     measurementType: 'distance',
      381                     pixelLength: 150,
      382                     labelText: '150.0 px',
      383                     markupAuthor: 'TestUser',
      384                     markupTimestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      385                     left: 50, top: 50, width: 150, height: 20,
      386                     scaleX: 1, scaleY: 1, angle: 0,
      387                     objects: [],
      388                 }],
      389                 version: '6.0.0',
      390             });
      391             window.app.measureSummary.refresh();
      392         });
      393         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      394
      395         const distCount3 = await page.$eval('#stat-distance-count', el => el.textContent.trim());
      396         assert(distCount3 === '3', 'Distance count = 3 after adding page-2 measurement via refresh()');
      397
      398         // =====================================================================
      399         // TEST GROUP 5: Type filter
      400         // =====================================================================
      401         console.log('\n--- Test Group 5: Type filter ---');
      402
      403         // Make sure Measures tab is open with all data
      404         await openMeasuresTab(page);
      405
      406         // 5.1 — Filter "Distance" shows only distance rows
      407         await page.selectOption('#measure-type-filter', 'distance');
      408         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      409         const distOnlyRows = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els =>
      410             els.map(r => r.querySelector('.markup-type-badge')?.textContent?.trim())
      411         );
      412         const allDist = distOnlyRows.every(b => b === 'Dist');
      413         assert(allDist && distOnlyRows.length > 0, `Filter "Distance" shows only Dist rows (${distOnlyR
          ows.length} rows)`);
      414
      415         // 5.2 — Filter "Area" shows only area rows
      416         await page.selectOption('#measure-type-filter', 'area');
      417         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      418         const areaOnlyRows = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els =>
      419             els.map(r => r.querySelector('.markup-type-badge')?.textContent?.trim())
      420         );
      421         const allArea = areaOnlyRows.every(b => b === 'Area');
      422         assert(allArea && areaOnlyRows.length > 0, `Filter "Area" shows only Area rows (${areaOnlyRows.
          length} rows)`);
      423
      424         // 5.3 — Filter "Count" shows only count rows
      425         await page.selectOption('#measure-type-filter', 'count');
      426         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      427         const countOnlyRows = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els =>
      428             els.map(r => r.querySelector('.markup-type-badge')?.textContent?.trim())
      429         );
      430         const allCount = countOnlyRows.every(b => b === 'Count');
      431         assert(allCount && countOnlyRows.length > 0, `Filter "Count" shows only Count rows (${countOnly
          Rows.length} rows)`);
      432
      433         // 5.4 — Filter "All" restores all rows
      434         await page.selectOption('#measure-type-filter', 'all');
      435         await page.waitForTimeout(150);
      436         const allRows = await page.$$eval('#measure-list .markup-row', els => els.length);
      437         assert(allRows > 3, `Filter "All" restores all rows (got ${allRows})`);
      438
      439         // =====================================================================
      440         // TEST GROUP 6: CSV export
      441         // =====================================================================
      442         console.log('\n--- Test Group 6: CSV export ---');
      443
      444         // 6.1 — Export CSV button exists
      445         const exportBtn = await page.$('#btn-export-csv');
      446         assert(exportBtn !== null, 'Export CSV button exists');
      447
      448         // 6.2-6.5 — Capture CSV content via JS-level Blob interception
      449         const csvResult = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      450             // Capture the generated CSV by intercepting URL.createObjectURL
      451             let capturedCsv = null;
      452             const origCreate = URL.createObjectURL.bind(URL);
      453             let blobPromise = null;
      454
      455             URL.createObjectURL = (blob) => {
      456                 blobPromise = blob.text().then(t => { capturedCsv = t; });
      457                 return 'blob:test-url';
      458             };
      459
      460             // Patch appendChild/removeChild to avoid JSDOM issues with synthetic <a> clicks
      461             const origAppend = document.body.appendChild.bind(document.body);
      462             const origRemove = document.body.removeChild.bind(document.body);
      463             const clicks = [];
      464             document.body.appendChild = (el) => {
      465                 if (el.tagName === 'A') { clicks.push(el); el.click = () => {}; return el; }
      466                 return origAppend(el);
      467             };
      468             document.body.removeChild = (el) => {
      469                 if (el.tagName === 'A') return el;
      470                 return origRemove(el);
      471             };
      472
      473             window.app.measureSummary._exportCSV();
      474             if (blobPromise) await blobPromise;
      475             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
      476
      477             URL.createObjectURL = origCreate;
      478             document.body.appendChild = origAppend;
      479             document.body.removeChild = origRemove;
      480
      481             return capturedCsv;
      482         });
      483
      484         // 6.2 — CSV starts with correct header line
      485         assert(
      486             typeof csvResult === 'string' &&
      487             csvResult.startsWith('Type,Value,Raw (px),Page,Author,Timestamp'),
      488             'CSV has correct header row'
      489         );
      490
      491         // 6.3 — CSV row count matches unfiltered entry count
      492         if (csvResult) {
      493             const lines = csvResult.split('\r\n').filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);
      494             const dataLines = lines.length - 1;  // subtract header
      495             const entryCount = await page.evaluate(() => window.app.measureSummary._entries.length);
      496             assert(dataLines === entryCount, `CSV has ${dataLines} data rows matching ${entryCount} ent
          ries`);
      497
      498             // 6.4 — CSV includes page labels
      499             assert(csvResult.includes('p.1'), 'CSV includes p.1 page label');
      500             assert(csvResult.includes('p.2') || csvResult.includes('p.3'),
      501                 'CSV includes p.2 or p.3 page label for multi-page entries');
      502
      503             // 6.5 — CSV formatted values match panel display
      504             const firstRow = await page.evaluate(() => {
      505                 const e = window.app.measureSummary._entries[0];
      506                 return e ? e.formattedValue : null;
      507             });
      508             if (firstRow) {
      509                 // The formatted value should appear in the CSV (possibly quoted)
      510                 assert(
      511                     csvResult.includes(firstRow.replace(/"/g, '""')),
      512                     'CSV formatted value matches panel display value'
      513                 );
      514             } else {
      515                 assert(true, 'CSV formatted value check skipped (no entries)');
      516             }
      517         } else {
      518             // CSV capture failed — mark these as failed
      519             assert(false, 'CSV row count check (CSV capture failed)');
      520             assert(false, 'CSV page labels check (CSV capture failed)');
      521             assert(false, 'CSV formatted values match (CSV capture failed)');
      522         }
      523
      524     } finally {
      525         await browser.close();
      526     }
      527
      528     // ==========================================================================
      529     // RESULTS SUMMARY
      530     // ==========================================================================
      531     console.log('\n');
      532     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      533     console.log('  Phase 4A: Measurement Summary Panel');
      534     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      535     console.log(`  Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed, ${passed + failed} total`);
      536     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      537
      538     process.exit(failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
      539 }
      540
      541 run().catch(err => {
      542     console.error('[Phase 4A Test] Fatal error:', err);
      543     process.exit(1);
      544 });

● Step 7: Add test_phase4a.mjs to run_tests.mjs

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The test ended up with 34 tests (not 30) because the type badge assertions were split into three individual assertions
   (Dist/Area/Count) rather than one combined check. The Playwright Blob-interception technique used in Groups 2 and 6 —
   patching URL.createObjectURL to capture the CSV text before it becomes an opaque blob URL — is a useful pattern
  whenever you need to assert on client-side file downloads without actually writing to disk.

∴ Thinking…

  Now I have a very clear picture of both the plugin stub and ExtendedCognition v1. Let me think about what Phase 4B
  should actually be.

  Key findings:

  1. Plugin stub: Completely empty. Just a PluginLoader class with an empty Map and a no-op loadPlugins().
  2. ExtendedCognition v1: It's a full standalone FastAPI app that:
    - Has its own SQLite database (different from OpenRevu's)
    - Has its own HTML pages/templates
    - Runs on port 8000 (same as OpenRevu!)
    - Features a complex SVG canvas UI for floor plan equipment placement with 39 API endpoints and a completely
  separate UI paradigm from OpenRevu

  The Integration Challenge:

  This isn't just embedding EC as a simple panel plugin—it's a whole separate application with its own pages, routes,
  database, and UI design. I need to decide between running it on a different port and embedding it as an iframe,
  merging its routes into OpenRevu's FastAPI app to share the same server, or doing a full port of the functionality.

  For Phase 4B, I think the right move is to build a proper plugin loader API first that lets plugins register
  themselves, add tabs to the right panel, and respond to canvas events. Then I can port ExtendedCognition as a
  companion app running on a separate port—either showing an iframe of the EC floor canvas for the current building in a
   right-panel tab, or displaying a more integrated view of the data. lightweight EC widget (equipment search/quick-add)

  Actually, let me reconsider the scope. Phase 4B shouldn't try to fully port EC. The right approach for a plugin system
   is a real Plugin Loader API with registration, panel tab injection, and event hooks forwarding canvas events to
  plugins, then ExtendedCognition becomes the first plugin implementation.

  Looking back at the original plan, EC should get its own tab in the right panel (similar to the left panel's
  Pages/Markups/Measures tabs), where the plugin injects a lightweight Building/Floor Navigator UI with a link to open
  the full EC canvas. Alternatively, I could merge EC's routes into OpenRevu's FastAPI server under a /ec/ prefix and
  show an iframe in the right panel tab pointing to the relevant floor page.

  Thinking about the actual user workflow—a facilities or HVAC person with a PDF open in OpenRevu would want to see
  which building/floor the drawing corresponds to, then jump to the EC floor canvas to view equipment placement, which
  suggests the panel tab approach with an iframe might be more practical than trying to embed the full EC interface. add
   markup context to EC from OpenRevu selections

  So the RIGHT integration for Phase 4B is probably:

  1. Build real Plugin Loader with:
    - Plugin manifest (name, version, init(app) function)
    - Right panel tab injection
    - App event hooks
  2. EC Plugin — a lightweight "EC Bridge" plugin that:
    - Adds a "Buildings" tab to the right panel
    - Shows a simple building/floor search/nav UI
    - Has a button to open the current floor in EC (in a new tab or iframe)
    - This keeps the scope manageable and doesn't require rewriting EC's entire canvas
  3. EC itself stays as a separate app (run on port 8001), accessible from the plugin panel

  This is the right decomposition. The 200px right panel is too narrow for a full EC canvas, so instead I should add a
  tab system to the right panel alongside the existing Properties tab. The EC tab would have building and floor
  selectors with a button to launch the full floor plan in a new tab or modal.

  Defining the plugin manifest structure... I'm realizing the user's question is pretty open-ended, so I need to figure
  out what matters most before laying out a plan. The architecture hinges on a few key decisions—whether EC should run
  separately or merge into OpenRevu's server, and how tightly integrated the plugin should be—but rather than ask for
  all of that, I'll focus on the one decision that shapes everything else: should EC live on its own port or get folded
  into OpenRevu's FastAPI server?

∴ Thinking…

  The user wants:
  1. Plugin API only (no EC integration yet)
  2. Generalized API first

  So Phase 4B is purely about building the Plugin API:
  - PluginLoader gets real registration, tab injection, and event emission
  - No EC integration yet (that's Phase 4C)
  - The right panel gets a tab system so plugins can inject tabs
  - Canvas events get forwarded to registered plugins

  Let me now design the full plan. Let me think through the architecture:

  Plugin API Design:

  // Plugin manifest shape
  {
    name: 'my-plugin',           // unique identifier
    label: 'My Plugin',          // display name for tab
    version: '1.0.0',
    init(container, app) {},     // called once, container is the panel tab DOM element
    // Optional lifecycle hooks
    onDocumentLoaded(info) {},   // called when a PDF is opened
    onPageChanged(page) {},      // called when page changes
    onObjectSelected(obj) {},    // called when Fabric object selected
    onObjectDeselected() {},     // called when selection cleared
  }

  Defining the plugin manifest structure with lifecycle hooks...

  PluginLoader responsibilities:
  1. register(manifest) — validate + store plugin, inject tab into right panel
  2. loadPlugins() — currently a stub, now needs to actually load and initialize registered plugins
  3. Event forwarding — app.js calls plugins.emit('event', data) at the right places
  4. Right panel tab system — right panel currently has a single .panel-header "Properties" div, needs to become a tab
  system

  Right panel tab system:
  Currently: #panel-properties has .panel-header "Properties" and .panel-body (id=properties-content)
  After: #panel-properties has .panel-tabs (Properties tab + plugin tabs) and .tab-content panels

  This is the same tab system the left panel uses (Pages/Markups/Measures). Same CSS classes.

  Event system:
  - document-loaded → fires in app._onDocumentLoaded(info)
  - page-changed → fires in app.viewer.onPageChange
  - object-selected → fires when a Fabric object is selected

  I need to wire up the right panel to use a tab system like the left panel, then make sure the PluginLoader properly
  registers plugins, injects their tabs, and forwards the relevant events from the app to each plugin's lifecycle hooks.
   (already has .panel-tabs + .panel-tab styles from left panel — might just work with minor additions)
  4. static/js/app.js — add plugins.emit() calls at the right lifecycle points
  5. test_phase4b.mjs — ~25 tests
  6. run_tests.mjs — add test_phase4b.mjs

  For the tab system, I need to restructure the right panel to add a tab header above the properties content, then
  inject plugin tabs alongside the default Properties tab. The HTML will get a .panel-tabs container with individual tab
   buttons, and each tab will toggle its corresponding .tab-content div.

  Now I'm realizing the left panel's tab switching logic in markup-list.js handles the click events and visibility
  toggling, so I'll need to extend that same pattern to the right panel.

  For the right panel tabs, I can either add the tab switching logic directly in plugins.js when each plugin tab gets
  injected, or use event delegation on the #right-panel-tabs container to handle clicks dynamically. The simpler
  approach is to wire up the click handler and content visibility each time a tab button is added.

  One thing to watch out for: the PropertiesPanel class currently listens to the .panel-header "Properties" button for
  selection events, so I need to make sure that still works when the Properties tab is now part of the tabbed interface.

  Looking at how the properties panel actually works, it shows and hides individual content sections like #markup-props
  and #doc-info by toggling their display styles directly, rather than hiding the entire panel container. So the
  Properties panel code itself doesn't need any changes — just the tab switching mechanism needs to handle it properly.

  For the event emission system, I'm setting up a method that iterates through registered plugins and calls their
  corresponding hook handlers when events fire, with error handling to catch any plugin that throws an exception.

  Now I need to figure out where to hook these emit calls into the app lifecycle — document load, page changes, object
  selection events, and deselection. Let me trace through the app.js flow to identify the exact spots.

  The tricky part is that the canvas gets recreated each time a document loads, so I'll need to rebind the selection
  event listeners in _onDocumentLoaded() right after canvas initialization, similar to how measureTools sets itself up.
  Then I can outline the test plan for plugin registration and initialization.

  For the test groups, I'm covering plugin registration basics like adding to the map and injecting tabs, the right
  panel tab switching behavior, the event emission system for document and selection changes, and making sure errors in
  one plugin don't break others.

  Now I'm thinking through the testing approach itself. Using page.evaluate() to register test plugins directly in
  Playwright is cleaner than needing actual plugin files, and I can verify DOM changes and event callbacks through
  JavaScript evaluation rather than relying on canvas rendering like the previous phases required.

  For the actual test cases, I'm breaking them into groups covering the plugin registration API, the right panel tab
  system, and event handling. The first group checks that register() properly adds plugins to the Map, creates tab
  buttons and content divs, calls init() with the right arguments, and handles edge cases like missing names. The second
   group verifies the Properties tab exists and is active by default, then tests switching between plugin tabs and the
  Properties tab. The third group is checking that various document events like document-loaded, page-changed, and
  object-selected properly trigger their corresponding plugin callbacks.

  The fourth group ensures the app exposes its plugin system through window.app.plugins, and that user actions like
  loading documents, navigating pages, and selecting objects all emit the appropriate events to registered plugins. I'm
  planning to implement the full PluginLoader class first in static/js/plugins.js, then wire it into the app.

  js lifecycle and canvas selection handlers, update the HTML template to support tabbed properties, and finally write
  out the test suite to verify all 25 new tests pass alongside the existing 387.

● Good — Plugin API only, generalized first. Let me now design the architecture and write the plan.

│ Plan to implement                                                                                                    │

│ OpenRevu Phase 4B — Plugin Loader API                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
