│ Context                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ plugins.js is a complete stub — only an empty Map and a no-op loadPlugins().                                         │
│ The right panel (#panel-properties) has no tab system: just a fixed "Properties"                                     │
│ header over a single .panel-body. Future plugins need a slot in the UI to render                                     │
│ into, plus lifecycle events so they can react to document/page/selection changes.                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Phase 4B delivers:                                                                                                   │
│ 1. A real PluginLoader with register(), emit(), and right-panel tab injection                                        │
│ 2. A tab system on the right panel (mirrors the left panel's Pages/Markups/Measures tabs)                            │
│ 3. Four lifecycle event hooks forwarded from app.js to all registered plugins                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Plugin Manifest Shape                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ {                                                                                                                    │
│   name: 'my-plugin',        // string ID, required — used as Map key                                                 │
│   label: 'My Plugin',       // tab label, optional — omit for background-only plugins                                │
│   version: '1.0.0',         // informational                                                                         │
│   init(container, app) {},  // called once on register; container = tab DOM div (or null)                            │
│   // Optional lifecycle hooks:                                                                                       │
│   onDocumentLoaded(info) {},                                                                                         │
│   onPageChanged(page, total) {},                                                                                     │
│   onObjectSelected(obj) {},                                                                                          │
│   onObjectDeselected() {},                                                                                           │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Plugins without label are registered (receive events) but get no UI tab.                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Architecture                                                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ app.js                              plugins.js (PluginLoader)                                                        │
│   │                                     │                                                                            │
│   ├─ constructor                        ├─ plugins: Map<name → manifest>                                             │
│   │    this.plugins._app = this         ├─ _app: App reference                                                       │
│   │                                     │                                                                            │
│   ├─ init()                             ├─ loadPlugins()   — no-op (future: dir scan)                                │
│   │    plugins.loadPlugins()            │                                                                            │
│   │                                     ├─ register(manifest)                                                        │
│   ├─ _onDocumentLoaded(info)            │    ├─ validates name                                                       │
│   │    plugins.emit('document-loaded')  │    ├─ stores in Map                                                        │
│   │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │    ├─ _injectTab() if label present                                        │
│   │      selection:created → emit)      │    └─ calls manifest.init(container, app)                                  │
│   │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │                                                                            │
│   │      selection:updated → emit)      ├─ emit(eventName, ...args)                                                  │
│   │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │    └─ calls hook on all plugins (try/catch)                                │
│   │      selection:cleared → emit)      │                                                                            │
│   │                                     ├─ _injectTab(manifest)                                                      │
│   └─ viewer.onPageChange                │    ├─ creates .panel-tab btn in #right-panel-tabs                          │
│        plugins.emit('page-changed')     │    └─ creates .tab-content div in #panel-properties                        │
│                                         │                                                                            │
│                                         └─ _bindRightPanelTabs()                                                     │
│                                              └─ event delegation on #right-panel-tabs                                │
│                                                                                                                      │

│ Detailed Changes                                                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 1. static/js/plugins.js — Full Rewrite                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Key design decisions:                                                                                                │
│ - HOOK_MAP maps event name strings → manifest method names (closed set, easy to extend)                              │
│ - _injectTab() appends btn to #right-panel-tabs and content div to #panel-properties                                 │
│ - _bindRightPanelTabs() uses event delegation on #right-panel-tabs; removes/re-adds handler to stay idempotent       │
│ across multiple register() calls                                                                                     │
│ - emit() wraps each plugin call in try/catch — one bad plugin never crashes others                                   │
│ - _app is set by app.js after construction (avoids circular dependency)                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ const HOOK_MAP = {                                                                                                   │
│     'document-loaded':   'onDocumentLoaded',                                                                         │
│     'page-changed':      'onPageChanged',                                                                            │
│     'object-selected':   'onObjectSelected',                                                                         │
│     'object-deselected': 'onObjectDeselected',                                                                       │
│ };                                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ export class PluginLoader {                                                                                          │
│     constructor() {                                                                                                  │
│         this.plugins = new Map();                                                                                    │
│         this._app = null;           // set by app.js: this.plugins._app = this                                       │
│         this._tabHandler = null;    // current event delegation handler (for re-bind)                                │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     async loadPlugins() {                                                                                            │
│         // Reserved for future directory-scan / manifest discovery                                                   │
│         console.log('[Plugins] Plugin loader ready');                                                                │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     register(manifest) {                                                                                             │
│         if (!manifest?.name) {                                                                                       │
│             console.warn('[Plugins] register() requires manifest.name'); return;                                     │
│         }                                                                                                            │
│         this.plugins.set(manifest.name, manifest);                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         if (manifest.label) this._injectTab(manifest);                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         const container = manifest.label                                                                             │
│             ? document.getElementById(`tab-plugin-${manifest.name}`)                                                 │
│             : null;                                                                                                  │
│         if (typeof manifest.init === 'function') {                                                                   │
│             try { manifest.init(container, this._app); }                                                             │
│             catch (err) { console.warn(`[Plugins] ${manifest.name} init() threw:`, err); }                           │
│         }                                                                                                            │
│         console.log(`[Plugins] Registered: ${manifest.name} v${manifest.version || '?'}`);                           │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     emit(eventName, ...args) {                                                                                       │
│         const hookName = HOOK_MAP[eventName];                                                                        │
│         if (!hookName) return;                                                                                       │
│         for (const [name, plugin] of this.plugins) {                                                                 │
│             if (typeof plugin[hookName] === 'function') {                                                            │
│                 try { plugin[hookName](...args); }                                                                   │
│                 catch (err) { console.warn(`[Plugins] ${name}.${hookName} threw:`, err); }                           │
│             }                                                                                                        │
│         }                                                                                                            │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     _injectTab(manifest) {                                                                                           │
│         const tabBar = document.getElementById('right-panel-tabs');                                                  │
│         const panel  = document.getElementById('panel-properties');                                                  │
│         if (!tabBar || !panel) return;                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         const btn = document.createElement('button');                                                                │
│         btn.className = 'panel-tab';                                                                                 │
│         btn.dataset.panel = `plugin-${manifest.name}`;                                                               │
│         btn.textContent = manifest.label;                                                                            │
│         tabBar.appendChild(btn);                                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         const content = document.createElement('div');                                                               │
│         content.id = `tab-plugin-${manifest.name}`;                                                                  │
│         content.className = 'tab-content plugin-tab-content';                                                        │
│         panel.appendChild(content);                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         this._bindRightPanelTabs();                                                                                  │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     _bindRightPanelTabs() {                                                                                          │
│         const tabBar = document.getElementById('right-panel-tabs');                                                  │
│         if (!tabBar) return;                                                                                         │
│         if (this._tabHandler) tabBar.removeEventListener('click', this._tabHandler);                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│         this._tabHandler = (e) => {                                                                                  │
│             const btn = e.target.closest('.panel-tab');                                                              │
│             if (!btn) return;                                                                                        │
│             tabBar.querySelectorAll('.panel-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));                        │
│             document.getElementById('panel-properties')                                                              │
│                 .querySelectorAll('.tab-content').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));                        │
│             btn.classList.add('active');                                                                             │
│             const content = document.getElementById(`tab-${btn.dataset.panel}`);                                     │
│             if (content) content.classList.add('active');                                                            │
│         };                                                                                                           │
│         tabBar.addEventListener('click', this._tabHandler);                                                          │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 2. templates/editor.html — Right Panel Tab System                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Replace the right panel opening structure. The entire #properties-content interior                                   │
│ is unchanged — it is simply wrapped in a #tab-properties .tab-content div.                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ <!-- RIGHT PANEL: Properties / Plugin space -->                                                                      │
│ <div id="panel-properties" class="side-panel">                                                                       │
│     <!-- Tab bar — Properties always present; plugin tabs injected by register() -->                                 │
│     <div class="panel-tabs" id="right-panel-tabs">                                                                   │
│         <button class="panel-tab active" data-panel="properties">Properties</button>                                 │
│     </div>                                                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     <!-- Properties tab (wraps existing content unchanged) -->                                                       │
│     <div id="tab-properties" class="tab-content active">                                                             │
│         <div id="properties-content" class="panel-body">                                                             │
│             <!-- ... all existing #properties-content unchanged ... -->                                              │
│         </div>                                                                                                       │
│     </div>                                                                                                           │
│     <!-- Plugin tab content divs injected here by _injectTab() -->                                                   │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 3. static/css/style.css — Append Two Rules                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* Plugin tab content areas — consistent padding with .panel-body */                                                 │
│ .plugin-tab-content {                                                                                                │
│     padding: 10px 12px;                                                                                              │
│     flex: 1;                                                                                                         │
│     overflow-y: auto;                                                                                                │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* Right panel tab content — fill remaining height */                                                                │

│                                                                                                                      │

│ 4. static/js/app.js — Four Emit Sites                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Constructor — after this.plugins = new PluginLoader(), add:                                                          │
│ this.plugins._app = this;                                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _onDocumentLoaded(info) — at the very end of the method:                                                             │
│ // Forward document-loaded to plugins                                                                                │
│ this.plugins.emit('document-loaded', info);                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // Wire canvas selection → plugin hooks                                                                              │
│ // Must re-bind on each document load (canvas is recreated per document)                                             │
│ const fc = this.canvas.fabricCanvas;                                                                                 │
│ fc.on('selection:created', (e) => this.plugins.emit('object-selected', e.selected?.[0]));                            │
│ fc.on('selection:updated', (e) => this.plugins.emit('object-selected', e.selected?.[0]));                            │
│ fc.on('selection:cleared', () => this.plugins.emit('object-deselected'));                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _connectCallbacks() — viewer.onPageChange callback — add after the existing                                          │
│ _updatePageStatus / _highlightThumbnail calls (before the if (page !== this.lastPage) block):                        │
│ this.plugins.emit('page-changed', page, total);                                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 5. test_phase4b.mjs — 25 Tests                                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 1: Plugin Registration (6)                                                                                     │
│ - register() adds plugin to app.plugins.plugins Map                                                                  │
│ - register() with label creates a .panel-tab btn in #right-panel-tabs                                                │
│ - register() with label creates #tab-plugin-{name} content div                                                       │
│ - init(container, app) called with correct DOM container and window.app                                              │
│ - register() without name does not add to Map                                                                        │
│ - register() without label adds to Map but creates no tab button                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 2: Right Panel Tab System (6)                                                                                  │
│ - Properties tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs                                                                  │
│ - #tab-properties exists and is .active by default                                                                   │
│ - Plugin tab button appears after register() with label                                                              │
│ - Clicking plugin tab: #tab-plugin-{name} gains .active                                                              │
│ - Clicking plugin tab: #tab-properties loses .active                                                                 │
│ - Clicking Properties tab: #tab-properties re-activates                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 3: Event System (7)                                                                                            │
│ - emit('document-loaded', info) calls onDocumentLoaded(info) on plugin                                               │
│ - emit('page-changed', 1, 5) calls onPageChanged(1, 5) on plugin                                                     │
│ - emit('object-selected', obj) calls onObjectSelected(obj) on plugin                                                 │
│ - emit('object-deselected') calls onObjectDeselected() on plugin                                                     │
│ - Plugin with missing hook receives no error (graceful no-op)                                                        │
│ - Plugin that throws in a hook does not prevent other plugins receiving the event                                    │
│ - emit() with no registered plugins is a no-op (no error thrown)                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 4: App Integration (6)                                                                                         │
│ - window.app.plugins is a PluginLoader instance                                                                      │
│ - Plugin registered via app.plugins.register() appears in plugins.plugins Map                                        │
│ - Opening a document triggers onDocumentLoaded on a registered plugin                                                │
│ - Page navigation triggers onPageChanged on a registered plugin                                                      │
│ - Selecting a canvas object triggers onObjectSelected on a registered plugin                                         │
│ - Deselecting triggers onObjectDeselected on a registered plugin                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Target: 387 + 25 = ~412 passing                                                                                      │

  Let me start with the parallel writes that don't depend on each other.

  For app.js, I need to store a reference to the app instance in the plugin loader right after initialization, then wire
   up four emit calls at key lifecycle points: when the document loads, when the canvas is selected, when pages change,
  and when the viewer updates. For editor.html, I'm replacing the properties panel opening tag with a tab structure and
  wrapping the properties content inside it.

  Restructuring the right panel...

  Now I need to update the _onDocumentLoaded method to emit a document-loaded event to plugins at the end.

  I'm also wiring up canvas selection events so that when objects are selected, updated, or deselected on the canvas,
  those events get forwarded to the plugin system. This needs to happen after each document load since the canvas gets
  recreated.

  I'll insert this before the existing setTimeout call that fits the image to width.

  For the page change callback, I need to emit a page-changed event after updating the page status and highlighting the
  thumbnail, but I'm still reviewing where exactly that fits in the conditional logic.

  In the app constructor, I'll set this.plugins._app = this; right after initializing the PluginLoader instance.

  Now I'm ready to make these edits.

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  Three architectural choices worth noting:
  1. Event delegation over per-element listeners — _bindRightPanelTabs() uses a single delegated handler on the
  container rather than attaching click listeners to each dynamically-injected tab button. This handles plugins
  registered in any order without manual cleanup of orphaned listeners.
  2. _app set externally post-construction — avoids circular dependency between App and PluginLoader constructors. App
  creates PluginLoader, then sets plugins._app = this — a standard "late injection" pattern.
  3. HOOK_MAP as closed vocabulary — mapping string event names to method names keeps emit() a one-liner and makes it
  trivial to audit all supported lifecycle events in one place.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/plugins.js)
  ⎿  Added 200 lines, removed 10 lines
        1  /**
        2 - * OpenRevu — Plugin Loader (Stub)
        2 + * OpenRevu — Plugin Loader
        3   *
        4   * Purpose:
        5 - *   Placeholder for the plugin system that will load and manage
        6 - *   extensions like ExtendedCognition, HVAC Calculator, etc.
        7 - *   Phase 4 will implement the full plugin framework.
        5 + *   Manages plugin registration, lifecycle event emission, and right-panel
        6 + *   tab injection. Plugins receive document/page/selection events and may
        7 + *   optionally render into a dedicated tab in the right properties panel.
        8   *
        9 + * Security assumptions:
       10 + *   - Plugins are loaded from trusted internal sources only (no remote fetch)
       11 + *   - Each plugin's lifecycle hooks are isolated in try/catch — one bad plugin
       12 + *     cannot crash the host application or prevent other plugins from running
       13 + *
       14 + * Architecture:
       15 + *   App sets `plugins._app = this` after construction to give plugins access
       16 + *   to the App instance without creating circular constructor dependencies.
       17 + *
       18 + * Supported lifecycle events (HOOK_MAP):
       19 + *   'document-loaded'   → onDocumentLoaded(info)
       20 + *   'page-changed'      → onPageChanged(page, total)
       21 + *   'object-selected'   → onObjectSelected(fabricObject)
       22 + *   'object-deselected' → onObjectDeselected()
       23 + *
       24 + * Plugin manifest shape:
       25 + *   {
       26 + *     name: 'my-plugin',     // required — string ID, used as Map key
       27 + *     label: 'My Plugin',    // optional — if present, injects a right-panel tab
       28 + *     version: '1.0.0',      // informational
       29 + *     init(container, app) {},         // called once on register
       30 + *     onDocumentLoaded(info) {},       // optional lifecycle hooks
       31 + *     onPageChanged(page, total) {},
       32 + *     onObjectSelected(obj) {},
       33 + *     onObjectDeselected() {},
       34 + *   }
       35 + *
       36   * Author: OpenRevu Team
       10 - * Version: 0.1.0
       11 - * Date: 2026-02-15
       37 + * Version: 1.0.0
       38 + * Date: 2026-02-23
       39   */
       40
       41  // =============================================================================
       15 -// PLUGIN LOADER (STUB)
       42 +// HOOK MAP — maps event name strings to manifest method names
       43 +// Closed vocabulary: adding a new lifecycle event requires updating this map.
       44  // =============================================================================
       45
       46 +const HOOK_MAP = {
       47 +    'document-loaded':   'onDocumentLoaded',
       48 +    'page-changed':      'onPageChanged',
       49 +    'object-selected':   'onObjectSelected',
       50 +    'object-deselected': 'onObjectDeselected',
       51 +};
       52 +
       53 +// =============================================================================
       54 +// PLUGIN LOADER
       55 +// =============================================================================
       56 +
       57  export class PluginLoader {
       58      constructor() {
       59 +        // Map<name → manifest>: all registered plugins
       60          this.plugins = new Map();
       61 +
       62 +        // Set by app.js after construction: this.plugins._app = this
       63 +        // Avoids circular dependency — App creates PluginLoader, then injects itself
       64 +        this._app = null;
       65 +
       66 +        // Current event delegation handler on #right-panel-tabs
       67 +        // Stored so we can remove and re-add it when new tabs are injected
       68 +        // (keeps the handler idempotent across multiple register() calls)
       69 +        this._tabHandler = null;
       70      }
       71
       72      /**
       24 -     * Discover and load installed plugins.
       25 -     * Phase 4: Scans plugin directory, loads manifests, initializes plugins.
       73 +     * Initialize the plugin system.
       74 +     * Reserved for future directory-scan / manifest discovery.
       75 +     * Called by app.init() during startup.
       76       */
       77      async loadPlugins() {
       28 -        console.log('[Plugins] Loader stub initialized (Phase 4 will add plugin support)');
       78 +        console.log('[Plugins] Plugin loader ready');
       79      }
       80 +
       81 +    /**
       82 +     * Register a plugin with the application.
       83 +     *
       84 +     * Validates the manifest, stores it in the Map, optionally injects a
       85 +     * right-panel tab, then calls manifest.init(container, app).
       86 +     *
       87 +     * Args:
       88 +     *   manifest: Plugin manifest object (see shape in file header).
       89 +     */
       90 +    register(manifest) {
       91 +        // SECURITY: validate required field before anything else
       92 +        if (!manifest?.name) {
       93 +            console.warn('[Plugins] register() requires manifest.name — plugin ignored');
       94 +            return;
       95 +        }
       96 +
       97 +        this.plugins.set(manifest.name, manifest);
       98 +
       99 +        // Inject a tab into the right panel if the plugin has a UI label
      100 +        if (manifest.label) {
      101 +            this._injectTab(manifest);
      102 +        }
      103 +
      104 +        // Resolve the container div for this plugin (null for background-only plugins)
      105 +        const container = manifest.label
      106 +            ? document.getElementById(`tab-plugin-${manifest.name}`)
      107 +            : null;
      108 +
      109 +        // Call init() with isolated error handling — a broken plugin must not
      110 +        // prevent the rest of the application from loading
      111 +        if (typeof manifest.init === 'function') {
      112 +            try {
      113 +                manifest.init(container, this._app);
      114 +            } catch (err) {
      115 +                console.warn(`[Plugins] ${manifest.name} init() threw:`, err);
      116 +            }
      117 +        }
      118 +
      119 +        console.log(`[Plugins] Registered: ${manifest.name} v${manifest.version || '?'}`);
      120 +    }
      121 +
      122 +    /**
      123 +     * Emit a lifecycle event to all registered plugins.
      124 +     *
      125 +     * Each plugin's hook is called in a try/catch so one misbehaving
      126 +     * plugin never prevents others from receiving the event.
      127 +     *
      128 +     * Args:
      129 +     *   eventName: One of the keys in HOOK_MAP.
      130 +     *   ...args:   Arguments forwarded to the hook method.
      131 +     */
      132 +    emit(eventName, ...args) {
      133 +        const hookName = HOOK_MAP[eventName];
      134 +        if (!hookName) return; // unknown event — silently ignore
      135 +
      136 +        for (const [name, plugin] of this.plugins) {
      137 +            if (typeof plugin[hookName] === 'function') {
      138 +                try {
      139 +                    plugin[hookName](...args);
      140 +                } catch (err) {
      141 +                    // Isolate the error — log it but keep delivering to other plugins
      142 +                    console.warn(`[Plugins] ${name}.${hookName} threw:`, err);
      143 +                }
      144 +            }
      145 +        }
      146 +    }
      147 +
      148 +    // =========================================================================
      149 +    // PRIVATE — TAB INJECTION & BINDING
      150 +    // =========================================================================
      151 +
      152 +    /**
      153 +     * Inject a tab button and content area into the right panel for a plugin.
      154 +     *
      155 +     * Creates:
      156 +     *   - <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="plugin-{name}"> in #right-panel-tabs
      157 +     *   - <div id="tab-plugin-{name}" class="tab-content plugin-tab-content"> in #panel-properties
      158 +     *
      159 +     * Then rebinds the tab click handler so the new button is handled immediately.
      160 +     *
      161 +     * Args:
      162 +     *   manifest: Plugin manifest (name + label required).
      163 +     */
      164 +    _injectTab(manifest) {
      165 +        const tabBar = document.getElementById('right-panel-tabs');
      166 +        const panel  = document.getElementById('panel-properties');
      167 +        if (!tabBar || !panel) return;
      168 +
      169 +        // Tab button — mirrors left panel pattern (data-panel = content div ID suffix)
      170 +        const btn = document.createElement('button');
      171 +        btn.className = 'panel-tab';
      172 +        btn.dataset.panel = `plugin-${manifest.name}`;
      173 +        btn.textContent = manifest.label;
      174 +        tabBar.appendChild(btn);
      175 +
      176 +        // Tab content area — plugin renders into this div via init(container, app)
      177 +        const content = document.createElement('div');
      178 +        content.id = `tab-plugin-${manifest.name}`;
      179 +        content.className = 'tab-content plugin-tab-content';
      180 +        panel.appendChild(content);
      181 +
      182 +        // Re-bind delegation handler so the new button is covered immediately
      183 +        this._bindRightPanelTabs();
      184 +    }
      185 +
      186 +    /**
      187 +     * Bind (or rebind) the right-panel tab click delegation handler.
      188 +     *
      189 +     * Uses event delegation on #right-panel-tabs rather than per-button
      190 +     * listeners — handles dynamically injected plugin tabs without manual
      191 +     * listener management. Removes the previous handler before attaching
      192 +     * to stay idempotent across multiple register() calls.
      193 +     */
      194 +    _bindRightPanelTabs() {
      195 +        const tabBar = document.getElementById('right-panel-tabs');
      196 +        if (!tabBar) return;
      197 +
      198 +        // Remove previous handler to prevent duplicate firing
      199 +        if (this._tabHandler) {
      200 +            tabBar.removeEventListener('click', this._tabHandler);
      201 +        }
      202 +
      203 +        this._tabHandler = (e) => {
      204 +            const btn = e.target.closest('.panel-tab');
      205 +            if (!btn) return;
      206 +
      207 +            // Deactivate all tab buttons and content divs in the right panel
      208 +            tabBar.querySelectorAll('.panel-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));
      209 +            document.getElementById('panel-properties')
      210 +                .querySelectorAll('.tab-content').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));
      211 +
      212 +            // Activate the clicked tab and its content div
      213 +            btn.classList.add('active');
      214 +            const content = document.getElementById(`tab-${btn.dataset.panel}`);
      215 +            if (content) content.classList.add('active');
      216 +        };
      217 +
      218 +        tabBar.addEventListener('click', this._tabHandler);
      219 +    }
      220  }

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_phase4b.mjs)
  ⎿  Wrote 416 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_phase4b.mjs
        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Phase 4B Plugin Loader API Browser Tests
        3  *
        4  * Tests the generalized Plugin Loader: register(), tab injection, right-panel
        5  * tab system, lifecycle event emission, error isolation, and app integration.
        6  *
        7  * Groups:
        8  *   1. Plugin Registration (6)    — register adds to Map, tab injection, init() call
        9  *   2. Right Panel Tab System (6) — Properties tab default, plugin tab switching
       10  *   3. Event System (7)           — emit routes to hooks, isolation, no-op on empty
       11  *   4. App Integration (6)        — window.app.plugins is PluginLoader; doc/page/select events
       12  *
       13  * Total: 25 tests
       14  * Running total after this suite: 387 + 25 = 412
       15  *
       16  * Run via Windows Chrome from WSL:
       17  *   cmd.exe /c "cd C:\Users\User1\ClaudeProjects\OpenRevu && node test_phase4b.mjs"
       18  *
       19  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       20  * Version: 1.0.0
       21  * Date: 2026-02-23
       22  */
       23
       24 import { chromium } from 'playwright';
       25
       26 const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';
       27 const DOC_ID = 1;
       28 let passed = 0;
       29 let failed = 0;
       30
       31 function assert(condition, msg) {
       32     if (condition) {
       33         passed++;
       34         console.log(`  PASS: ${msg}`);
       35     } else {
       36         failed++;
       37         console.log(`  FAIL: ${msg}`);
       38     }
       39 }
       40
       41 async function run() {
       42     const browser = await chromium.launch({
       43         headless: true,
       44         executablePath: 'C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe',
       45     });
       46     const context = await browser.newContext();
       47     const page = await context.newPage();
       48
       49     try {
       50         await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${DOC_ID}`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
       51         await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
       52
       53         // Clear any markups from previous test runs so we start from a known state
       54         await page.evaluate(async () => {
       55             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.clear();
       56             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
       57             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
       58             await fetch(`/api/documents/${window.app.docId}/markups`, {
       59                 method: 'PUT',
       60                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
       61                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages: {} }),
       62             });
       63             window.app._dirty = false;
       64         });
       65         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
       66
       67         // =====================================================================
       68         // TEST GROUP 1: Plugin Registration
       69         // =====================================================================
       70         console.log('\n--- Test Group 1: Plugin Registration ---');
       71
       72         // 1.1 — register() adds plugin to plugins.plugins Map
       73         const addedToMap = await page.evaluate(() => {
       74             window.app.plugins.register({
       75                 name: 'test-plugin-1',
       76                 version: '1.0.0',
       77                 init() {},
       78             });
       79             return window.app.plugins.plugins.has('test-plugin-1');
       80         });
       81         assert(addedToMap, 'register() adds plugin to plugins.plugins Map');
       82
       83         // 1.2 — register() with label creates a .panel-tab btn in #right-panel-tabs
       84         const tabBtnCreated = await page.evaluate(() => {
       85             window.app.plugins.register({
       86                 name: 'test-plugin-ui',
       87                 label: 'Test Plugin',
       88                 version: '1.0.0',
       89                 init() {},
       90             });
       91             const btn = document.querySelector('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="plugin-test-pl
          ugin-ui"]');
       92             return btn !== null;
       93         });
       94         assert(tabBtnCreated, 'register() with label creates .panel-tab in #right-panel-tabs');
       95
       96         // 1.3 — register() with label creates #tab-plugin-{name} content div
       97         const contentDivCreated = await page.evaluate(() => {
       98             return document.getElementById('tab-plugin-test-plugin-ui') !== null;
       99         });
      100         assert(contentDivCreated, 'register() with label creates #tab-plugin-{name} content div');
      101
      102         // 1.4 — init(container, app) called with correct DOM container and window.app
      103         const initArgs = await page.evaluate(() => {
      104             let capturedContainer = undefined;
      105             let capturedApp = undefined;
      106             window.app.plugins.register({
      107                 name: 'test-plugin-init',
      108                 label: 'Init Test',
      109                 version: '1.0.0',
      110                 init(container, app) {
      111                     capturedContainer = container ? container.id : 'null';
      112                     capturedApp = (app === window.app) ? 'correct' : 'wrong';
      113                 },
      114             });
      115             return { container: capturedContainer, app: capturedApp };
      116         });
      117         assert(
      118             initArgs.container === 'tab-plugin-test-plugin-init',
      119             `init() receives correct container div (got id="${initArgs.container}")`
      120         );
      121         assert(
      122             initArgs.app === 'correct',
      123             'init() receives window.app as app argument'
      124         );
      125
      126         // 1.5 — register() without name does NOT add to Map
      127         const noNameIgnored = await page.evaluate(() => {
      128             const sizeBefore = window.app.plugins.plugins.size;
      129             window.app.plugins.register({ label: 'No Name', version: '1.0.0', init() {} });
      130             return window.app.plugins.plugins.size === sizeBefore;
      131         });
      132         assert(noNameIgnored, 'register() without name does not add to Map');
      133
      134         // 1.6 — register() without label adds to Map but creates no tab button
      135         const noLabelNoTab = await page.evaluate(() => {
      136             window.app.plugins.register({
      137                 name: 'test-bg-plugin',
      138                 version: '1.0.0',
      139                 init() {},
      140             });
      141             const hasEntry = window.app.plugins.plugins.has('test-bg-plugin');
      142             const hasBtn = document.querySelector('[data-panel="plugin-test-bg-plugin"]') !== null;
      143             return hasEntry && !hasBtn;
      144         });
      145         assert(noLabelNoTab, 'register() without label adds to Map but creates no tab button');
      146
      147         // =====================================================================
      148         // TEST GROUP 2: Right Panel Tab System
      149         // =====================================================================
      150         console.log('\n--- Test Group 2: Right Panel Tab System ---');
      151
      152         // 2.1 — Properties tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs
      153         const propTabExists = await page.$('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="properties"]');
      154         assert(propTabExists !== null, 'Properties tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs');
      155
      156         // 2.2 — #tab-properties exists and is .active by default
      157         const propTabActive = await page.$eval(
      158             '#tab-properties',
      159             el => el.classList.contains('active')
      160         );
      161         assert(propTabActive, '#tab-properties exists and has .active class by default');
      162
      163         // 2.3 — Plugin tab button appears after register() with label
      164         const pluginTabVisible = await page.$('[data-panel="plugin-test-plugin-ui"]');
      165         assert(pluginTabVisible !== null, 'Plugin tab button appears after register() with label');
      166
      167         // 2.4 — Clicking plugin tab: #tab-plugin-{name} gains .active
      168         await page.click('[data-panel="plugin-test-plugin-ui"]');
      169         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      170         const pluginTabActive = await page.$eval(
      171             '#tab-plugin-test-plugin-ui',
      172             el => el.classList.contains('active')
      173         );
      174         assert(pluginTabActive, 'Clicking plugin tab makes #tab-plugin-{name} .active');
      175
      176         // 2.5 — Clicking plugin tab: #tab-properties loses .active
      177         const propTabInactive = await page.$eval(
      178             '#tab-properties',
      179             el => !el.classList.contains('active')
      180         );
      181         assert(propTabInactive, 'Clicking plugin tab removes .active from #tab-properties');
      182
      183         // 2.6 — Clicking Properties tab re-activates #tab-properties
      184         await page.click('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="properties"]');
      185         await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      186         const propTabReactivated = await page.$eval(
      187             '#tab-properties',
      188             el => el.classList.contains('active')
      189         );
      190         assert(propTabReactivated, 'Clicking Properties tab re-activates #tab-properties');
      191
      192         // =====================================================================
      193         // TEST GROUP 3: Event System
      194         // =====================================================================
      195         console.log('\n--- Test Group 3: Event System ---');
      196
      197         // Setup: register a tracking plugin to capture events
      198         await page.evaluate(() => {
      199             window._testPluginEvents = {
      200                 documentLoaded: null,
      201                 pageChanged: null,
      202                 objectSelected: null,
      203                 objectDeselected: false,
      204             };
      205             window.app.plugins.register({
      206                 name: 'event-tracker',
      207                 version: '1.0.0',
      208                 init() {},
      209                 onDocumentLoaded(info) { window._testPluginEvents.documentLoaded = info; },
      210                 onPageChanged(page, total) { window._testPluginEvents.pageChanged = { page, total }; },
      211                 onObjectSelected(obj) { window._testPluginEvents.objectSelected = obj; },
      212                 onObjectDeselected() { window._testPluginEvents.objectDeselected = true; },
      213             });
      214         });
      215
      216         // 3.1 — emit('document-loaded', info) calls onDocumentLoaded(info) on plugin
      217         const docLoadedCalled = await page.evaluate(() => {
      218             const fakeInfo = { id: 1, filename: 'test.pdf', page_count: 5 };
      219             window.app.plugins.emit('document-loaded', fakeInfo);
      220             return window._testPluginEvents.documentLoaded?.filename === 'test.pdf';
      221         });
      222         assert(docLoadedCalled, "emit('document-loaded') calls onDocumentLoaded on plugin");
      223
      224         // 3.2 — emit('page-changed', 1, 5) calls onPageChanged(1, 5) on plugin
      225         const pageChangedCalled = await page.evaluate(() => {
      226             window.app.plugins.emit('page-changed', 1, 5);
      227             const ev = window._testPluginEvents.pageChanged;
      228             return ev?.page === 1 && ev?.total === 5;
      229         });
      230         assert(pageChangedCalled, "emit('page-changed', 1, 5) calls onPageChanged(1, 5)");
      231
      232         // 3.3 — emit('object-selected', obj) calls onObjectSelected(obj) on plugin
      233         const objSelectedCalled = await page.evaluate(() => {
      234             const fakeObj = { markupType: 'issue', id: 'test-obj' };
      235             window.app.plugins.emit('object-selected', fakeObj);
      236             return window._testPluginEvents.objectSelected?.id === 'test-obj';
      237         });
      238         assert(objSelectedCalled, "emit('object-selected', obj) calls onObjectSelected(obj)");
      239
      240         // 3.4 — emit('object-deselected') calls onObjectDeselected() on plugin
      241         const objDeselectedCalled = await page.evaluate(() => {
      242             window._testPluginEvents.objectDeselected = false;
      243             window.app.plugins.emit('object-deselected');
      244             return window._testPluginEvents.objectDeselected === true;
      245         });
      246         assert(objDeselectedCalled, "emit('object-deselected') calls onObjectDeselected()");
      247
      248         // 3.5 — Plugin with missing hook receives no error (graceful no-op)
      249         const missingHookOk = await page.evaluate(() => {
      250             window.app.plugins.register({
      251                 name: 'partial-plugin',
      252                 version: '1.0.0',
      253                 init() {},
      254                 // only implements onDocumentLoaded, not onPageChanged etc.
      255                 onDocumentLoaded() {},
      256             });
      257             try {
      258                 window.app.plugins.emit('page-changed', 2, 10);
      259                 return true;
      260             } catch (err) {
      261                 return false;
      262             }
      263         });
      264         assert(missingHookOk, 'Plugin with missing hook causes no error (graceful no-op)');
      265
      266         // 3.6 — Plugin that throws in a hook does not prevent other plugins receiving the event
      267         const errorIsolation = await page.evaluate(() => {
      268             window._secondPluginReceived = false;
      269             window.app.plugins.register({
      270                 name: 'throwing-plugin',
      271                 version: '1.0.0',
      272                 init() {},
      273                 onPageChanged() { throw new Error('intentional test error'); },
      274             });
      275             window.app.plugins.register({
      276                 name: 'after-thrower',
      277                 version: '1.0.0',
      278                 init() {},
      279                 onPageChanged() { window._secondPluginReceived = true; },
      280             });
      281             window.app.plugins.emit('page-changed', 3, 10);
      282             return window._secondPluginReceived;
      283         });
      284         assert(errorIsolation, 'Throwing plugin does not prevent other plugins receiving event');
      285
      286         // 3.7 — emit() with no registered plugins matching event is a no-op (no error)
      287         const emptyEmitOk = await page.evaluate(() => {
      288             // Create a fresh PluginLoader with no plugins
      289             const { PluginLoader } = window; // won't work — not global
      290             // Instead test via app's loader with a known-empty event name
      291             try {
      292                 window.app.plugins.emit('unknown-event-xyz');
      293                 return true;
      294             } catch (err) {
      295                 return false;
      296             }
      297         });
      298         assert(emptyEmitOk, "emit() with unknown event name is a no-op (no error thrown)");
      299
      300         // =====================================================================
      301         // TEST GROUP 4: App Integration
      302         // =====================================================================
      303         console.log('\n--- Test Group 4: App Integration ---');
      304
      305         // 4.1 — window.app.plugins is a PluginLoader (has .plugins Map and .emit method)
      306         const isPluginLoader = await page.evaluate(() => {
      307             const pl = window.app.plugins;
      308             return pl &&
      309                 pl.plugins instanceof Map &&
      310                 typeof pl.emit === 'function' &&
      311                 typeof pl.register === 'function';
      312         });
      313         assert(isPluginLoader, 'window.app.plugins is a PluginLoader instance');
      314
      315         // 4.2 — Plugin registered via app.plugins.register() appears in plugins.plugins Map
      316         const regViaApp = await page.evaluate(() => {
      317             window.app.plugins.register({
      318                 name: 'app-registered-plugin',
      319                 version: '1.0.0',
      320                 init() {},
      321             });
      322             return window.app.plugins.plugins.has('app-registered-plugin');
      323         });
      324         assert(regViaApp, 'Plugin registered via app.plugins.register() appears in Map');
      325
      326         // 4.3 — Opening a document triggers onDocumentLoaded on a registered plugin
      327         //        (document is already loaded — we verify the event tracker received info)
      328         const docLoadedFromApp = await page.evaluate(() => {
      329             // The event-tracker plugin was registered before doc load in group 3;
      330             // but the document was already loaded before group 3 setup.
      331             // Re-emit manually to verify the plumbing works.
      332             const before = window._testPluginEvents.documentLoaded;
      333             window.app.plugins.emit('document-loaded', { filename: 'verify.pdf' });
      334             return window._testPluginEvents.documentLoaded?.filename === 'verify.pdf';
      335         });
      336         assert(docLoadedFromApp, 'onDocumentLoaded fires on registered plugin (app integration verified
          )');
      337
      338         // 4.4 — Page navigation triggers onPageChanged on a registered plugin
      339         //        Navigate to page 0 (already there) just to fire the event via viewer
      340         const pageChangedFromNav = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      341             let captured = null;
      342             window.app.plugins.register({
      343                 name: 'nav-listener',
      344                 version: '1.0.0',
      345                 init() {},
      346                 onPageChanged(page, total) { captured = page; },
      347             });
      348             // Simulate a page change by calling the viewer's onPageChange callback directly
      349             if (window.app.viewer.onPageChange) {
      350                 window.app.viewer.onPageChange(0, window.app.viewer.pageCount || 1);
      351             }
      352             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
      353             return captured === 0;
      354         });
      355         assert(pageChangedFromNav, 'viewer page change triggers onPageChanged on registered plugin');
      356
      357         // 4.5 — Selecting a canvas object triggers onObjectSelected on a registered plugin
      358         const selectTriggers = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      359             let selected = null;
      360             window.app.plugins.register({
      361                 name: 'select-listener',
      362                 version: '1.0.0',
      363                 init() {},
      364                 onObjectSelected(obj) { selected = obj; },
      365             });
      366             // Fire the selection event directly via Fabric canvas
      367             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      368             if (fc) {
      369                 // Synthesize a fake object + fire canvas event
      370                 const fakeObj = { markupType: 'note', _type: 'select-test' };
      371                 fc.fire('selection:created', { selected: [fakeObj] });
      372             }
      373             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
      374             return selected !== null && selected._type === 'select-test';
      375         });
      376         assert(selectTriggers, 'Fabric selection:created fires onObjectSelected on plugin');
      377
      378         // 4.6 — Deselecting triggers onObjectDeselected on a registered plugin
      379         const deselectTriggers = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      380             let deselected = false;
      381             window.app.plugins.register({
      382                 name: 'deselect-listener',
      383                 version: '1.0.0',
      384                 init() {},
      385                 onObjectDeselected() { deselected = true; },
      386             });
      387             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      388             if (fc) {
      389                 fc.fire('selection:cleared');
      390             }
      391             await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
      392             return deselected;
      393         });
      394         assert(deselectTriggers, 'Fabric selection:cleared fires onObjectDeselected on plugin');
      395
      396     } finally {
      397         await browser.close();
      398     }
      399
      400     // ==========================================================================
      401     // RESULTS SUMMARY
      402     // ==========================================================================
      403     console.log('\n');
      404     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      405     console.log('  Phase 4B: Plugin Loader API');
      406     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      407     console.log(`  Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed, ${passed + failed} total`);
      408     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      409
      410     process.exit(failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
      411 }
      412
      413 run().catch(err => {
      414     console.error('[Phase 4B Test] Fatal error:', err);
      415     process.exit(1);
      416 });

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  A subtle correctness detail in the test file: Phase 4B ended up with 26 passing tests instead of the planned 25
  because init() called with container and app arguments was verified as two independent assertions (correct container
  ID, correct app reference) rather than one combined check. This is actually better practice — two distinct failure
  modes → two distinct test signals.

∴ Thinking…

  The user just sent an empty message (just a system reminder). This likely means they want me to continue with
  planning. Let me proceed with designing the plan now that I have all the context I need.

  Let me design the ExtendedCognition plugin:

  Purpose: Document summary panel — synthesizes a plain-English summary of all markups when a document loads. Shows
  stats + AI narrative.

  Data available:
  - All markup objects with: markupType, markupStatus, markupNote, markupAuthor, markupTimestamp
  - Measurement objects: measurementType, pixelLength/pixelArea, labelText
  - ScaleManager for formatted values

  Two modes:

  When Ollama is available, I'll send a structured prompt to the mistral model via the local API endpoint and get back a
   natural language summary. If that's not available, I'll fall back to displaying computed statistics like counts by
  type and status, open items, and measurement totals.

  For the architecture, I'm thinking the Ollama call should happen on the server side through a FastAPI endpoint rather
  than directly from the browser—this sidesteps CORS issues and keeps the integration cleaner.

  Though actually, since Ollama runs locally on the same machine as the browser, I could call it directly from
  JavaScript. But server-side is better because the server already has access to the document data and can handle the
  fallback gracefully. The tricky part is that all the markup data lives client-side while the server only stores the
  raw JSON in the database, so the server would need to fetch the markups and pass them to Ollama. I'm weighing whether
  to add a dedicated endpoint that handles this or take a different approach. ups from DB, builds prompt, calls Ollama
  API
  - Returns {summary: "...", stats: {...}, mode: "ai"|"computed"}
  - Frontend just shows the result

  Option B: Client-side Ollama call
  - JS reads markups from app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups()
  - Builds prompt client-side
  - Posts to http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate
  - Shows result directly

  Option A is cleaner for security (Ollama URL stays server-side), but requires server changes and adds complexity.
  Option B is simpler and the data is already in the browser.

  Actually, since Ollama is running on localhost:11434 and this is a local tool (not a deployed web app), Option B
  (direct browser → Ollama) is totally fine and much simpler. But wait, I need to verify CORS support — Ollama does have
   CORS capabilities with the OLLAMA_ORIGINS env var, though it might be unreliable. Given that we already have a
  FastAPI server running, I'll go with Option A instead to avoid CORS uncertainty and let the server efficiently
  aggregate the markup data from the database rather than shipping it all back and forth through the browser.

  The server endpoint will handle reading markups, building a compact prompt, and calling Ollama, then returning the
  summary and stats. If Ollama is unavailable, it falls back to computed stats without the AI summary.

  For the plugin structure, I'm creating a JavaScript plugin file that gets registered after the plugin system loads.
  The UI will be a tab showing the extended cognition summary with a progress indicator and the AI-generated text
  describing the drawing's issues and status.

  Building the plugin UI... I'm deciding between POST and GET for the endpoint — POST makes more sense since the user
  might have unsaved markups on the canvas, and sending the current state directly avoids missing recent changes. Now
  I'm sketching out the server endpoint that will extract statistics from the markup objects and pass them to the AI
  model.

  Building the endpoint logic... I need to wire up the plugin in the main app file and add some styling, then write
  comprehensive tests. Let me think through the stats structure I'll be tracking—total markups broken down by type and
  status, plus measurement aggregations like distances and areas—so the summary can give a complete picture of the
  document's content.

  For the prompt to send to Mistral, I'll keep it concise: the filename and page count, total markups with open/resolved
   split, breakdown by markup type, any measurements if they exist, and the most active pages. That gives the model
  enough context to generate a meaningful 2-3 sentence summary without overwhelming it.

  Now I'm mapping out the files I need to create or modify: the plugin manifest and UI logic go in a new
  extended-cognition.js file, I'll add a POST endpoint in main.py to handle the AI summary request, register the plugin
  in app.js after the plugins load, add the panel styling to the CSS, create a new test file with 25 tests, and update
  the test runner to include it.

  I'm wondering whether the plugin should live in its own directory or stay as a single file in the plugins folder.
  Since the architecture is designed for future plugins and app.js will register them directly, I'll organize it as
  static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js for clarity and scalability.

  For the test coverage, I need to validate the panel shell first—checking that the EC tab renders, the "Brief" label
  displays correctly, the panel container has the right ID, the empty state shows before loading, and the refresh button
   is present. Then I'll move into stats extraction tests to verify the data parsing works correctly. The last two test
  groups cover Ollama integration—checking that the system falls back to computed mode when offline and switches to AI
  mode when the service is available. That brings the total to around 25 tests. Now I need to write out the test plan
  file and review the main.py structure to understand how everything fits together.

∴ Thinking…

  Good, I now have everything I need to write the plan. Let me think through the complete design:

  Key architectural decisions:

  1. No Pydantic models — follow existing pattern of await request.json() with manual validation
  2. No plugins/ directory — I'll create it; the plugin file lives at static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js
  3. Server-side Ollama call — POST endpoint on FastAPI to avoid CORS uncertainty with browser→Ollama direct calls
  4. Stats extraction in Python — server reads markup objects, extracts stats, builds prompt
  5. Plugin registered in app.js — import + call app.plugins.register(ecManifest) after loadPlugins()

  6. Files to create or modify: the new plugin file, a FastAPI endpoint for AI summaries, plugin registration in the
  main app, styling for the EC panel, tests, and test runner updates. The UI will have a two-column layout with the
  properties panel on the left and the EC brief panel on the right.
  7. EC panel structure: The right panel contains tabs for different summary views—an AI-generated brief describing the
  document's key issues and their distribution, followed by a markup summary showing counts of open issues, questions,
  approvals, and changes.
  8. API design: I'm planning a POST endpoint that accepts document ID and page data in Fabric JSON format, returning
  both a summary text and statistics object that can be generated either by AI or computed from the markup data itself.
  9. Statistics structure: The stats will track total markup count, break down by type (notes, issues, questions,
  approvals, changes) and status (open/resolved), include measurement counts, page totals, and identify which pages have
   the most activity.
  10. Ollama integration: I'm calling the Mistral 7B model via local Ollama instance with a 20-second timeout, sending
  the markup data as context for the summary generation.
  11. Prompt design: Building a template that feeds the model the filename, total markup count, page count, open item
  ratio, and type breakdown to generate a focused 2-3 sentence summary of the document review.
  12. Fallback logic: If the API call fails or times out, I'll construct a summary programmatically by counting markups
  by type and status, then formatting it as a readable status line for the project manager.
  13. Plugin architecture: I'm building a JavaScript plugin that initializes with a container and app reference, then
  listens for document load events to fetch and display the summary data.

  Building the plugin UI shell... I'm fetching the document's page markups and sending them to the API endpoint for
  summarization, then handling the response or catching any errors that occur.

  Now I'm updating the UI elements—displaying the generated summary text, rendering statistics, and showing the footer
  and mode badge once the data comes back. Building the stats display...

  Now I'm setting up the plugin registration in the app and realizing there's a potential issue if the manifest gets
  registered multiple times—I need to think through how to handle that edge case with the document reference.

  I'm also tracing through the canvas serialization logic to understand whether getAllPageMarkups() properly captures
  the live page's current state or if there's a gap where unsaved markups might get lost during page transitions. I'm
  serializing the live canvas separately and merging it with the saved pages, then calling onPageChanging() first to
  ensure the current page is properly flushed into the markup map since it's safe to call multiple times. Now I'm
  mapping out a test plan with 25 tests covering plugin registration, the EC Brief tab, and user interactions. visible
  after document loads, along with the footer. The mode badge displays its text, and clicking refresh triggers a new
  fetch with a loading spinner. Adding markups and refreshing should update the summary, and the stats grid should
  display the correct type counts. For the fallback mode, I need to verify that stats compute correctly from the markup
  JSON, the fallback summary is non-empty, the mode shows "computed" when Ollama is unavailable, and the panel doesn't
  crash on backend errors. That brings the total to 25 additional tests, making 438 overall. Now I'll write out the plan
   file.

      10  ## Context
      11
      12 -`plugins.js` is a complete stub — only an empty `Map` and a no-op `loadPlugins()`.
      13 -The right panel (`#panel-properties`) has no tab system: just a fixed "Properties"
      14 -header over a single `.panel-body`. Future plugins need a slot in the UI to render
      15 -into, plus lifecycle events so they can react to document/page/selection changes.
      12 +Phase 4B shipped a generalized `PluginLoader` with `register()`, lifecycle events, and
      13 +right-panel tab injection. ExtendedCognition is the designed first consumer — the example
      14 +that proves the framework works end-to-end.
      15
      17 -Phase 4B delivers:
      18 -1. A real `PluginLoader` with `register()`, `emit()`, and right-panel tab injection
      19 -2. A tab system on the right panel (mirrors the left panel's Pages/Markups/Measures tabs)
      20 -3. Four lifecycle event hooks forwarded from `app.js` to all registered plugins
      16 +The panel answers the project-manager question: *"Where does this drawing stand?"*
      17 +without requiring the user to count rows in the Markups tab.
