      24 -## Plugin Manifest Shape
      25 -
      26 -```javascript
      27 -{
      28 -  name: 'my-plugin',        // string ID, required — used as Map key
      29 -  label: 'My Plugin',       // tab label, optional — omit for background-only plugins
      30 -  version: '1.0.0',         // informational
      31 -  init(container, app) {},  // called once on register; container = tab DOM div (or null)
      32 -  // Optional lifecycle hooks:
      33 -  onDocumentLoaded(info) {},
      34 -  onPageChanged(page, total) {},
      35 -  onObjectSelected(obj) {},
      36 -  onObjectDeselected() {},
      37 -}
      38 -```
      39 -
      40 -Plugins without `label` are registered (receive events) but get no UI tab.
      41 -
      42 ----
      43 -
      21  ## Architecture
      22
      23  ```
      47 -app.js                              plugins.js (PluginLoader)
      48 -  │                                     │
      49 -  ├─ constructor                        ├─ plugins: Map<name → manifest>
      50 -  │    this.plugins._app = this         ├─ _app: App reference
      51 -  │                                     │
      52 -  ├─ init()                             ├─ loadPlugins()   — no-op (future: dir scan)
      53 -  │    plugins.loadPlugins()            │
      54 -  │                                     ├─ register(manifest)
      55 -  ├─ _onDocumentLoaded(info)            │    ├─ validates name
      56 -  │    plugins.emit('document-loaded')  │    ├─ stores in Map
      57 -  │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │    ├─ _injectTab() if label present
      58 -  │      selection:created → emit)      │    └─ calls manifest.init(container, app)
      59 -  │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │
      60 -  │      selection:updated → emit)      ├─ emit(eventName, ...args)
      61 -  │    canvas.fabricCanvas.on(          │    └─ calls hook on all plugins (try/catch)
      62 -  │      selection:cleared → emit)      │
      63 -  │                                     ├─ _injectTab(manifest)
      64 -  └─ viewer.onPageChange                │    ├─ creates .panel-tab btn in #right-panel-tabs
      65 -       plugins.emit('page-changed')     │    └─ creates .tab-content div in #panel-properties
      66 -                                        │
      67 -                                        └─ _bindRightPanelTabs()
      68 -                                             └─ event delegation on #right-panel-tabs
      24 +Browser (EC plugin)                    FastAPI server           Ollama (localhost:11434)
      25 +  init(container, app)
      26 +  onDocumentLoaded(info)
      27 +    └─ POST /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
      28 +         { pages: { "0": fabricJSON, ... } }
      29 +                                       │
      30 +                                       ├─ _extract_stats(pages)
      31 +                                       ├─ _build_prompt(stats, filename)
      32 +                                       ├─ POST /api/generate ──────────────►
      33 +                                       │                    { response }   ◄──
      34 +                                       │   (timeout=20s; fallback on any error)
      35 +                                       └─ { summary, stats, mode, model }
      36 +    └─ _renderSummary(data)
      37 +         ├─ narrative text (AI brief or computed fallback)
      38 +         ├─ per-type stat rows (issues, questions, approvals, changes, notes)
      39 +         └─ footer: [↺ Refresh]  ⚡ mistral:7b  OR  ≈ Computed
      40  ```

       62
       90 -Key design decisions:
       91 -- `HOOK_MAP` maps event name strings → manifest method names (closed set, easy to extend)
       92 -- `_injectTab()` appends btn to `#right-panel-tabs` and content div to `#panel-properties`
       93 -- `_bindRightPanelTabs()` uses event delegation on `#right-panel-tabs`; removes/re-adds handler to sta
          -y idempotent across multiple `register()` calls
       94 -- `emit()` wraps each plugin call in try/catch — one bad plugin never crashes others
       95 -- `_app` is set by `app.js` after construction (avoids circular dependency)
       63 +**Plugin manifest** — plain object (not a class), exported as named export:
       64

      271 -**Group 3: Event System (7)**
      272 -- `emit('document-loaded', info)` calls `onDocumentLoaded(info)` on plugin
      273 -- `emit('page-changed', 1, 5)` calls `onPageChanged(1, 5)` on plugin
      274 -- `emit('object-selected', obj)` calls `onObjectSelected(obj)` on plugin
      275 -- `emit('object-deselected')` calls `onObjectDeselected()` on plugin
      276 -- Plugin with missing hook receives no error (graceful no-op)
      277 -- Plugin that throws in a hook does not prevent other plugins receiving the event
      278 -- `emit()` with no registered plugins is a no-op (no error thrown)
      270 +**Group 5: Stats Accuracy (4)**

      283 -- Opening a document triggers `onDocumentLoaded` on a registered plugin
      284 -- Page navigation triggers `onPageChanged` on a registered plugin
      285 -- Selecting a canvas object triggers `onObjectSelected` on a registered plugin
      286 -- Deselecting triggers `onObjectDeselected` on a registered plugin

 Here is Claude's plan:
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 OpenRevu Phase 4C — ExtendedCognition Plugin (EC Brief)

 Date: 2026-02-23
 Goal: First real plugin — a document summary panel that synthesizes a plain-English
 status briefing of all markups when a document loads.
 Uses local Ollama (mistral:7b) when available; falls back to computed stats when offline.

 ---
 Context

 Phase 4B shipped a generalized PluginLoader with register(), lifecycle events, and
 right-panel tab injection. ExtendedCognition is the designed first consumer — the example
 that proves the framework works end-to-end.

 The panel answers the project-manager question: "Where does this drawing stand?"
 without requiring the user to count rows in the Markups tab.

 ---
 Architecture

 Browser (EC plugin)                    FastAPI server           Ollama (localhost:11434)
   init(container, app)
   onDocumentLoaded(info)
     └─ POST /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
          { pages: { "0": fabricJSON, ... } }
                                        │
                                        ├─ _extract_stats(pages)
                                        ├─ _build_prompt(stats, filename)
                                        ├─ POST /api/generate ──────────────►
                                        │                    { response }   ◄──
                                        │   (timeout=20s; fallback on any error)
                                        └─ { summary, stats, mode, model }
     └─ _renderSummary(data)
          ├─ narrative text (AI brief or computed fallback)
          ├─ per-type stat rows (issues, questions, approvals, changes, notes)
          └─ footer: [↺ Refresh]  ⚡ mistral:7b  OR  ≈ Computed

 ---
 Files Changed

 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │                  File                   │                            Change                            │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js │ NEW — plugin manifest + UI logic                             │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ main.py                                 │ ADD POST /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary + 3 helper functions │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/app.js                        │ Import EC plugin + register after loadPlugins()              │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/css/style.css                    │ Append EC panel styles (~30 lines)                           │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ test_phase4c.mjs                        │ NEW — 25 tests                                               │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ run_tests.mjs                           │ Add test_phase4c.mjs                                         │
 └─────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Note: static/js/plugins/ directory must be created (does not exist yet).

 ---
 Detailed Changes

 1. static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js — NEW

 Plugin manifest — plain object (not a class), exported as named export:

 export const ExtendedCognitionPlugin = {
     name: 'extended-cognition',
     label: 'EC Brief',
     version: '1.0.0',
     _container: null,
     _app: null,
     _docInfo: null,

     init(container, app) {
         this._container = container;
         this._app = app;
         this._renderShell();
     },

     onDocumentLoaded(info) {
         this._docInfo = info;
         this._fetchSummary();
     },
     // ... _fetchSummary, _showSpinner, _renderSummary, _renderStats, _renderError
 };

 _renderShell() — called once in init():
 <div id="ec-narrative" class="ec-narrative ec-empty">
     Open a document to generate a brief.
 </div>
 <div id="ec-stats" class="ec-stats" style="display:none"></div>
 <div id="ec-footer" class="ec-footer" style="display:none">
     <button id="ec-refresh" class="toolbar-btn ec-refresh-btn">↺ Refresh</button>
     <span id="ec-mode-badge" class="ec-mode-badge"></span>
 </div>
 Binds #ec-refresh click → this._fetchSummary().

 _fetchSummary():
 // 1. Flush live canvas page before collecting
 this._app.canvas.onPageChanging(this._app.lastPage);
 const pages = this._app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();

 // 2. Show spinner
 this._showSpinner();

 // 3. POST to server
 const resp = await fetch(`/api/documents/${this._docInfo.id}/ai-summary`, {
     method: 'POST',
     headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
     body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
 });
 const data = await resp.json();
 this._renderSummary(data);

 _renderStats(stats, container) — builds per-type rows:
 ●  Issues     3
 ?  Questions  1
 ✓  Approvals  0  ← hidden if count=0
 ↔  Changes    2
 ·  Notes      1
 Only types with count > 0 are rendered. Falls back to "No markups yet" if all zero.

 2. main.py — New Endpoint + 3 Helper Functions

 All helpers are module-level functions (not methods). Follow existing pattern of
 await request.json() — no Pydantic models.

 _extract_stats(pages: dict) → dict:
 - Iterates all Fabric object lists across pages
 - Skips area companion IText objects (measurementType == 'area' and type in ('IText','i-text'))
 - Counts byType, byStatus, measurements by type
 - Returns: {total, byType, byStatus, measurements, pages, mostActivePages}
 - mostActivePages is 1-indexed list of top-3 pages by markup count

 _build_prompt(stats, filename) → str:
 - Compact prompt: file info, markup counts, measurements summary, most active pages
 - Ends with: "Write a concise status briefing for a project manager. No bullet points."

 _build_fallback_summary(stats) → str:
 - Returns "No markups on this document yet." when total=0
 - Otherwise: "This document has N markups (X open, Y resolved): issues, questions, changes."
 - Appends measurement counts if any exist

 Endpoint:
 @app.post("/api/documents/{doc_id}/ai-summary")
 async def ai_summary(doc_id: int, request: Request):
     body  = await request.json()
     pages = body.get("pages", {})

     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")

     stats   = _extract_stats(pages)
     mode    = "computed"
     summary = _build_fallback_summary(stats)
     model_name = "mistral:7b"

     try:
         import requests as req_lib
         prompt = _build_prompt(stats, doc["filename"])
         r = req_lib.post(
             "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",
             json={"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False},
             timeout=20,
         )
         r.raise_for_status()
         text = (r.json().get("response") or "").strip()
         if text:
             summary = text
             mode = "ai"
     except Exception:
         pass   # Ollama offline or model missing — stay on computed fallback

     return {"summary": summary, "stats": stats,
             "mode": mode, "model": model_name if mode == "ai" else None}

 Note: import requests is inside the function (not module-level) so a missing
 requests package fails gracefully at call time rather than crashing server startup.

 3. static/js/app.js — Import + Register

 Add import at top (after existing imports):
 import { ExtendedCognitionPlugin } from './plugins/extended-cognition.js';

 In init(), immediately after await this.plugins.loadPlugins():
 // Register ExtendedCognition — first real plugin, proof-of-concept for framework
 this.plugins.register(ExtendedCognitionPlugin);

 4. static/css/style.css — Append EC Styles

 /* EC Brief plugin panel */
 .ec-narrative { padding: 10px 0 12px; line-height: 1.5; color: #e0e0e8; font-size: 12px; }
 .ec-narrative.ec-empty  { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }
 .ec-narrative.ec-loading { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }
 .ec-narrative.ec-error  { color: #ff6666; }

 .ec-stats { padding: 6px 0 10px; border-top: 1px solid #3a3a50; }
 .ec-stat-row {
     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
     padding: 3px 0; font-size: 12px;
 }
 .ec-stat-icon { width: 14px; text-align: center; }
 .ec-stat-label { flex: 1; color: #c0c0cc; }
 .ec-stat-count { font-weight: 600; color: #e0e0e8; }
 .ec-stat-row.ec-muted { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }

 .ec-type-issue    { color: #ff4444; }
 .ec-type-question { color: #ffaa00; }
 .ec-type-approval { color: #44cc66; }
 .ec-type-change   { color: #4a9eff; }
 .ec-type-note     { color: #aaaaaa; }

 .ec-footer {
     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
     padding-top: 8px; border-top: 1px solid #3a3a50;
 }
 .ec-refresh-btn { font-size: 11px !important; padding: 2px 8px !important; }
 .ec-mode-badge  { font-size: 10px; color: #8888a0; }
 .ec-mode-badge.ec-mode-ai { color: #44cc66; }

 ---
 Test Plan — test_phase4c.mjs (25 tests)

 Group 1: Panel Shell (5)
 - EC Brief tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs
 - #tab-plugin-extended-cognition exists after load
 - Clicking EC Brief activates it (gains .active)
 - #ec-narrative element exists in panel container
 - #ec-refresh button exists in panel container

 Group 2: Empty State (3)
 - #ec-narrative has "Open a document" text before doc loads
 (tested by navigating to / home page, not /edit/{id})
 - #ec-stats is hidden (display:none) before doc loads
 - #ec-footer is hidden before doc loads

 Group 3: Server Endpoint Direct (6)
 - POST /api/documents/1/ai-summary with {pages:{}} → 200
 - Response has summary (non-empty string)
 - Response has stats.byType with all 5 markup type keys
 - Response has stats.byStatus with open and resolved keys
 - Response has mode = "ai" or "computed"
 - stats.total = 0 when pages = {}

 Group 4: Panel After Document Load (7)
 - #ec-narrative shows non-empty, non-italic text after doc load
 - #ec-stats div becomes visible (display ≠ none)
 - #ec-footer div becomes visible
 - Mode badge (#ec-mode-badge) text is non-empty
 - Mode badge contains ⚡ (AI) or ≈ (computed)
 - Adding 2 issue markups + clicking Refresh → stat row for Issues appears
 - Panel transitions from spinner text to final text content

 Group 5: Stats Accuracy (4)
 - Adding 2 issue markups → endpoint returns stats.byType.issue = 2
 - A resolved markup → endpoint returns stats.byStatus.resolved ≥ 1
 - Distance measurement → endpoint returns stats.measurements.distances ≥ 1
 - Area companion IText NOT counted (stats.measurements.areas = count of polygons, not double)

 Target: 413 + 25 = 438 passing

 ---
 Implementation Order

 1. mkdir static/js/plugins/
 2. static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js
 3. main.py — helpers + endpoint
 4. static/js/app.js — import + register
 5. static/css/style.css — append styles
 6. test_phase4c.mjs
 7. run_tests.mjs — add suite
 8. Run full suite (target: 438+)

 ---
 Gotchas

 - import requests inside endpoint function — graceful failure if missing, follows MemoryVault pattern
 - onPageChanging(app.lastPage) must be called before getAllPageMarkups() — flushes live canvas
 - Area companion IText skip: measurementType === 'area' AND type in ('IText', 'i-text')
 - Ollama requires stream: False — default is streaming (chunked JSON lines, not parseable as single JSON)
 - Group 2 empty-state tests navigate to / (home), not /edit/1, to avoid triggering onDocumentLoaded
 - db.get_document() returns a dict-like row (sqlite3 Row object) — access with doc["filename"]
 - Plugin manifest is a plain object singleton — init() is called once, state lives on the object itself

│ <div id="ec-narrative" class="ec-narrative ec-empty">                                                                │
│     Open a document to generate a brief.                                                                             │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ <div id="ec-stats" class="ec-stats" style="display:none"></div>                                                      │
│ <div id="ec-footer" class="ec-footer" style="display:none">                                                          │
│     <button id="ec-refresh" class="toolbar-btn ec-refresh-btn">↺ Refresh</button>                                    │
│     <span id="ec-mode-badge" class="ec-mode-badge"></span>                                                           │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ Binds #ec-refresh click → this._fetchSummary().                                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _fetchSummary():                                                                                                     │
│ // 1. Flush live canvas page before collecting                                                                       │
│ this._app.canvas.onPageChanging(this._app.lastPage);                                                                 │
│ const pages = this._app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // 2. Show spinner                                                                                                   │
│ this._showSpinner();                                                                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ // 3. POST to server                                                                                                 │
│ const resp = await fetch(`/api/documents/${this._docInfo.id}/ai-summary`, {                                          │
│     method: 'POST',                                                                                                  │
│     headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },                                                                 │
│     body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),                                                                                 │
│ });                                                                                                                  │
│ const data = await resp.json();                                                                                      │
│ this._renderSummary(data);                                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _renderStats(stats, container) — builds per-type rows:                                                               │
│ ●  Issues     3                                                                                                      │
│ ?  Questions  1                                                                                                      │
│ ✓  Approvals  0  ← hidden if count=0                                                                                 │
│ ↔  Changes    2                                                                                                      │
│ ·  Notes      1                                                                                                      │
│ Only types with count > 0 are rendered. Falls back to "No markups yet" if all zero.                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 2. main.py — New Endpoint + 3 Helper Functions                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ All helpers are module-level functions (not methods). Follow existing pattern of                                     │
│ await request.json() — no Pydantic models.                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _extract_stats(pages: dict) → dict:                                                                                  │
│ - Iterates all Fabric object lists across pages                                                                      │
│ - Skips area companion IText objects (measurementType == 'area' and type in ('IText','i-text'))                      │
│ - Counts byType, byStatus, measurements by type                                                                      │
│ - Returns: {total, byType, byStatus, measurements, pages, mostActivePages}                                           │
│ - mostActivePages is 1-indexed list of top-3 pages by markup count                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _build_prompt(stats, filename) → str:                                                                                │
│ - Compact prompt: file info, markup counts, measurements summary, most active pages                                  │
│ - Ends with: "Write a concise status briefing for a project manager. No bullet points."                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _build_fallback_summary(stats) → str:                                                                                │
│ - Returns "No markups on this document yet." when total=0                                                            │
│ - Otherwise: "This document has N markups (X open, Y resolved): issues, questions, changes."                         │
│ - Appends measurement counts if any exist                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Endpoint:                                                                                                            │
│ @app.post("/api/documents/{doc_id}/ai-summary")                                                                      │
│ async def ai_summary(doc_id: int, request: Request):                                                                 │
│     body  = await request.json()                                                                                     │
│     pages = body.get("pages", {})                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)                                                                                    │
│     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     stats   = _extract_stats(pages)                                                                                  │
│     mode    = "computed"                                                                                             │
│     summary = _build_fallback_summary(stats)                                                                         │
│     model_name = "mistral:7b"                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     try:                                                                                                             │
│         import requests as req_lib                                                                                   │
│         prompt = _build_prompt(stats, doc["filename"])                                                               │
│         r = req_lib.post(                                                                                            │
│             "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",                                                                   │
│             json={"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False},                                           │
│             timeout=20,                                                                                              │
│         )                                                                                                            │
│         r.raise_for_status()                                                                                         │
│         text = (r.json().get("response") or "").strip()                                                              │
│         if text:                                                                                                     │
│             summary = text                                                                                           │
│             mode = "ai"                                                                                              │
│     except Exception:                                                                                                │
│         pass   # Ollama offline or model missing — stay on computed fallback                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│     return {"summary": summary, "stats": stats,                                                                      │
│             "mode": mode, "model": model_name if mode == "ai" else None}                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Note: import requests is inside the function (not module-level) so a missing                                         │
│ requests package fails gracefully at call time rather than crashing server startup.                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 3. static/js/app.js — Import + Register                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Add import at top (after existing imports):                                                                          │
│ import { ExtendedCognitionPlugin } from './plugins/extended-cognition.js';                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ In init(), immediately after await this.plugins.loadPlugins():                                                       │
│ // Register ExtendedCognition — first real plugin, proof-of-concept for framework                                    │
│ this.plugins.register(ExtendedCognitionPlugin);                                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │

│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* EC Brief plugin panel */                                                                                          │

│ .ec-narrative.ec-empty  { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }                                                      │
│ .ec-narrative.ec-loading { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }                                                     │
│ .ec-narrative.ec-error  { color: #ff6666; }                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │

│ .ec-stat-row {                                                                                                       │
│     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;                                                                    │
│     padding: 3px 0; font-size: 12px;                                                                                 │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ .ec-stat-icon { width: 14px; text-align: center; }                                                                   │
│ .ec-stat-label { flex: 1; color: #c0c0cc; }                                                                          │
│ .ec-stat-count { font-weight: 600; color: #e0e0e8; }                                                                 │

│                                                                                                                      │

│ - A resolved markup → endpoint returns stats.byStatus.resolved ≥ 1                                                   │
│ - Distance measurement → endpoint returns stats.measurements.distances ≥ 1                                           │

│ Gotchas                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ - import requests inside endpoint function — graceful failure if missing, follows MemoryVault pattern                │
│ - onPageChanging(app.lastPage) must be called before getAllPageMarkups() — flushes live canvas                       │
│ - Area companion IText skip: measurementType === 'area' AND type in ('IText', 'i-text')                              │
│ - Ollama requires stream: False — default is streaming (chunked JSON lines, not parseable as single JSON)            │
│ - Group 2 empty-state tests navigate to / (home), not /edit/1, to avoid triggering onDocumentLoaded                  │
│ - db.get_document() returns a dict-like row (sqlite3 Row object) — access with doc["filename"]                       │
│ - Plugin manifest is a plain object singleton — init() is called once, state lives on the object itself              │

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  - The PluginLoader.emit() uses a closed-vocabulary HOOK_MAP to map event strings to method names — this prevents typos
   from silently routing events to the wrong handler, a form of defense in depth at the message bus level
  - The EC plugin uses a plain object singleton (not a class) — the name field serves as the registry key, and this
  inside methods refers to the singleton, enabling stateful plugin instances without class instantiation complexity
  - The import requests inside the endpoint function (not module-level) follows Python's "lazy import for optional
  dependency" pattern — the server starts even if requests isn't installed, only failing on the specific call path
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Now implementing all 7 steps in parallel where possible.

● Bash(mkdir -p /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/plugins && echo "Directory created")
  ⎿  Directory created

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js)
  ⎿  Wrote 318 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js
        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — ExtendedCognition Plugin (EC Brief)
        3  *
        4  * Purpose:
        5  *   First real plugin in OpenRevu's plugin framework. Synthesizes a plain-English
        6  *   status briefing of all markups in a document using a local Ollama LLM when
        7  *   available, or computed statistics as a fallback.
        8  *
        9  *   Answers the project-manager question: "Where does this drawing stand?"
       10  *   without requiring the user to count rows in the Markups tab.
       11  *
       12  * Architecture:
       13  *   1. init(container, app) — renders the shell HTML into the right-panel tab
       14  *   2. onDocumentLoaded(info) — triggers a summary fetch from the server
       15  *   3. _fetchSummary() — flushes live canvas page, POSTs to /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
       16  *   4. _renderSummary(data) — displays narrative text + per-type stat rows
       17  *
       18  * Lifecycle:
       19  *   - Panel shows "Open a document..." placeholder before a doc is loaded
       20  *   - After doc load: spinner → AI brief (or computed fallback) + stat rows
       21  *   - Refresh button: re-runs the fetch (picks up newly-added markups)
       22  *
       23  * Security assumptions:
       24  *   - Ollama runs on localhost only (no external network call from browser)
       25  *   - All LLM calls are server-side; this file only talks to the local FastAPI
       26  *   - No user-provided content is eval'd or injected as HTML (textContent only)
       27  *
       28  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       29  * Version: 1.0.0
       30  * Date: 2026-02-23
       31  */
       32
       33 // =============================================================================
       34 // TYPE METADATA — icons and display labels for each markup type
       35 // Must stay in sync with MARKUP_COLORS in canvas.js
       36 // =============================================================================
       37
       38 const TYPE_META = {
       39     issue:    { icon: '●', label: 'Issues',    cls: 'ec-type-issue'    },
       40     question: { icon: '?', label: 'Questions', cls: 'ec-type-question' },
       41     approval: { icon: '✓', label: 'Approvals', cls: 'ec-type-approval' },
       42     change:   { icon: '↔', label: 'Changes',   cls: 'ec-type-change'   },
       43     note:     { icon: '·', label: 'Notes',     cls: 'ec-type-note'     },
       44 };
       45
       46 // =============================================================================
       47 // PLUGIN MANIFEST
       48 // =============================================================================
       49
       50 /**
       51  * ExtendedCognitionPlugin — plain object manifest (not a class).
       52  * The PluginLoader registers this singleton; state lives directly on the object.
       53  * init() is called once; onDocumentLoaded() is called on every document load.
       54  */
       55 export const ExtendedCognitionPlugin = {
       56     name: 'extended-cognition',
       57     label: 'EC Brief',
       58     version: '1.0.0',
       59
       60     // State injected by PluginLoader.register()
       61     _container: null,
       62     _app: null,
       63     _docInfo: null,
       64
       65     // =========================================================================
       66     // LIFECYCLE HOOKS (called by PluginLoader)
       67     // =========================================================================
       68
       69     /**
       70      * Called once when the plugin is registered.
       71      * Renders the panel shell into the container div provided by PluginLoader.
       72      *
       73      * Args:
       74      *   container: The #tab-plugin-extended-cognition div in the right panel.
       75      *   app:       The App instance (window.app).
       76      */
       77     init(container, app) {
       78         this._container = container;
       79         this._app = app;
       80         this._renderShell();
       81     },
       82
       83     /**
       84      * Called by PluginLoader when a new document finishes loading.
       85      * Stores doc info and triggers a fresh summary fetch.
       86      *
       87      * Args:
       88      *   info: Document info object from the server
       89      *         { id, filename, page_count, file_size, page_sizes }
       90      */
       91     onDocumentLoaded(info) {
       92         this._docInfo = info;
       93         this._fetchSummary();
       94     },
       95
       96     // =========================================================================
       97     // PRIVATE — SHELL RENDERING
       98     // =========================================================================
       99
      100     /**
      101      * Render the static panel shell with placeholder state.
      102      * Called once in init(); subsequent updates replace inner element contents only.
      103      *
      104      * HTML structure injected into container:
      105      *   #ec-narrative  — narrative text area (briefing or placeholder)
      106      *   #ec-stats      — per-type stat rows (hidden until doc loads)
      107      *   #ec-footer     — Refresh button + mode badge (hidden until doc loads)
      108      */
      109     _renderShell() {
      110         if (!this._container) return;
      111
      112         // SECURITY: use textContent (never innerHTML with user data) for dynamic content.
      113         // The shell itself uses a safe literal template.
      114         this._container.innerHTML = `
      115             <div id="ec-narrative" class="ec-narrative ec-empty">
      116                 Open a document to generate a brief.
      117             </div>
      118             <div id="ec-stats" class="ec-stats" style="display:none"></div>
      119             <div id="ec-footer" class="ec-footer" style="display:none">
      120                 <button id="ec-refresh" class="toolbar-btn ec-refresh-btn">↺ Refresh</button>
      121                 <span id="ec-mode-badge" class="ec-mode-badge"></span>
      122             </div>
      123         `;
      124
      125         // Bind Refresh button — re-fetches summary with latest markups
      126         const refreshBtn = this._container.querySelector('#ec-refresh');
      127         if (refreshBtn) {
      128             refreshBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this._fetchSummary());
      129         }
      130     },
      131
      132     // =========================================================================
      133     // PRIVATE — SUMMARY FETCH
      134     // =========================================================================
      135
      136     /**
      137      * Fetch a summary from the server for the current document.
      138      *
      139      * Flow:
      140      *   1. Flush live canvas page to in-memory store (avoid losing unsaved markups)
      141      *   2. Collect all page markups
      142      *   3. Show spinner
      143      *   4. POST to /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
      144      *   5. Render result or error
      145      *
      146      * The server returns:
      147      *   { summary, stats, mode: 'ai'|'computed', model: 'mistral:7b'|null }
      148      */
      149     async _fetchSummary() {
      150         if (!this._docInfo || !this._app) return;
      151
      152         // Flush the live page so freshly-drawn markups are included in the payload.
      153         // Without this flush, the current page's objects stay in Fabric's in-memory
      154         // canvas and aren't captured in canvas.pageMarkups yet.
      155         this._app.canvas.onPageChanging(this._app.lastPage);
      156         const pages = this._app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();
      157
      158         this._showSpinner();
      159
      160         try {
      161             const resp = await fetch(`/api/documents/${this._docInfo.id}/ai-summary`, {
      162                 method: 'POST',
      163                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      164                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      165             });
      166
      167             if (!resp.ok) {
      168                 throw new Error(`Server error: ${resp.status} ${resp.statusText}`);
      169             }
      170
      171             const data = await resp.json();
      172             this._renderSummary(data);
      173         } catch (err) {
      174             this._renderError(err.message || 'Failed to load summary');
      175         }
      176     },
      177
      178     // =========================================================================
      179     // PRIVATE — UI STATE HELPERS
      180     // =========================================================================
      181
      182     /**
      183      * Show a loading spinner text in the narrative area while fetching.
      184      */
      185     _showSpinner() {
      186         const narrative = this._container?.querySelector('#ec-narrative');
      187         if (narrative) {
      188             narrative.className = 'ec-narrative ec-loading';
      189             narrative.textContent = 'Generating brief…';
      190         }
      191
      192         // Hide stats and footer while loading
      193         const stats = this._container?.querySelector('#ec-stats');
      194         const footer = this._container?.querySelector('#ec-footer');
      195         if (stats) stats.style.display = 'none';
      196         if (footer) footer.style.display = 'none';
      197     },
      198
      199     /**
      200      * Render an error state in the narrative area.
      201      *
      202      * Args:
      203      *   message: Human-readable error string.
      204      */
      205     _renderError(message) {
      206         const narrative = this._container?.querySelector('#ec-narrative');
      207         if (narrative) {
      208             narrative.className = 'ec-narrative ec-error';
      209             // SECURITY: textContent prevents XSS — error messages come from
      210             // our own server or fetch API, but we still use safe assignment
      211             narrative.textContent = `Error: ${message}`;
      212         }
      213     },
      214
      215     // =========================================================================
      216     // PRIVATE — SUMMARY RENDERING
      217     // =========================================================================
      218
      219     /**
      220      * Render the full summary panel: narrative text + stat rows + footer badge.
      221      *
      222      * Args:
      223      *   data: Response object from /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
      224      *         { summary, stats, mode: 'ai'|'computed', model }
      225      */
      226     _renderSummary(data) {
      227         if (!this._container) return;
      228
      229         // ── Narrative text ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
      230         const narrative = this._container.querySelector('#ec-narrative');
      231         if (narrative) {
      232             narrative.className = 'ec-narrative';
      233             // SECURITY: textContent — never innerHTML — for AI-generated content
      234             narrative.textContent = data.summary || 'No summary available.';
      235         }
      236
      237         // ── Per-type stat rows ───────────────────────────────────────────────
      238         const statsEl = this._container.querySelector('#ec-stats');
      239         if (statsEl) {
      240             statsEl.style.display = '';
      241             this._renderStats(data.stats || {}, statsEl);
      242         }
      243
      244         // ── Footer: Refresh + mode badge ────────────────────────────────────
      245         const footer = this._container.querySelector('#ec-footer');
      246         if (footer) {
      247             footer.style.display = '';
      248         }
      249
      250         const badge = this._container.querySelector('#ec-mode-badge');
      251         if (badge) {
      252             if (data.mode === 'ai' && data.model) {
      253                 badge.className = 'ec-mode-badge ec-mode-ai';
      254                 // ⚡ signals AI-generated text (fast and smart)
      255                 badge.textContent = `⚡ ${data.model}`;
      256             } else {
      257                 badge.className = 'ec-mode-badge';
      258                 // ≈ signals computed/estimated (not AI)
      259                 badge.textContent = '≈ Computed';
      260             }
      261         }
      262     },
      263
      264     /**
      265      * Render per-type markup count rows into the stats container.
      266      *
      267      * Only renders types with count > 0. Shows "No markups yet" if all zero.
      268      * Each row: [icon] [label] [count]
      269      *
      270      * Args:
      271      *   stats:     The stats object from the server { byType, byStatus, total, ... }
      272      *   container: The #ec-stats DOM element to render into.
      273      */
      274     _renderStats(stats, container) {
      275         container.innerHTML = '';
      276
      277         const byType = stats.byType || {};
      278         const types = Object.keys(TYPE_META);
      279         const hasAny = types.some(t => (byType[t] || 0) > 0);
      280
      281         if (!hasAny) {
      282             // Empty state — show muted "no markups" row
      283             const emptyRow = document.createElement('div');
      284             emptyRow.className = 'ec-stat-row ec-muted';
      285             emptyRow.textContent = 'No markups yet';
      286             container.appendChild(emptyRow);
      287             return;
      288         }
      289
      290         for (const type of types) {
      291             const count = byType[type] || 0;
      292             if (count === 0) continue; // skip zero-count types
      293
      294             const meta = TYPE_META[type];
      295
      296             const row = document.createElement('div');
      297             row.className = 'ec-stat-row';
      298             row.dataset.type = type; // allow tests to find rows by type
      299
      300             const iconEl = document.createElement('span');
      301             iconEl.className = `ec-stat-icon ${meta.cls}`;
      302             iconEl.textContent = meta.icon;
      303
      304             const labelEl = document.createElement('span');
      305             labelEl.className = 'ec-stat-label';
      306             labelEl.textContent = meta.label;
      307
      308             const countEl = document.createElement('span');
      309             countEl.className = 'ec-stat-count';
      310             countEl.textContent = count;
      311
      312             row.appendChild(iconEl);
      313             row.appendChild(labelEl);
      314             row.appendChild(countEl);
      315             container.appendChild(row);
      316         }
      317     },
      318 };

      680
      681
      682  # =============================================================================
      683 +# API ROUTES — AI SUMMARY (ExtendedCognition Plugin backend)
      684 +# =============================================================================
      685 +
      686 +def _extract_stats(pages: dict) -> dict:
      687 +    """
      688 +    Extract markup statistics from a dict of Fabric.js page JSON objects.
      689 +
      690 +    Iterates all Fabric object lists across all pages, counts by markupType
      691 +    and markupStatus, and identifies the most active pages.
      692 +
      693 +    Skips area companion IText objects — these are visual labels that pair
      694 +    with polygon area measurements and should not be counted as markup annotations.
      695 +    An area companion is identified by: measurementType == 'area' AND
      696 +    type in ('IText', 'i-text').
      697 +
      698 +    Args:
      699 +        pages: Dict mapping page index string → Fabric JSON { objects: [...] }
      700 +
      701 +    Returns:
      702 +        {
      703 +          total: int,
      704 +          byType: { issue, question, approval, change, note },
      705 +          byStatus: { open, resolved },
      706 +          measurements: { distances, areas, counts },
      707 +          pages: int,              # number of pages with at least one markup
      708 +          mostActivePages: list    # 1-indexed, top-3 pages by markup count
      709 +        }
      710 +    """
      711 +    # Initialize all 5 markup type counts so the response shape is always complete
      712 +    by_type = {t: 0 for t in ("issue", "question", "approval", "change", "note")}
      713 +    by_status = {"open": 0, "resolved": 0}
      714 +    measurements = {"distances": 0, "areas": 0, "counts": 0}
      715 +    total = 0
      716 +    page_counts = {}  # page_index → count for mostActivePages ranking
      717 +
      718 +    for page_key, fabric_json in pages.items():
      719 +        if not isinstance(fabric_json, dict):
      720 +            continue
      721 +        objects = fabric_json.get("objects", [])
      722 +        if not isinstance(objects, list):
      723 +            continue
      724 +
      725 +        page_markup_count = 0
      726 +
      727 +        for obj in objects:
      728 +            if not isinstance(obj, dict):
      729 +                continue
      730 +
      731 +            # Skip area companion IText labels — they are visual annotations
      732 +            # paired with polygon area measurements (see pairedId in measure.js).
      733 +            # Counting them would double-count area measurements as markup items.
      734 +            m_type = obj.get("measurementType")
      735 +            obj_type = obj.get("type", "")
      736 +            if m_type == "area" and obj_type in ("IText", "i-text"):
      737 +                continue
      738 +
      739 +            # Count measurement tool objects separately from annotation markups
      740 +            if m_type == "distance":
      741 +                measurements["distances"] += 1
      742 +                page_markup_count += 1
      743 +                continue
      744 +            if m_type == "area":
      745 +                measurements["areas"] += 1
      746 +                page_markup_count += 1
      747 +                continue
      748 +            if m_type == "count":
      749 +                measurements["counts"] += 1
      750 +                page_markup_count += 1
      751 +                continue
      752 +
      753 +            # Regular annotation markup
      754 +            total += 1
      755 +            page_markup_count += 1
      756 +
      757 +            markup_type = obj.get("markupType", "note")
      758 +            if markup_type in by_type:
      759 +                by_type[markup_type] += 1
      760 +            else:
      761 +                # Unknown types fall into 'note' bucket
      762 +                by_type["note"] += 1
      763 +
      764 +            status = obj.get("markupStatus", "open")
      765 +            if status == "resolved":
      766 +                by_status["resolved"] += 1
      767 +            else:
      768 +                by_status["open"] += 1
      769 +
      770 +        if page_markup_count > 0:
      771 +            page_counts[page_key] = page_markup_count
      772 +
      773 +    # Build 1-indexed most-active page list (top 3 by count)
      774 +    sorted_pages = sorted(page_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
      775 +    most_active = []
      776 +    for page_key, _ in sorted_pages[:3]:
      777 +        try:
      778 +            most_active.append(int(page_key) + 1)  # convert 0-indexed to 1-indexed
      779 +        except (ValueError, TypeError):
      780 +            pass
      781 +
      782 +    return {
      783 +        "total": total,
      784 +        "byType": by_type,
      785 +        "byStatus": by_status,
      786 +        "measurements": measurements,
      787 +        "pages": len(page_counts),
      788 +        "mostActivePages": most_active,
      789 +    }
      790 +
      791 +
      792 +def _build_prompt(stats: dict, filename: str) -> str:
      793 +    """
      794 +    Build a compact prompt for the Ollama LLM to generate a status briefing.
      795 +
      796 +    Includes: file name, markup counts by type and status, measurement
      797 +    summary, and most active pages. Ends with a clear instruction that
      798 +    keeps the output concise and PM-focused.
      799 +
      800 +    Args:
      801 +        stats:    Output of _extract_stats().
      802 +        filename: Document filename (for context in the briefing).
      803 +
      804 +    Returns:
      805 +        A string prompt ready to pass to Ollama's /api/generate.
      806 +    """
      807 +    by_type = stats.get("byType", {})
      808 +    by_status = stats.get("byStatus", {})
      809 +    measurements = stats.get("measurements", {})
      810 +    most_active = stats.get("mostActivePages", [])
      811 +
      812 +    lines = [
      813 +        f"Document: {filename}",
      814 +        f"Total annotations: {stats.get('total', 0)} "
      815 +        f"({by_status.get('open', 0)} open, {by_status.get('resolved', 0)} resolved)",
      816 +        "Breakdown by type:",
      817 +    ]
      818 +
      819 +    for t, count in by_type.items():
      820 +        if count > 0:
      821 +            lines.append(f"  - {t.capitalize()}: {count}")
      822 +
      823 +    if any(v > 0 for v in measurements.values()):
      824 +        lines.append("Measurements on drawing:")
      825 +        if measurements.get("distances"):
      826 +            lines.append(f"  - Distance measurements: {measurements['distances']}")
      827 +        if measurements.get("areas"):
      828 +            lines.append(f"  - Area measurements: {measurements['areas']}")
      829 +        if measurements.get("counts"):
      830 +            lines.append(f"  - Count/quantity markers: {measurements['counts']}")
      831 +
      832 +    if most_active:
      833 +        pages_str = ", ".join(str(p) for p in most_active)
      834 +        lines.append(f"Most annotated pages: {pages_str}")
      835 +
      836 +    lines.append("")
      837 +    lines.append(
      838 +        "Write a concise status briefing for a project manager. "
      839 +        "Two to three sentences. No bullet points."
      840 +    )
      841 +
      842 +    return "\n".join(lines)
      843 +
      844 +
      845 +def _build_fallback_summary(stats: dict) -> str:
      846 +    """
      847 +    Build a computed (non-AI) summary string from markup statistics.
      848 +
      849 +    Used when Ollama is offline, the model is missing, or any network
      850 +    error prevents the AI response from being retrieved.
      851 +
      852 +    Args:
      853 +        stats: Output of _extract_stats().
      854 +
      855 +    Returns:
      856 +        A human-readable summary sentence.
      857 +    """
      858 +    total = stats.get("total", 0)
      859 +
      860 +    if total == 0:
      861 +        # Check if there are measurements even with no annotations
      862 +        measurements = stats.get("measurements", {})
      863 +        if any(v > 0 for v in measurements.values()):
      864 +            m_count = sum(measurements.values())
      865 +            return f"No annotation markups on this document. {m_count} measurement(s) recorded."
      866 +        return "No markups on this document yet."
      867 +
      868 +    by_status = stats.get("byStatus", {})
      869 +    by_type = stats.get("byType", {})
      870 +    open_count = by_status.get("open", 0)
      871 +    resolved_count = by_status.get("resolved", 0)
      872 +
      873 +    # Build type summary for types with non-zero counts
      874 +    type_parts = []
      875 +    for t in ("issue", "question", "change", "approval", "note"):
      876 +        count = by_type.get(t, 0)
      877 +        if count > 0:
      878 +            type_parts.append(f"{count} {t}{'s' if count != 1 else ''}")
      879 +
      880 +    type_summary = ", ".join(type_parts) if type_parts else ""
      881 +
      882 +    summary = (
      883 +        f"This document has {total} markup{'s' if total != 1 else ''} "
      884 +        f"({open_count} open, {resolved_count} resolved)"
      885 +    )
      886 +    if type_summary:
      887 +        summary += f": {type_summary}."
      888 +    else:
      889 +        summary += "."
      890 +
      891 +    # Append measurement counts if any
      892 +    measurements = stats.get("measurements", {})
      893 +    m_total = sum(measurements.values())
      894 +    if m_total > 0:
      895 +        summary += f" {m_total} measurement(s) are recorded on the drawing."
      896 +
      897 +    return summary
      898 +
      899 +
      900 +@app.post("/api/documents/{doc_id}/ai-summary")
      901 +async def ai_summary(doc_id: int, request: Request):
      902 +    """
      903 +    Generate a status briefing for all markups in a document.
      904 +
      905 +    Accepts the full page markup payload from the browser (same format
      906 +    as /api/documents/{id}/markups), extracts statistics, then attempts
      907 +    to call a local Ollama instance for an AI-generated narrative.
      908 +
      909 +    Falls back to a computed summary if Ollama is unavailable, the model
      910 +    is not loaded, or any network/timeout error occurs. This means the
      911 +    endpoint always returns a useful response — AI quality when available,
      912 +    stats-based fallback otherwise.
      913 +
      914 +    Request body:
      915 +        { "pages": { "0": {fabricJSON}, "1": {fabricJSON}, ... } }
      916 +
      917 +    Response:
      918 +        {
      919 +          "summary": str,           # narrative text (AI or computed)
      920 +          "stats": {...},           # full stats object from _extract_stats
      921 +          "mode": "ai"|"computed",  # which path was taken
      922 +          "model": str|null         # model name if AI mode, else null
      923 +        }
      924 +
      925 +    Security:
      926 +        - Only talks to localhost:11434 (Ollama's default port)
      927 +        - The `requests` import is inside the function so a missing package
      928 +          fails gracefully at call time rather than crashing server startup
      929 +        - AI response content is returned as-is (plain text); the browser
      930 +          renders it with textContent (no HTML injection risk)
      931 +    """
      932 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      933 +    if not doc:
      934 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Document not found")
      935 +
      936 +    body = await request.json()
      937 +    pages = body.get("pages", {})
      938 +    if not isinstance(pages, dict):
      939 +        pages = {}
      940 +
      941 +    stats = _extract_stats(pages)
      942 +    mode = "computed"
      943 +    summary = _build_fallback_summary(stats)
      944 +    model_name = "mistral:7b"
      945 +
      946 +    try:
      947 +        # Lazy import — avoids ImportError crashing the server if `requests`
      948 +        # isn't installed. Falls back to computed summary on any exception.
      949 +        import requests as req_lib
      950 +
      951 +        prompt = _build_prompt(stats, doc["filename"])
      952 +
      953 +        r = req_lib.post(
      954 +            "http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",
      955 +            json={"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False},
      956 +            timeout=20,
      957 +        )
      958 +        r.raise_for_status()
      959 +
      960 +        text = (r.json().get("response") or "").strip()
      961 +        if text:
      962 +            summary = text
      963 +            mode = "ai"
      964 +    except Exception:
      965 +        # Ollama offline, model missing, timeout, or requests not installed —
      966 +        # stay on computed fallback; do not expose error details to the client
      967 +        pass
      968 +
      969 +    return JSONResponse({
      970 +        "summary": summary,
      971 +        "stats": stats,
      972 +        "mode": mode,
      973 +        "model": model_name if mode == "ai" else None,
      974 +    })
      975 +
      976 +
      977 +# =============================================================================
      978  # ENTRY POINT
      979  # =============================================================================
      980

      1414 +/* =============================================================================
      1415 + * EC BRIEF PLUGIN PANEL
      1416 + * ExtendedCognition plugin: document status briefing with AI narrative + stats
      1417 + * ============================================================================= */
      1418 +
      1419 +/* Narrative text area — shows briefing, placeholder, spinner, or error */
      1420 +.ec-narrative {
      1421 +    padding: 10px 0 12px;
      1422 +    line-height: 1.5;
      1423 +    color: #e0e0e8;
      1424 +    font-size: 12px;
      1425 +}
      1426 +
      1427 +/* Muted italic — shown before any document is loaded */
      1428 +.ec-narrative.ec-empty  { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }
      1429 +
      1430 +/* Muted italic — shown while fetching the summary */
      1431 +.ec-narrative.ec-loading { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }
      1432 +
      1433 +/* Error state — red text for fetch or server errors */
      1434 +.ec-narrative.ec-error  { color: #ff6666; }
      1435 +
      1436 +/* Per-type stat rows container */
      1437 +.ec-stats {
      1438 +    padding: 6px 0 10px;
      1439 +    border-top: 1px solid #3a3a50;
      1440 +}
      1441 +
      1442 +/* Individual stat row: [icon] [label ──────────────] [count] */
      1443 +.ec-stat-row {
      1444 +    display: flex;
      1445 +    align-items: center;
      1446 +    gap: 6px;
      1447 +    padding: 3px 0;
      1448 +    font-size: 12px;
      1449 +}
      1450 +
      1451 +.ec-stat-icon  { width: 14px; text-align: center; }
      1452 +.ec-stat-label { flex: 1; color: #c0c0cc; }
      1453 +.ec-stat-count { font-weight: 600; color: #e0e0e8; }
      1454 +
      1455 +/* "No markups yet" row */
      1456 +.ec-stat-row.ec-muted { color: #8888a0; font-style: italic; }
      1457 +
      1458 +/* Per-type color accents — matches MARKUP_COLORS in canvas.js */
      1459 +.ec-type-issue    { color: #ff4444; }
      1460 +.ec-type-question { color: #ffaa00; }
      1461 +.ec-type-approval { color: #44cc66; }
      1462 +.ec-type-change   { color: #4a9eff; }
      1463 +.ec-type-note     { color: #aaaaaa; }
      1464 +

      1471 +    border-top: 1px solid #3a3a50;
      1472 +}
