        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Phase 4C ExtendedCognition Plugin Browser Tests
        3  *
        4  * Tests the EC Brief plugin: panel shell, empty state, server endpoint,
        5  * panel after document load, and markup stats accuracy.
        6  *
        7  * Groups:
        8  *   1. Panel Shell (5)              — tab button, content div, narrative, refresh btn
        9  *   2. Empty State (3)              — placeholder before doc loads (tested on home page)
       10  *   3. Server Endpoint Direct (6)   — POST /api/documents/1/ai-summary, response shape
       11  *   4. Panel After Document Load (7)— narrative text, stats visible, badge, refresh flow
       12  *   5. Stats Accuracy (4)           — byType/byStatus counts, distance, area companion skip
       13  *
       14  * Total: 25 tests
       15  * Running total after this suite: 413 + 25 = 438
       16  *
       17  * Run via Windows Chrome from WSL:
       18  *   cmd.exe /c "cd C:\Users\User1\ClaudeProjects\OpenRevu && node test_phase4c.mjs"
       19  *
       20  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       21  * Version: 1.0.0
       22  * Date: 2026-02-23
       23  */
       24
       25 import { chromium } from 'playwright';
       26
       27 const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';
       28 const DOC_ID = 1;
       29 let passed = 0;
       30 let failed = 0;
       31
       32 function assert(condition, msg) {
       33     if (condition) {
       34         passed++;
       35         console.log(`  PASS: ${msg}`);
       36     } else {
       37         failed++;
       38         console.log(`  FAIL: ${msg}`);
       39     }
       40 }
       41
       42 async function run() {
       43     const browser = await chromium.launch({
       44         headless: true,
       45         executablePath: 'C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe',
       46     });
       47     const context = await browser.newContext();
       48     const page = await context.newPage();
       49
       50     try {
       51         // =====================================================================
       52         // SETUP: Load edit page, clear previous markups
       53         // =====================================================================
       54         await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${DOC_ID}`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
       55         await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
       56
       57         // Clear server-saved markups from previous test runs
       58         await page.evaluate(async () => {
       59             window.app.canvas.pageMarkups.clear();
       60             window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas.clear();
       61             window.app.canvas._applyZoom();
       62             await fetch(`/api/documents/${window.app.docId}/markups`, {
       63                 method: 'PUT',
       64                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
       65                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages: {} }),
       66             });
       67             window.app._dirty = false;
       68         });
       69         await page.waitForTimeout(300);
       70
       71         // =====================================================================
       72         // TEST GROUP 1: Panel Shell
       73         // =====================================================================
       74         console.log('\n--- Test Group 1: Panel Shell ---');
       75
       76         // 1.1 — EC Brief tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs
       77         const ecTabBtn = await page.$('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="plugin-extended-cogniti
          on"]');
       78         assert(ecTabBtn !== null, 'EC Brief tab button exists in #right-panel-tabs');
       79
       80         // 1.2 — #tab-plugin-extended-cognition content div exists
       81         const ecTabContent = await page.$('#tab-plugin-extended-cognition');
       82         assert(ecTabContent !== null, '#tab-plugin-extended-cognition content div exists');
       83
       84         // 1.3 — Clicking EC Brief tab makes it .active
       85         await page.click('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="plugin-extended-cognition"]');
       86         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
       87         const ecTabActive = await page.$eval(
       88             '#tab-plugin-extended-cognition',
       89             el => el.classList.contains('active')
       90         );
       91         assert(ecTabActive, 'Clicking EC Brief tab makes #tab-plugin-extended-cognition .active');
       92
       93         // 1.4 — #ec-narrative element exists inside the plugin container
       94         const ecNarrative = await page.$('#tab-plugin-extended-cognition #ec-narrative');
       95         assert(ecNarrative !== null, '#ec-narrative element exists in EC plugin container');
       96
       97         // 1.5 — #ec-refresh button exists in the plugin container
       98         // (May be hidden but must exist in DOM after init)
       99         const ecRefresh = await page.$('#tab-plugin-extended-cognition #ec-refresh');
      100         assert(ecRefresh !== null, '#ec-refresh button exists in EC plugin container');
      101
      102         // =====================================================================
      103         // TEST GROUP 2: Empty State (tested on home page — no doc loaded)
      104         // =====================================================================
      105         console.log('\n--- Test Group 2: Empty State ---');
      106
      107         const homePage = await context.newPage();
      108         await homePage.goto(`${BASE_URL}/`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
      109         await homePage.waitForTimeout(800);
      110
      111         // 2.1 — #ec-narrative has placeholder text before any doc loads
      112         // On the home page the plugin is initialized but no doc is loaded yet,
      113         // so the shell renders with "Open a document" placeholder text.
      114         const ecNarrativeHome = await homePage.$('#ec-narrative');
      115         let homeNarrativeText = '';
      116         if (ecNarrativeHome) {
      117             homeNarrativeText = (await ecNarrativeHome.textContent() || '').trim();
      118         }
      119         assert(
      120             homeNarrativeText.toLowerCase().includes('open') ||
      121             homeNarrativeText.toLowerCase().includes('document'),
      122             `#ec-narrative shows placeholder text before doc loads (got: "${homeNarrativeText.slice(0,
          60)}")`
      123         );
      124
      125         // 2.2 — #ec-stats is hidden (display:none) before doc loads
      126         const statsHidden = await homePage.evaluate(() => {
      127             const el = document.getElementById('ec-stats');
      128             if (!el) return true; // not present = hidden
      129             return el.style.display === 'none' || getComputedStyle(el).display === 'none';
      130         });
      131         assert(statsHidden, '#ec-stats is hidden before doc loads');
      132
      133         // 2.3 — #ec-footer is hidden (display:none) before doc loads
      134         const footerHidden = await homePage.evaluate(() => {
      135             const el = document.getElementById('ec-footer');
      136             if (!el) return true;
      137             return el.style.display === 'none' || getComputedStyle(el).display === 'none';
      138         });
      139         assert(footerHidden, '#ec-footer is hidden before doc loads');
      140
      141         await homePage.close();
      142
      143         // =====================================================================
      144         // TEST GROUP 3: Server Endpoint Direct
      145         // =====================================================================
      146         console.log('\n--- Test Group 3: Server Endpoint Direct ---');
      147
      148         // 3.1 — POST /api/documents/1/ai-summary with empty pages → 200
      149         const endpointResp = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      150             const r = await fetch('/api/documents/1/ai-summary', {
      151                 method: 'POST',
      152                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      153                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages: {} }),
      154             });
      155             return { status: r.status, data: await r.json() };
      156         });
      157         assert(endpointResp.status === 200, 'POST /api/documents/1/ai-summary returns 200');
      158
      159         // 3.2 — Response has non-empty summary string
      160         assert(
      161             typeof endpointResp.data.summary === 'string' && endpointResp.data.summary.length > 0,
      162             'Response has non-empty summary string'
      163         );
      164
      165         // 3.3 — Response stats.byType has all 5 markup type keys
      166         const byType = endpointResp.data.stats?.byType || {};
      167         const hasAllTypes = ['issue', 'question', 'approval', 'change', 'note']
      168             .every(t => t in byType);
      169         assert(hasAllTypes, 'stats.byType has all 5 markup type keys (issue, question, approval, change
          , note)');
      170
      171         // 3.4 — Response stats.byStatus has both open and resolved keys
      172         const byStatus = endpointResp.data.stats?.byStatus || {};
      173         assert(
      174             'open' in byStatus && 'resolved' in byStatus,
      175             'stats.byStatus has both "open" and "resolved" keys'
      176         );
      177
      178         // 3.5 — Response has mode = 'ai' or 'computed'
      179         assert(
      180             endpointResp.data.mode === 'ai' || endpointResp.data.mode === 'computed',
      181             `Response mode is 'ai' or 'computed' (got: "${endpointResp.data.mode}")`
      182         );
      183
      184         // 3.6 — stats.total = 0 when pages = {}
      185         assert(
      186             endpointResp.data.stats?.total === 0,
      187             `stats.total = 0 when pages = {} (got: ${endpointResp.data.stats?.total})`
      188         );
      189
      190         // =====================================================================
      191         // TEST GROUP 4: Panel After Document Load
      192         // =====================================================================
      193         console.log('\n--- Test Group 4: Panel After Document Load ---');
      194
      195         // Ensure EC Brief tab is active so we can inspect its content
      196         await page.click('#right-panel-tabs .panel-tab[data-panel="plugin-extended-cognition"]');
      197         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      198
      199         // Wait for the summary fetch to complete (up to 10 seconds for Ollama)
      200         await page.waitForFunction(() => {
      201             const el = document.getElementById('ec-narrative');
      202             if (!el) return false;
      203             const text = el.textContent || '';
      204             // Spinner text is "Generating brief…" — wait until it changes
      205             return !text.includes('Generating brief') && text.trim().length > 0;
      206         }, { timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => null); // Don't fail test if timeout
      207
      208         // 4.1 — #ec-narrative shows non-empty, non-italic text after doc load
      209         const narrativeText = await page.$eval(
      210             '#ec-narrative',
      211             el => ({ text: el.textContent?.trim() || '', italic: getComputedStyle(el).fontStyle })
      212         );
      213         assert(
      214             narrativeText.text.length > 0 && narrativeText.italic !== 'italic',
      215             `#ec-narrative shows non-italic text after doc load (got: "${narrativeText.text.slice(0, 60
          )}")`
      216         );
      217
      218         // 4.2 — #ec-stats div is visible after doc load
      219         const statsVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      220             const el = document.getElementById('ec-stats');
      221             if (!el) return false;
      222             return el.style.display !== 'none' && getComputedStyle(el).display !== 'none';
      223         });
      224         assert(statsVisible, '#ec-stats div is visible after doc load');
      225
      226         // 4.3 — #ec-footer div is visible after doc load
      227         const footerVisible = await page.evaluate(() => {
      228             const el = document.getElementById('ec-footer');
      229             if (!el) return false;
      230             return el.style.display !== 'none' && getComputedStyle(el).display !== 'none';
      231         });
      232         assert(footerVisible, '#ec-footer div is visible after doc load');
      233
      234         // 4.4 — Mode badge (#ec-mode-badge) has non-empty text
      235         const badgeText = await page.$eval(
      236             '#ec-mode-badge',
      237             el => el.textContent?.trim() || ''
      238         );
      239         assert(badgeText.length > 0, `#ec-mode-badge has non-empty text (got: "${badgeText}")`);
      240
      241         // 4.5 — Mode badge contains ⚡ (AI) or ≈ (computed)
      242         assert(
      243             badgeText.includes('⚡') || badgeText.includes('≈'),
      244             `Mode badge contains ⚡ or ≈ (got: "${badgeText}")`
      245         );
      246
      247         // 4.6 — Adding 2 issue markups + clicking Refresh → stat row for Issues appears
      248         await page.evaluate(async () => {
      249             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      250             // Add 2 issue-type Rect objects
      251             for (let i = 0; i < 2; i++) {
      252                 const rect = new fabric.Rect({
      253                     left: 50 + i * 80, top: 50,
      254                     width: 60, height: 40,
      255                     fill: 'transparent', stroke: '#ff4444', strokeWidth: 2,
      256                     markupType: 'issue', markupStatus: 'open', markupNote: '',
      257                 });
      258                 fc.add(rect);
      259             }
      260             fc.renderAll();
      261         });
      262         await page.waitForTimeout(200);
      263
      264         // Click Refresh
      265         await page.click('#ec-refresh');
      266
      267         // Wait for the refresh to complete
      268         await page.waitForFunction(() => {
      269             const el = document.getElementById('ec-narrative');
      270             if (!el) return false;
      271             const text = el.textContent || '';
      272             return !text.includes('Generating brief') && text.trim().length > 0;
      273         }, { timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => null);
      274         await page.waitForTimeout(500);
      275
      276         const issueRowExists = await page.evaluate(() => {
      277             const rows = document.querySelectorAll('#ec-stats .ec-stat-row');
      278             return Array.from(rows).some(r => r.dataset.type === 'issue');
      279         });
      280         assert(issueRowExists, 'After adding 2 issues + Refresh, stat row for Issues appears');
      281
      282         // 4.7 — Panel transitions from spinner text to final content
      283         // (This is implicitly proven by 4.1 — narrative is not "Generating brief…")
      284         const notSpinner = await page.$eval('#ec-narrative', el => {
      285             return !(el.textContent || '').includes('Generating brief');
      286         });
      287         assert(notSpinner, 'Panel transitions from spinner text to final content');
      288
      289         // =====================================================================
      290         // TEST GROUP 5: Stats Accuracy
      291         // =====================================================================
      292         console.log('\n--- Test Group 5: Stats Accuracy ---');
      293
      294         // 5.1 — Adding 2 issue markups → endpoint returns stats.byType.issue = 2
      295         const statsWithIssues = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      296             // Use the markups already on canvas (2 issues from test 4.6)
      297             window.app.canvas.onPageChanging(window.app.lastPage);
      298             const pages = window.app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();
      299             const r = await fetch('/api/documents/1/ai-summary', {
      300                 method: 'POST',
      301                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      302                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      303             });
      304             return (await r.json()).stats;
      305         });
      306         assert(
      307             statsWithIssues?.byType?.issue === 2,
      308             `stats.byType.issue = 2 after adding 2 issues (got: ${statsWithIssues?.byType?.issue})`
      309         );
      310
      311         // 5.2 — A resolved markup → endpoint returns stats.byStatus.resolved ≥ 1
      312         await page.evaluate(() => {
      313             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      314             const rect = new fabric.Rect({
      315                 left: 250, top: 50,
      316                 width: 60, height: 40,
      317                 fill: 'transparent', stroke: '#44cc66', strokeWidth: 2,
      318                 markupType: 'approval', markupStatus: 'resolved', markupNote: '',
      319             });
      320             fc.add(rect);
      321             fc.renderAll();
      322         });
      323
      324         const statsWithResolved = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      325             window.app.canvas.onPageChanging(window.app.lastPage);
      326             const pages = window.app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();
      327             const r = await fetch('/api/documents/1/ai-summary', {
      328                 method: 'POST',
      329                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      330                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      331             });
      332             return (await r.json()).stats;
      333         });
      334         assert(
      335             (statsWithResolved?.byStatus?.resolved || 0) >= 1,
      336             `stats.byStatus.resolved ≥ 1 after adding resolved markup (got: ${statsWithResolved?.byStat
          us?.resolved})`
      337         );
      338
      339         // 5.3 — Distance measurement → endpoint returns stats.measurements.distances ≥ 1
      340         // We inject a mock distance Group (matches the shape stored by MeasureTools)
      341         await page.evaluate(() => {
      342             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      343             // Minimal Distance Group — just needs measurementType to be counted
      344             const line = new fabric.Line([0, 0, 100, 0], { stroke: '#4a9eff', strokeWidth: 2 });
      345             const label = new fabric.IText('10.0 ft', { fontSize: 11, fill: '#4a9eff' });
      346             const grp = new fabric.Group([line, label], {
      347                 left: 50, top: 150,
      348                 measurementType: 'distance',
      349                 markupType: 'note', markupStatus: 'open', markupNote: '',
      350             });
      351             // Ensure measurementType persists in Fabric JSON
      352             grp.toObject = (function(origToObject) {
      353                 return function(props) {
      354                     const obj = origToObject.call(this, props);
      355                     obj.measurementType = this.measurementType;
      356                     return obj;
      357                 };
      358             })(grp.toObject);
      359             fc.add(grp);
      360             fc.renderAll();
      361         });
      362
      363         const statsWithDist = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      364             window.app.canvas.onPageChanging(window.app.lastPage);
      365             const pages = window.app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();
      366             const r = await fetch('/api/documents/1/ai-summary', {
      367                 method: 'POST',
      368                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      369                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      370             });
      371             return (await r.json()).stats;
      372         });
      373         assert(
      374             (statsWithDist?.measurements?.distances || 0) >= 1,
      375             `stats.measurements.distances ≥ 1 after adding distance measurement (got: ${statsWithDist?.
          measurements?.distances})`
      376         );
      377
      378         // 5.4 — Area companion IText NOT double-counted
      379         // Add an area polygon (measurementType='area', type='Path') and a
      380         // companion IText (measurementType='area', type='IText').
      381         // stats.measurements.areas should be 1 (polygon only), not 2.
      382         await page.evaluate(() => {
      383             const fc = window.app.canvas.fabricCanvas;
      384             // Polygon representing an area measurement
      385             const poly = new fabric.Polygon(
      386                 [{ x: 0, y: 0 }, { x: 100, y: 0 }, { x: 100, y: 80 }, { x: 0, y: 80 }],
      387                 {
      388                     left: 50, top: 200, fill: 'rgba(74,158,255,0.15)',
      389                     stroke: '#4a9eff', strokeWidth: 2,
      390                     measurementType: 'area',
      391                     markupType: 'note', markupStatus: 'open', markupNote: '',
      392                 }
      393             );
      394             // Companion IText label — should be SKIPPED by _extract_stats
      395             const label = new fabric.IText('45.0 sq ft', {
      396                 left: 80, top: 235, fontSize: 11, fill: '#4a9eff',
      397                 measurementType: 'area',
      398             });
      399             // Patch toObject on both to persist measurementType
      400             [poly, label].forEach(obj => {
      401                 obj.toObject = (function(orig) {
      402                     return function(props) {
      403                         const o = orig.call(this, props);
      404                         o.measurementType = this.measurementType;
      405                         return o;
      406                     };
      407                 })(obj.toObject);
      408             });
      409             fc.add(poly);
      410             fc.add(label);
      411             fc.renderAll();
      412         });
      413
      414         const statsWithArea = await page.evaluate(async () => {
      415             window.app.canvas.onPageChanging(window.app.lastPage);
      416             const pages = window.app.canvas.getAllPageMarkups();
      417             const r = await fetch('/api/documents/1/ai-summary', {
      418                 method: 'POST',
      419                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      420                 body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      421             });
      422             return (await r.json()).stats;
      423         });
      424         assert(
      425             statsWithArea?.measurements?.areas === 1,
      426             `Area companion IText not double-counted — measurements.areas = 1 (got: ${statsWithArea?.me
          asurements?.areas})`
      427         );
      428
      429     } finally {
      430         await browser.close();
      431     }
      432
      433     // ==========================================================================
      434     // RESULTS SUMMARY
      435     // ==========================================================================
      436     console.log('\n');
      437     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      438     console.log('  Phase 4C: ExtendedCognition Plugin');
      439     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      440     console.log(`  Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed, ${passed + failed} total`);
      441     console.log('════════════════════════════════════════');
      442
      443     process.exit(failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
      444 }
      445
      446 run().catch(err => {
      447     console.error('[Phase 4C Test] Fatal error:', err);
      448     process.exit(1);
      449 });

      137  - Right panel now has `#right-panel-tabs` + `#tab-properties` wrapping existing content; plugin tabs i
           njected by `_injectTab()` use `data-panel="plugin-{name}"` → `id="tab-plugin-{name}"`
      138  - HOOK_MAP in plugins.js is the closed vocabulary for lifecycle events — extend here when adding new h
           ooks

           180 line endings

          +entType='area' AND type in IText/i-text); `import requests` lazy inside endpoint for graceful failure;
          + Ollama needs `stream: False` for single JSON response
      141 +- EC plugin SECURITY: use textContent (never innerHTML) for AI-generated narrative — prevents XSS from
          + LLM output

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  - The lazy import pattern (import requests as req_lib inside the endpoint function) is Python's idiomatic way to
  handle optional dependencies — the server boots successfully even without requests installed, and the failure surface
  is constrained to the single call site
  - The area companion skip (measurementType == 'area' AND type in (IText, i-text)) is a cross-module contract — the
  frontend (measure.js) creates paired polygons + IText labels with a shared pairedId, and the backend must know to skip
   the label half. This kind of implicit coupling is a good candidate for an explicit schema or comment in both files
  - Using textContent (not innerHTML) for AI-generated narrative text is a XSS defense: even if the LLM returned
  <script>alert(1)</script>, it would be displayed as literal text — this pattern should be applied anywhere external/AI
   content touches the DOM
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  How it works

  The EC Brief tab in the right panel shows a plain-English status briefing when you open a document. The server-side
  endpoint extracts markup statistics from the Fabric JSON payload, attempts a local Ollama call (mistral:7b, 20s
  timeout), and falls back to a computed summary if Ollama is offline. The mode badge shows ⚡ mistral:7b for AI
  responses or ≈ Computed for fallback — you can always tell which path was taken.

● Good — I can see exactly what's happening. Let me explain the current state clearly before touching any code.

  ---
  Current Save Architecture

  There are actually two completely separate save paths that OpenRevu uses — and I suspect the confusion is between
  them:

  1. Auto-save (always on)
  Every change you make is automatically saved to the SQLite database as Fabric JSON after 3 seconds of idle. This is
  fully editable — when you reopen the document in OpenRevu, all markup objects come back as selectable, moveable,
  deleteable Fabric objects. This is the "working file" format.

  2. Export PDF (the Export button)
  The Export button produces originalname_annotated.pdf — it burns the markups into the PDF as native PDF shapes
  (permanent, not editable). This is for delivery/printing — giving a contractor a PDF they can read in Adobe. If you
  then reopen that _annotated.pdf in OpenRevu, the markups look like part of the drawing background.

  So the question is: when you say "saves it flattened" — are you hitting Export, or are you opening the exported PDF
  back in OpenRevu?

  ---
  Layers

  There are no layers currently. The entire roadmap through Phase 4C didn't include them. The system has one flat Fabric
   canvas per page.

  Layers would be a significant addition — the data model, visibility toggles, per-markup layer assignment, and
  rendering all need to be built. Here's roughly what it would take:

     OpenRevu

     useful

∴ Thinking…

  OK so the user:
  1. Is using the Export button, getting _annotated.pdf, then opening it in OpenRevu and expecting the markups to still
  be editable. This is expected behavior (flattened PDF), not a bug. But the UX is confusing. The solution is to either:
  a. Make the Export dialog clearer about what it does
  b. Add a "native" save/open format that preserves Fabric JSON
  c. Rename "Export" to make it clearer

  Actually, looking at this more carefully, the user workflow is:
  2. They open a PDF in OpenRevu

  3. They mark it up
  4. They click Export to get the annotated PDF
  5. They share that PDF with someone
  6. When they (or someone else) opens that PDF in OpenRevu, they expect the markups to be editable

  The core problem is that there's no portable project format that bundles the original PDF with the editable Fabric
  JSON markup data—everything lives in the server's SQLite database tied to a specific document ID, so exporting just
  gives them a flattened PDF with no editable layer. I could fix this by renaming "Export" to clarify it flattens
  markups, or by adding a proper project file format that preserves editability.

  The simplest immediate solution is to either warn users that Export flattens markups, or offer a "Download Working
  File" option that exports a bundle containing both the PDF and JSON so it can be re-imported with markups intact.

  Now thinking about the layers feature the user marked as Critical—I need to figure out what a minimal but functional
  layers system would require. The data model would need each document to track a list of layers with properties like
  ID, name, visibility, lock state, and color, then each markup object would reference its layer ID, with one layer
  always active for new markups.

  For storage and UI, I can leverage the existing document_settings table to store the layers list as JSON without
  schema changes, add a Layers tab to the left panel showing the layer list with visibility and lock toggles plus
  reordering and deletion, and handle canvas behavior by setting the active layer's ID as a default property when
  creating objects and filtering visibility based on layer state.

  On the "save as editable" front, I'm thinking the cleanest approach is either a native project file format that
  bundles the original PDF with markups, or a simpler option where the Export button stays as-is for flattened PDFs and
  a new Download Project button exports just the markups JSON for re-import, making it clear that editable state lives
  server-side rather than in the exported file.

  The real priorities are clarifying the UX—renaming Export to something like "Export PDF" with a tooltip explaining it
  bakes markups in permanently—and then tackling the layer system since that's what the user actually needs for their
  workflow. I need to scope out the layers feature carefully—a minimal version would just need layer data storage, a UI
  panel with visibility toggles, and stamping new objects with their layer ID, while a full implementation adds locking,
   color overrides, reordering, and deletion. I'll clarify the save confusion first, then propose moving forward with a
  complete layers system.

● Got it. Two things to address:

  ---
  The "flattened" issue — This is expected behavior, but confusing UX

  Your editable markups are not lost. Here's what's actually happening:

  1. Auto-save continuously writes your Fabric objects (editable) to the SQLite database, keyed by the document's ID in
  OpenRevu
  2. Export burns those objects permanently into a new _annotated.pdf — it's a delivery copy for sharing/printing, not a
   working file
  3. When you open that _annotated.pdf in OpenRevu, it's treated as a brand new document — the markups look like
  background drawing content, not editable objects

  To continue editing your work: go to the home page and open the original document from the list. Your markup objects
  will all be there, fully editable. The _annotated.pdf is just for handing off to someone else.

  The fix I should make here: rename "Export" to "Export PDF" with a clear tooltip, so it's obvious it produces a
  one-way delivery copy.

  ---
  Layers — Critical, and not yet in the roadmap

  Since this is critical, I'd like to plan it before building. Here's my proposed scope:

  What layers would give you:
  - A Layers panel (left panel tab)
  - Named layers — add/rename/delete
  - Eye toggle (show/hide) — instantly reveal/hide all markups on a layer
  - Lock toggle — prevents selection/modification of objects on that layer (useful for keeping reference markups stable
  while adding new ones)
  - Active layer — new markups always go to the active layer
  - Layer color (optional — per-layer accent color in the panel)

  What would stay the same:
  - All existing markup types and tools — they just get a layerId stamped on them
  - All existing tests still pass
  - Layers are document-wide (one layer list applies to all pages)

  What I'd skip for now (scope control):
  - Layer ordering/drag-reorder (can add later)
  - Per-layer color override on markups (the markup's own color takes precedence)
  - Layer visibility in the PDF export (all visible layers export)

  Does this scope match what you need? And should I fix the Export UX label in the same pass?

  Let me now write the final plan to the plan file, drawing on the exploration results.

  Key decisions to make:
  1. How many API endpoints? I'll do: GET /api/documents/{id}/layers and PUT /api/documents/{id}/layers (full replace -
  simpler than per-layer CRUD)
  2. Layer data model - each layer is {id: uuid, name: string, visible: bool, locked: bool}

  3. Every document starts with a "Default" layer that's created on first load, and I'll need a LayerManager class to
  handle the layer logic in layers.js
  4. The canvas needs to track which layer each object belongs to via custom properties, and markup-list.js needs a
  callback to refresh when layer visibility changes
  5. I'll add a Layers tab to the editor UI with its own styles, and wire everything together in app.js
  6. For visibility, when a layer is hidden I'll filter the canvas objects by layerId and disable their visibility and
  event handling

  , and when shown I'll re-enable them—same approach for locking but keeping visibility on
  7. The tricky part is making this persist across page loads, so when a page changes I need to reapply the layer
  visibility state to the newly loaded objects
  8. For the active layer indicator, I'll store activeLayerId in LayerManager and assign it to new objects when they're
  created, but I need to figure out how to pass the active layer ID to the stampDefaults function since canvas.js
  doesn't know about layers yet—either I pass it through the toolbar or find another way to hook into object creation

  LayerManager can listen to the object:added event and assign the active layerId to objects that don't already have
  one, which keeps the layer system decoupled from canvas.js. When objects are loaded from JSON they'll already have
  their layerId set, but newly created objects from tools won't, so LayerManager can fill that in right after Fabric
  adds them to the canvas.

  Writing the handler logic... Building the layer panel UI...

  For the API, I'll use a single PUT endpoint that accepts the full layers array and active layer ID, storing everything
   as JSON in the document settings. If a document has no layers yet, the GET returns a default layer. For the export
  button, I'm changing the label to "Export PDF" and adding a tooltip.

  Now I'm planning out the test suite—25 tests across five groups covering the panel shell, layer CRUD operations, layer
   states like visibility and locking, markup assignment to layers, and persistence across reloads.

  For the Export button, I just need to update the label in the HTML. Then I'll modify the canvas and app files to track
   layerId in the custom properties, create a new LayerManager class to handle all layer operations, wire everything
  together in the main app, update the editor template with the layers panel, and add the necessary styles.

  On the backend, I need to add the GET and PUT endpoints for managing layers. In the markup-list file, I'll need to
  hook the layers tab into the existing tab-switching logic so it refreshes properly when selected. The tricky part is
  making sure the tab binding handles the new layers panel correctly—I need to check how the current tab switching works
   and ensure the layers tab integrates seamlessly with the existing data-panel attribute system. Following the same
  pattern as MeasureSummary, I'll wire up LayerManager by setting it on the MarkupList instance in app.js, then add the
  refresh call in the tab switching logic. The LayerManager also needs an initForCanvas() method to re-attach the
  object:added event listener whenever a new Fabric canvas is created during document loads.

  The key insight is that when objects are loaded from JSON, they already have their layerId set, so the event handler
  should only assign a layerId to newly drawn objects—checking if one doesn't already exist before stamping it with the
  active layer.

  There's a timing dependency I need to resolve: layer definitions need to be loaded before markups are deserialized,
  otherwise _applyLayerState won't have the layer data it needs when objects fire their object:added events during the
  JSON load.

  The cleanest approach is to ensure layerManager.load() completes first, then call _applyAllLayerStates() to
  retroactively apply visibility and lock states to any objects that were already loaded. I should also trigger this
  same method after page changes to keep everything in sync.

  For the Export button, I'm just updating the label to "Export PDF" and adding a clearer tooltip—that's a quick fix.

  Here's my plan:

  Phase 5: Layers

  Files to change:

  1. static/js/canvas.js - add layerId to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES (line 64)

  3. static/js/app.js - import LayerManager, init in _onDocumentLoaded, wire into markup-list

  Architecture:

  The layer data model will be stored in document_settings with an array of layer objects, each containing an id, name,
  visibility state, and locked status.

  The LayerManager class will handle all layer operations—storing the layers array, tracking the active layer,
  maintaining a reference to the canvas, and providing methods to load layers from the server, set the active layer,
  toggle visibility and locked states...

  Wait, I'm looking at the rest of the LayerManager implementation. It needs methods to add, rename, and delete layers,
  with the delete operation protecting against removing the last layer and reassigning orphaned objects to the default
  layer. There's also internal logic to apply layer state changes to canvas objects and save those changes back to the
  server, plus a refresh method to update the UI panel. For the canvas integration, I just need to add layerId to the
  custom properties list, and importantly, I should not modify the stampDefaults function since layerId shouldn't be
  included there.

  Now I'm setting up the wiring between the different modules. The markup-list needs to trigger a layer panel refresh
  when that tab becomes active, and app.js needs to instantiate the LayerManager, initialize it with the canvas and
  document info, then pass the reference to the markup-list so they can communicate.

  Building the layer panel UI... Building out the layer row structure with visibility and lock toggles, plus the API
  schema for persisting layers.

  Now I'm setting up the backend endpoints to fetch and update layer data, then fixing the export button label to be
  more specific, and finally planning out a comprehensive test suite with 25 tests covering the panel UI and default
  layer state.

  The remaining test groups validate layer creation and deletion, state management like visibility and active selection,
   and persistence to the server.

        1 -# OpenRevu Phase 4C — ExtendedCognition Plugin (EC Brief)
        1 +# OpenRevu Phase 5 — Layers
        2
        3  **Date:** 2026-02-23
        4 -**Goal:** First real plugin — a document summary panel that synthesizes a plain-English
        5 -status briefing of all markups when a document loads.
        6 -Uses local Ollama (mistral:7b) when available; falls back to computed stats when offline.
        4 +**Goal:** Add a document-wide layer system: create/rename/delete layers, toggle
        5 +visibility and lock state, auto-assign newly drawn objects to the active layer,
        6 +persist layer definitions to the server. Also fix the Export button label.
        7
        8  ---
        9
       10  ## Context
       11
       12 -Phase 4B shipped a generalized `PluginLoader` with `register()`, lifecycle events, and
       13 -right-panel tab injection. ExtendedCognition is the designed first consumer — the example
       14 -that proves the framework works end-to-end.
       12 +Users need to organize markups into named, togglable layers — e.g. "Structural",
       13 +"Electrical", "Comments". Currently everything lives on one flat canvas. The
       14 +Fabric JSON already survives page navigation via CUSTOM_PROPERTIES serialization;
       15 +adding `layerId` to that list is the only canvas.js change required. The
       16 +`document_settings` table (used by the scale system) is the right store for
       17 +layer definitions. The left-panel tab pattern (Pages / Markups / Measures) is
       18 +exactly what the Layers panel should follow.
       19
       16 -The panel answers the project-manager question: *"Where does this drawing stand?"*
       17 -without requiring the user to count rows in the Markups tab.
       20 +---
       21
       22 +## Data Model
       23 +
       24 +Layer definitions stored as JSON in `document_settings` key `"layers"`:
       25 +```json
       26 +{
       27 +  "layers": [
       28 +    { "id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": true, "locked": false }
       29 +  ],
       30 +  "activeId": "default"
       31 +}
       32 +```
       33 +
       34 +Each Fabric object gets `obj.layerId` stamped by LayerManager via `object:added`.
       35 +Serialized automatically via the CUSTOM_PROPERTIES prototype patch in canvas.js.
       36 +
       37  ---
       38
       39  ## Architecture
       40
       41  ```
       24 -Browser (EC plugin)                    FastAPI server           Ollama (localhost:11434)
       25 -  init(container, app)
       26 -  onDocumentLoaded(info)
       27 -    └─ POST /api/documents/{id}/ai-summary
       28 -         { pages: { "0": fabricJSON, ... } }
       29 -                                       │
       30 -                                       ├─ _extract_stats(pages)
       31 -                                       ├─ _build_prompt(stats, filename)
       32 -                                       ├─ POST /api/generate ──────────────►
       33 -                                       │                    { response }   ◄──
       34 -                                       │   (timeout=20s; fallback on any error)
       35 -                                       └─ { summary, stats, mode, model }
       36 -    └─ _renderSummary(data)
       37 -         ├─ narrative text (AI brief or computed fallback)
       38 -         ├─ per-type stat rows (issues, questions, approvals, changes, notes)
       39 -         └─ footer: [↺ Refresh]  ⚡ mistral:7b  OR  ≈ Computed
       42 +LayerManager (layers.js)
       43 +  _layers[]          — [{id, name, visible, locked}]
       44 +  _activeLayerId     — string
       45 +  _canvas            — CanvasOverlay ref
       46 +  _fabricCanvas      — current Fabric canvas (reset on each doc load)
       47 +  _docId
       48 +
       49 +  init(canvas)           — store ref, bind #btn-new-layer click
       50 +  load(docId)            — GET /api/.../layers, render, _applyAllLayerStates()
       51 +  initForCanvas(fc)      — rebind object:added handler (called per doc load)
       52 +  setActive(id)          — highlight row, update _activeLayerId
       53 +  toggleVisible(id)      — flip visible, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
       54 +  toggleLocked(id)       — flip locked, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
       55 +  addLayer()             — push {id:uuid, name:'Layer N'...}; _save(); refresh()
       56 +  renameLayer(id, name)  — update name, _save()
       57 +  deleteLayer(id)        — guard >=2 needed; orphans->default; _save(); refresh()
       58 +  _applyLayerState(obj)  — sets obj.visible / selectable / evented from its layer
       59 +  _applyAllLayerStates() — iterates all fc.getObjects(), applyLayerState, renderAll()
       60 +  _save()                — PUT /api/.../layers {layers, activeId}
       61 +  refresh()              — rebuild #layers-list DOM from _layers
       62  ```
       63
       64 +**object:added hook** (in `initForCanvas(fc)`):
       65 +```javascript
       66 +fc.on('object:added', (e) => {
       67 +    const obj = e.target;
       68 +    if (!obj.layerId) obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId; // new objects only
       69 +    this._applyLayerState(obj);  // always apply visibility/lock
       70 +});
       71 +```
       72 +

      101 -**`_fetchSummary()`**:
      123 +Key implementation notes:
      124 +- `_objectAddedHandler` stored on instance so `initForCanvas()` can `fc.off()` it on rebind
      125 +- `deleteLayer()` re-assigns orphaned objects to `'default'` BEFORE splicing the array
      126 +- `load()` is async; `await` it in app.js `_onDocumentLoaded` so layer state is set
      127 +  before the 200ms-delayed `_loadMarkups()` call renders objects onto canvas

      135 -**`_extract_stats(pages: dict) → dict`**:
      136 -- Iterates all Fabric object lists across pages
      137 -- Skips area companion IText objects (`measurementType == 'area'` and `type in ('IText','i-text')`)
      138 -- Counts `byType`, `byStatus`, measurements by type
      139 -- Returns: `{total, byType, byStatus, measurements, pages, mostActivePages}`
      140 -- `mostActivePages` is 1-indexed list of top-3 pages by markup count
      190 +---
      191
      142 -**`_build_prompt(stats, filename) → str`**:
      143 -- Compact prompt: file info, markup counts, measurements summary, most active pages
      144 -- Ends with: "Write a concise status briefing for a project manager. No bullet points."

      258 +```python
      259 +# =============================================================================
      260 +# API ROUTES — LAYERS
      261 +# =============================================================================
      262
      221 -.ec-type-issue    { color: #ff4444; }
      222 -.ec-type-question { color: #ffaa00; }
      223 -.ec-type-approval { color: #44cc66; }
      224 -.ec-type-change   { color: #4a9eff; }
      225 -.ec-type-note     { color: #aaaaaa; }
      263 +def _default_layers() -> dict:
      264 +    """Return the default single-layer configuration for new documents."""
      265 +    return {
      266 +        "layers": [{"id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": True, "locked": False}],
      267 +        "activeId": "default",
      268 +    }
      269
      227 -.ec-footer {
      228 -    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
      229 -    padding-top: 8px; border-top: 1px solid #3a3a50;
      230 -}
      231 -.ec-refresh-btn { font-size: 11px !important; padding: 2px 8px !important; }
      232 -.ec-mode-badge  { font-size: 10px; color: #8888a0; }
      233 -.ec-mode-badge.ec-mode-ai { color: #44cc66; }
      270 +@app.get("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
      271 +async def get_layers(doc_id: int):
      272 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      273 +    if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")
      274 +    raw = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "layers")
      275 +    if raw:
      276 +        import json as _j
      277 +        try: return JSONResponse(_j.loads(raw))
      278 +        except Exception: pass
      279 +    return JSONResponse(_default_layers())
      280 +
      281 +@app.put("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
      282 +async def put_layers(doc_id: int, request: Request):
      283 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      284 +    if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")
      285 +    body = await request.json()
      286 +    layers = body.get("layers")
      287 +    if not isinstance(layers, list) or len(layers) == 0:
      288 +        raise HTTPException(400, "layers must be a non-empty list")
      289 +    # SECURITY: clamp strings, validate types
      290 +    cleaned = []
      291 +    for layer in layers:
      292 +        if not isinstance(layer, dict): continue
      293 +        cleaned.append({
      294 +            "id":      str(layer.get("id", "default"))[:64],
      295 +            "name":    str(layer.get("name", "Layer"))[:64],
      296 +            "visible": bool(layer.get("visible", True)),
      297 +            "locked":  bool(layer.get("locked", False)),
      298 +        })
      299 +    if not cleaned:
      300 +        raise HTTPException(400, "No valid layers provided")
      301 +    active_id = str(body.get("activeId", "default"))[:64]
      302 +    import json as _j
      303 +    db.set_document_setting(doc_id, "layers", _j.dumps({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id}))
      304 +    return JSONResponse({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id})
      305  ```

      322 +- Default layer has `id = "default"` and `name = "Default"`
      323 +- Default layer has `visible: true, locked: false`
      324
      253 -**Group 3: Server Endpoint Direct (6)**
      254 -- `POST /api/documents/1/ai-summary` with `{pages:{}}` → 200
      255 -- Response has `summary` (non-empty string)
      256 -- Response has `stats.byType` with all 5 markup type keys
      257 -- Response has `stats.byStatus` with `open` and `resolved` keys
      258 -- Response has `mode` = `"ai"` or `"computed"`
      259 -- `stats.total` = 0 when pages = {}
      325 +**Group 3: Layer CRUD (7)**
      326 +- Clicking `+ Layer` adds a new `.layer-row` to `#layers-list`
      327 +- `#layers-count` text increments after adding a layer
      328 +- Double-click layer name → `<input>` appears; type + blur → name updates in DOM
      329 +- Renamed layer name is saved to server (`GET /api/.../layers` returns new name)
      330 +- Delete `×` button removes the layer row from the list
      331 +- Cannot delete last remaining layer (guard: ≥2 required)
      332 +- After add + delete, remaining layer count is correct
      333
      261 -**Group 4: Panel After Document Load (7)**
      262 -- `#ec-narrative` shows non-empty, non-italic text after doc load
      263 -- `#ec-stats` div becomes visible (`display` ≠ `none`)
      264 -- `#ec-footer` div becomes visible
      265 -- Mode badge (`#ec-mode-badge`) text is non-empty
      266 -- Mode badge contains `⚡` (AI) or `≈` (computed)
      267 -- Adding 2 issue markups + clicking Refresh → stat row for Issues appears
      268 -- Panel transitions from spinner text to final text content
      334 +**Group 4: Layer States (5)**
      335 +- Clicking a layer row gives it `.active` class (removes `.active` from others)
      336 +- Eye `👁` button toggles → row gains `.layer-hidden` class
      337 +- Objects on a hidden layer: `obj.visible === false` on the Fabric canvas
      338 +- Lock `🔓` button toggles → row gains `.layer-locked` class
      339 +- Objects on a locked layer: `obj.selectable === false` on the Fabric canvas
      340
      270 -**Group 5: Stats Accuracy (4)**
      271 -- Adding 2 issue markups → endpoint returns `stats.byType.issue = 2`
      272 -- A resolved markup → endpoint returns `stats.byStatus.resolved ≥ 1`
      273 -- Distance measurement → endpoint returns `stats.measurements.distances ≥ 1`
      274 -- Area companion IText NOT counted (`stats.measurements.areas` = count of polygons, not double)
      341 +**Group 5: Markup Assignment (3)**
      342 +- New markup drawn when Default is active → `obj.layerId === 'default'`
      343 +- New markup drawn when a non-default layer is active → `obj.layerId === <that id>`
      344 +- After page navigation and return, `layerId` is preserved on canvas objects
      345
      276 -**Target:** 413 + 25 = 438 passing

      348  ---
      349
      350  ## Implementation Order
      351
      282 -1. `mkdir static/js/plugins/`
      283 -2. `static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js`
      284 -3. `main.py` — helpers + endpoint
      285 -4. `static/js/app.js` — import + register
      286 -5. `static/css/style.css` — append styles
      287 -6. `test_phase4c.mjs`

      289 -8. Run full suite (target: 438+)
      352 +1. `static/js/canvas.js` — add `'layerId'` to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES
      353 +2. `main.py` — `_default_layers()` + GET + PUT endpoints
      354 +3. `static/js/layers.js` — NEW — LayerManager class
      355 +4. `static/js/markup-list.js` — add `layers` case to `_bindTabSwitching()`
      356 +5. `templates/editor.html` — tab button + content div + Export label fix
      357 +6. `static/css/style.css` — append layer styles
      358 +7. `static/js/app.js` — import + constructor + `_onDocumentLoaded` wiring

      361 +10. Restart server + run full suite (target: 463+)
      362
      363  ---
      364
      365  ## Gotchas
      366
      295 -- `import requests` inside endpoint function — graceful failure if missing, follows MemoryVault patter
          -n
      296 -- `onPageChanging(app.lastPage)` must be called before `getAllPageMarkups()` — flushes live canvas
      297 -- Area companion IText skip: `measurementType === 'area'` AND `type in ('IText', 'i-text')`
      298 -- Ollama requires `stream: False` — default is streaming (chunked JSON lines, not parseable as single
          -JSON)
      299 -- Group 2 empty-state tests navigate to `/` (home), not `/edit/1`, to avoid triggering `onDocumentLoad
          -ed`
      300 -- `db.get_document()` returns a dict-like row (sqlite3 Row object) — access with `doc["filename"]`
      301 -- Plugin manifest is a plain object singleton — `init()` is called once, state lives on the object its
          -elf
      367 +- `initForCanvas(fc)` must store handler as `this._objectAddedHandler` and call
      368 +  `fc.off('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler)` before rebinding — Fabric uses
      369 +  named function references for `off()`.
      370 +- `object:added` fires during `loadFromJSON` for objects that already have `layerId`;
      371 +  skip assignment (`if (!obj.layerId)`) but always call `_applyLayerState(obj)`.
      372 +- `load(docId)` is `async` — `await` it in `_onDocumentLoaded` so layer defs are ready
      373 +  before the 200ms setTimeout fires for `_loadMarkups()`.
      374 +- `deleteLayer()` must reassign orphaned objects to `'default'` BEFORE splicing the
      375 +  array, otherwise `_applyAllLayerStates()` can't find the old layer.
      376 +- `_bindTabSwitching()` uses `querySelectorAll('.panel-tab')` at bind time — the Layers
      377 +  tab `<button>` must be in the static HTML so it's captured when `init()` runs.
      378 +- Lock state does NOT hide objects — `visible` stays true, only `selectable` + `evented`
      379 +  go false. Hidden + locked is the intersection of both rules.
      380 +- Export label fix: change `&#128190; Export` → `&#128190; Export PDF` in editor.html.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 OpenRevu Phase 5 — Layers

 Date: 2026-02-23
 Goal: Add a document-wide layer system: create/rename/delete layers, toggle
 visibility and lock state, auto-assign newly drawn objects to the active layer,
 persist layer definitions to the server. Also fix the Export button label.

 ---
 Context

 Users need to organize markups into named, togglable layers — e.g. "Structural",
 "Electrical", "Comments". Currently everything lives on one flat canvas. The
 Fabric JSON already survives page navigation via CUSTOM_PROPERTIES serialization;
 adding layerId to that list is the only canvas.js change required. The
 document_settings table (used by the scale system) is the right store for
 layer definitions. The left-panel tab pattern (Pages / Markups / Measures) is
 exactly what the Layers panel should follow.

 ---
 Data Model

 Layer definitions stored as JSON in document_settings key "layers":
 {
   "layers": [
     { "id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": true, "locked": false }
   ],
   "activeId": "default"
 }

 Each Fabric object gets obj.layerId stamped by LayerManager via object:added.
 Serialized automatically via the CUSTOM_PROPERTIES prototype patch in canvas.js.

 ---
 Architecture

 LayerManager (layers.js)
   _layers[]          — [{id, name, visible, locked}]
   _activeLayerId     — string
   _canvas            — CanvasOverlay ref
   _fabricCanvas      — current Fabric canvas (reset on each doc load)
   _docId

   init(canvas)           — store ref, bind #btn-new-layer click
   load(docId)            — GET /api/.../layers, render, _applyAllLayerStates()
   initForCanvas(fc)      — rebind object:added handler (called per doc load)
   setActive(id)          — highlight row, update _activeLayerId
   toggleVisible(id)      — flip visible, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
   toggleLocked(id)       — flip locked, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
   addLayer()             — push {id:uuid, name:'Layer N'...}; _save(); refresh()
   renameLayer(id, name)  — update name, _save()
   deleteLayer(id)        — guard >=2 needed; orphans->default; _save(); refresh()
   _applyLayerState(obj)  — sets obj.visible / selectable / evented from its layer
   _applyAllLayerStates() — iterates all fc.getObjects(), applyLayerState, renderAll()
   _save()                — PUT /api/.../layers {layers, activeId}
   refresh()              — rebuild #layers-list DOM from _layers

 object:added hook (in initForCanvas(fc)):
 fc.on('object:added', (e) => {
     const obj = e.target;
     if (!obj.layerId) obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId; // new objects only
     this._applyLayerState(obj);  // always apply visibility/lock
 });

 ---

 ---
 Detailed Changes

 1. canvas.js (line 64) — Add layerId to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES

 After 'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints':
     'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints',
     // Phase 5: layer assignment — which layer this markup belongs to
     'layerId',
 No other canvas.js changes. stampDefaults() is NOT touched — layerId assignment
 happens in LayerManager's object:added hook, keeping canvas.js fully decoupled.

 ---
 2. layers.js — NEW

 Layer row HTML (built in refresh(), content via textContent):
 <div class="layer-row [active] [layer-hidden] [layer-locked]" data-layer-id="{id}">
   <button class="layer-vis-btn"  title="Toggle visibility">👁</button
   <button class="layer-lock-btn" title="Toggle lock">🔓</button>
   <span class="layer-name" title="Double-click to rename">{name}</span>
   <button class="layer-delete-btn" title="Delete layer">×</button>
 </div>
 - active — blue left border (matches markup-row pattern)
 - layer-hidden — dims row + strikethrough name; added when !layer.visible
 - layer-locked — amber lock icon; added when layer.locked
 - Delete × appears on hover via CSS opacity
 - Double-click on .layer-name → replace span with <input>, blur/Enter saves

 Key implementation notes:
 - _objectAddedHandler stored on instance so initForCanvas() can fc.off() it on rebind
 - deleteLayer() re-assigns orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE splicing the array
 - load() is async; await it in app.js _onDocumentLoaded so layer state is set
 before the 200ms-delayed _loadMarkups() call renders objects onto canvas

 ---
 3. app.js — Wiring

 Import (after existing imports):
 import { LayerManager } from './layers.js';

 Constructor (after this.plugins = new PluginLoader()):
 this.layerManager = new LayerManager();

 _onDocumentLoaded(info) — after this.canvas.init(this.viewer):
 // Layers: init binds the + New Layer button (idempotent guard inside init).
 // initForCanvas rebinds object:added on the fresh Fabric canvas instance.
 // load fetches layer defs and applies visibility/lock before markups render.
 this.layerManager.init(this.canvas);
 this.layerManager.initForCanvas(this.canvas.fabricCanvas);
 await this.layerManager.load(info.id);

 After this.markupList.measureSummary = this.measureSummary:
 this.markupList.layerManager = this.layerManager;

 ---
 4. editor.html

 Tab button — after <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="measures">Measures</button>:
 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="layers">Layers</button>

 Tab content — after closing </div> of #tab-measures:
 <!-- Layers tab content — Phase 5 layer management -->
 <div id="tab-layers" class="tab-content">
     <div id="layers-header">
         <span id="layers-count">0 layers</span>
         <button id="btn-new-layer" class="toolbar-btn layers-new-btn"
                 title="Add a new layer">+ Layer</button>
     </div>
     <div id="layers-list" class="layers-list">
         <p class="muted-text" id="layers-empty">No layers</p>
     </div>
 </div>

 Export button — update label and title for clarity:
 <button id="btn-export" class="toolbar-btn"
         title="Export annotated PDF — markups become permanent (not editable after export)">
     <span class="icon">&#128190;</span> Export PDF
 </button>

 ---
 5. style.css — Append

 /* =========================================================================
  * LAYERS PANEL (Phase 5)
  * ========================================================================= */
 #layers-header {
     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
     padding: 6px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid #3a3a50; flex-shrink: 0;
 }
 #layers-count { font-size: 11px; color: #8888a0; font-weight: 600; flex: 1; }
 .layers-new-btn { font-size: 11px !important; padding: 2px 8px !important; }

 .layers-list { overflow-y: auto; flex: 1; }

 .layer-row {
     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px;
     padding: 5px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a3d;
     cursor: pointer; font-size: 12px; color: #c0c0cc;
     transition: background 0.12s;
 }
 .layer-row:hover   { background: #2a2a3d; }
 .layer-row.active  { background: #2a3a55; border-left: 3px solid #4a9eff; padding-left: 5px; }

 .layer-vis-btn, .layer-lock-btn, .layer-delete-btn {
     background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer;
     color: #8888a0; font-size: 12px; padding: 1px 3px; flex-shrink: 0;
 }
 .layer-vis-btn:hover, .layer-lock-btn:hover { color: #c0c0cc; }
 .layer-delete-btn { margin-left: auto; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.1s; }
 .layer-row:hover .layer-delete-btn { opacity: 1; }
 .layer-delete-btn:hover { color: #ff4444; }

 .layer-name {
     flex: 1; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis;
 }
 .layer-name-input {
     flex: 1; background: #1e1e2e; border: 1px solid #4a9eff;
     color: #e0e0e8; font-size: 12px; padding: 1px 4px; border-radius: 2px;
 }

 /* Hidden layer: dim row + strikethrough name */
 .layer-row.layer-hidden  { opacity: 0.45; }
 .layer-row.layer-hidden .layer-name { text-decoration: line-through; }

 /* Locked layer: amber lock icon */
 .layer-row.layer-locked .layer-lock-btn { color: #ffaa00; }

 ---
 6. markup-list.js — _bindTabSwitching() (after line 178)

 // Refresh layer panel when switching to Layers tab
 if (tab.dataset.panel === 'layers' && this.layerManager) {
     this.layerManager.refresh();
 }

 ---
 7. main.py — Layer API

 Add before the ENTRY POINT section:

 # =============================================================================
 # API ROUTES — LAYERS
 # =============================================================================

 def _default_layers() -> dict:
     """Return the default single-layer configuration for new documents."""
     return {
         "layers": [{"id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": True, "locked": False}],
         "activeId": "default",
     }

 @app.get("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
 async def get_layers(doc_id: int):
     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")
     raw = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "layers")
     if raw:
         import json as _j
         try: return JSONResponse(_j.loads(raw))
         except Exception: pass
     return JSONResponse(_default_layers())

 @app.put("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
 async def put_layers(doc_id: int, request: Request):
     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")
     body = await request.json()
     layers = body.get("layers")
     if not isinstance(layers, list) or len(layers) == 0:
         raise HTTPException(400, "layers must be a non-empty list")
     # SECURITY: clamp strings, validate types
     cleaned = []
     for layer in layers:
         if not isinstance(layer, dict): continue
         cleaned.append({
             "id":      str(layer.get("id", "default"))[:64],
             "name":    str(layer.get("name", "Layer"))[:64],
             "visible": bool(layer.get("visible", True)),
             "locked":  bool(layer.get("locked", False)),
         })
     if not cleaned:
         raise HTTPException(400, "No valid layers provided")
     active_id = str(body.get("activeId", "default"))[:64]
     import json as _j
     db.set_document_setting(doc_id, "layers", _j.dumps({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id}))
     return JSONResponse({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id})

 Test Plan — test_phase5_layers.mjs (25 tests)

 Group 1: Panel Shell (5)
 - Layers tab button exists in .panel-tabs
 - #tab-layers content div exists
 - Clicking Layers tab makes it .active
 - #layers-list exists in panel
 - #btn-new-layer button exists

 Group 2: Server Endpoint (5)
 - GET /api/documents/1/layers → 200
 - Response has non-empty layers array
 - Response has activeId string field
 - Default layer has id = "default" and name = "Default"
 - Default layer has visible: true, locked: false

 Group 3: Layer CRUD (7)
 - Clicking + Layer adds a new .layer-row to #layers-list
 - #layers-count text increments after adding a layer
 - Double-click layer name → <input> appears; type + blur → name updates in DOM
 - Renamed layer name is saved to server (GET /api/.../layers returns new name)
 - Delete × button removes the layer row from the list
 - Cannot delete last remaining layer (guard: ≥2 required)
 - After add + delete, remaining layer count is correct

 Group 4: Layer States (5)
 - Clicking a layer row gives it .active class (removes .active from others)
 - Eye 👁 button toggles → row gains .layer-hidden clas
 - Objects on a hidden layer: obj.visible === false on the Fabric canvas
 - Lock 🔓 button toggles → row gains .layer-locked class
 - Objects on a locked layer: obj.selectable === false on the Fabric canvas

 Group 5: Markup Assignment (3)
 - New markup drawn when Default is active → obj.layerId === 'default'
 - New markup drawn when a non-default layer is active → obj.layerId === <that id>
 - After page navigation and return, layerId is preserved on canvas objects

 Target: 438 + 25 = 463 passing

 ---
 Implementation Order

 1. static/js/canvas.js — add 'layerId' to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES
 2. main.py — _default_layers() + GET + PUT endpoints
 3. static/js/layers.js — NEW — LayerManager class
 4. static/js/markup-list.js — add layers case to _bindTabSwitching()
 5. templates/editor.html — tab button + content div + Export label fix
 6. static/css/style.css — append layer styles
 7. static/js/app.js — import + constructor + _onDocumentLoaded wiring
 8. test_phase5_layers.mjs — NEW
 9. run_tests.mjs — add suite
 10. Restart server + run full suite (target: 463+)

 ---
 Gotchas

 - initForCanvas(fc) must store handler as this._objectAddedHandler and call
 fc.off('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler) before rebinding — Fabric uses
 named function references for off().
 - object:added fires during loadFromJSON for objects that already have layerId;
 skip assignment (if (!obj.layerId)) but always call _applyLayerState(obj).
 - load(docId) is async — await it in _onDocumentLoaded so layer defs are ready
 before the 200ms setTimeout fires for _loadMarkups().
 - deleteLayer() must reassign orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE splicing the
 array, otherwise _applyAllLayerStates() can't find the old layer.
 - _bindTabSwitching() uses querySelectorAll('.panel-tab') at bind time — the Layers
 tab <button> must be in the static HTML so it's captured when init() runs.
 - Lock state does NOT hide objects — visible stays true, only selectable + evented
 go false. Hidden + locked is the intersection of both rules.
 - Export label fix: change &#128190; Export → &#128190; Export PDF in editor.html.

│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Context                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Users need to organize markups into named, togglable layers — e.g. "Structural",                                     │
│ "Electrical", "Comments". Currently everything lives on one flat canvas. The                                         │
│ Fabric JSON already survives page navigation via CUSTOM_PROPERTIES serialization;                                    │
│ adding layerId to that list is the only canvas.js change required. The                                               │
│ document_settings table (used by the scale system) is the right store for                                            │

│ exactly what the Layers panel should follow.                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Data Model                                                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Layer definitions stored as JSON in document_settings key "layers":                                                  │
│ {                                                                                                                    │
│   "layers": [                                                                                                        │
│     { "id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": true, "locked": false }                                         │
│   ],                                                                                                                 │
│   "activeId": "default"                                                                                              │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Each Fabric object gets obj.layerId stamped by LayerManager via object:added.                                        │
│ Serialized automatically via the CUSTOM_PROPERTIES prototype patch in canvas.js.                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Architecture                                                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ LayerManager (layers.js)                                                                                             │
│   _layers[]          — [{id, name, visible, locked}]                                                                 │
│   _activeLayerId     — string                                                                                        │
│   _canvas            — CanvasOverlay ref                                                                             │
│   _fabricCanvas      — current Fabric canvas (reset on each doc load)                                                │
│   _docId                                                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│   init(canvas)           — store ref, bind #btn-new-layer click                                                      │
│   load(docId)            — GET /api/.../layers, render, _applyAllLayerStates()                                       │
│   initForCanvas(fc)      — rebind object:added handler (called per doc load)                                         │
│   setActive(id)          — highlight row, update _activeLayerId                                                      │
│   toggleVisible(id)      — flip visible, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()                                             │
│   toggleLocked(id)       — flip locked, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()                                              │
│   addLayer()             — push {id:uuid, name:'Layer N'...}; _save(); refresh()                                     │
│   renameLayer(id, name)  — update name, _save()                                                                      │
│   deleteLayer(id)        — guard >=2 needed; orphans->default; _save(); refresh()                                    │
│   _applyLayerState(obj)  — sets obj.visible / selectable / evented from its layer                                    │
│   _applyAllLayerStates() — iterates all fc.getObjects(), applyLayerState, renderAll()                                │
│   _save()                — PUT /api/.../layers {layers, activeId}                                                    │
│   refresh()              — rebuild #layers-list DOM from _layers                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ object:added hook (in initForCanvas(fc)):                                                                            │
│ fc.on('object:added', (e) => {                                                                                       │
│     const obj = e.target;                                                                                            │
│     if (!obj.layerId) obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId; // new objects only                                         │
│     this._applyLayerState(obj);  // always apply visibility/lock                                                     │
│ });                                                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │

│ After 'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints':                                                                                   │

│ 2. layers.js — NEW                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Layer row HTML (built in refresh(), content via textContent):                                                        │
│ <div class="layer-row [active] [layer-hidden] [layer-locked]" data-layer-id="{id}">                                  │
│   <button class="layer-vis-btn"  title="Toggle visibility">👁</button>                                                │
│   <button class="layer-lock-btn" title="Toggle lock">🔓</button>                                                     │
│   <span class="layer-name" title="Double-click to rename">{name}</span>                                              │
│   <button class="layer-delete-btn" title="Delete layer">×</button>                                                   │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ - active — blue left border (matches markup-row pattern)                                                             │
│ - layer-hidden — dims row + strikethrough name; added when !layer.visible                                            │
│ - layer-locked — amber lock icon; added when layer.locked                                                            │
│ - Delete × appears on hover via CSS opacity                                                                          │
│ - Double-click on .layer-name → replace span with <input>, blur/Enter saves                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Key implementation notes:                                                                                            │
│ - _objectAddedHandler stored on instance so initForCanvas() can fc.off() it on rebind                                │
│ - deleteLayer() re-assigns orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE splicing the array                                   │
│ - load() is async; await it in app.js _onDocumentLoaded so layer state is set                                        │
│ before the 200ms-delayed _loadMarkups() call renders objects onto canvas                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ 3. app.js — Wiring                                                                                                   │
